The date on which the binding tariff ruling expires and is no longer valid for use. Nullable if the ruling has no expiration or is valid indefinitely.

Boolean flag indicating whether this classification is based on a binding tariff ruling issued by a customs authority. Binding rulings provide legal certainty and protection from reclassification penalties.
The date on which the binding tariff ruling was officially issued by the customs authority. Determines the start of the ruling validity period.
The name of the customs authority or government agency that issued the binding tariff ruling. Examples include US CBP, HMRC, EU Commission.
The official reference number or identifier of the binding tariff ruling issued by the customs authority. Used to cite the ruling in customs declarations and audits.
A numerical score (0-100) representing the confidence level in the assigned HS Code classification. Used for automated classifications to flag low-confidence assignments for manual review.
The date on which the HS Code classification was assigned or approved. Used to determine applicability of tariff rates and trade regulations in effect at that time.

The method by which the HS Code classification was determined. Automated indicates system-driven classification; manual indicates customs broker review; binding ruling indicates official customs authority determination; third party ruling indicates external consultant classification; customer declared indicates shipper-provided classification.
Free-text notes and comments from the classifier explaining the rationale for the HS Code assignment, including references to tariff schedule notes, explanatory notes, or ruling precedents.
Current lifecycle status of the HS classification record. Tracks whether the classification is in draft, under review, approved for use, rejected, expired, or superseded by a newer classification.

Professional license or certification number of the customs broker who performed the classification. Required for regulatory compliance and audit purposes in jurisdictions that mandate licensed brokers.
Detailed textual description of the goods being classified. Includes material composition, intended use, manufacturing process, and other characteristics relevant to tariff classification.
The 3-letter ISO country code indicating the jurisdiction for which this HS classification applies. HS codes may vary by country at the 8-digit and 10-digit levels.
The date and time when this HS classification record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage purposes.

Boolean flag indicating whether the classified goods are classified as dangerous goods under IMDG, IATA DGR, or other hazardous materials regulations. Affects handling, packaging, and transport requirements.

The date from which this HS classification becomes valid and can be used for customs declarations. Supports time-bound classification validity periods.
The date until which this HS classification remains valid. Nullable for open-ended classifications. Used to manage classification expiration and renewal cycles.
Boolean flag indicating whether excise taxes apply to the classified goods in addition to customs duties. Common for alcohol, tobacco, fuel, and luxury goods.
The ECCN assigned to the goods for export control purposes. Used to determine whether an export license is required under dual-use or military export regulations.

The 8-digit HS Code used in the European Union Combined Nomenclature (CN). Extends the 6-digit WCO code with two additional digits for EU-specific classification.
Boolean flag indicating whether an import license or permit is required for the classified goods in the destination country. Used to trigger license application workflows.
The date and time when this HS classification record was last updated or modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking purposes.
The date on which this HS classification was last reviewed or validated by a customs broker or trade compliance specialist. Used to track classification currency and trigger re-review cycles.
The username or identifier of the user who last modified this HS classification record. Used for accountability and audit trail purposes.
The scheduled date for the next review or re-validation of this HS classification. Nullable if no review is scheduled. Used to manage proactive classification maintenance.
The 3-letter ISO country code indicating the country where the goods were manufactured or produced. Country of origin affects duty rates and eligibility for preferential trade agreements.

description: The reduced duty rate applicable under a Free Trade Agreement (FTA) or preferential trade program. Nullable if no preferential treatment applies.

description: The high-level product category or commodity group to which the classified goods belong. Used for reporting and analytics on classification patterns by product type.

Sequential version number incremented each time the classification record is modified. Used for optimistic locking and change history tracking.
Boolean flag indicating whether the classified goods are subject to import or export restrictions, embargoes, or sanctions. Triggers compliance screening and approval workflows.

The unique identifier of this classification record in the source operational system. Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and troubleshooting.
The four-digit UN number assigned to dangerous goods for identification in transport. Format is UN followed by four digits (e.g., UN1203 for gasoline).
reference: UN Recommendations on the Transport of Dangerous Goods

description: The applicable VAT or Goods and Services Tax (GST) rate as a percentage, based on the HS Code and destination country. Used for total landed cost calculation.

Status of customs authority verification that the compliance program benefit was validly claimed and applied (verified, pending, rejected, not_applicable)
Indicates whether expedited customs release processing was granted based on this compliance program membership
Additional free-text notes or comments regarding the application of this compliance program benefit to the declaration
Type of compliance program benefit applied to this declaration (e.g., expedited_release, reduced_examination, priority_processing, dedicated_lane, fee_reduction)
Indicates whether reduced physical examination rate was applied to this declaration based on compliance program membership
Unique identifier for the customs declaration line item. Primary key for the declaration line entity.
Reference to the parent customs declaration header. Links this line item to its parent declaration document.

Foreign key linking to customs.hs_classification. Business justification: Each declaration line declares goods under a specific HS classification. Linking to hs_classification provides traceability to the classification decision. Not removing hs_code as it's a point-in-time

Foreign key linking to customs.license_permit. Business justification: Declaration lines for controlled goods reference a license/permit. Currently has license_number as denormalized string. Adding FK to license_permit normalizes this and enables tracking of license util

Foreign key linking to customs.origin_determination. Business justification: Each declaration line declares a country of origin. Linking to origin_determination provides the supporting determination for the declared origin, especially important for preferential tariff claims.

Foreign key linking to shipment.shipment_package. Business justification: Multi-SKU shipments require line-level mapping between customs declaration lines and physical packages for inspection, examination, and selective release. Enables package-level customs status tracking

Foreign key linking to procurement.po_line. Business justification: Customs declaration lines map to PO line items for item-level duty tracking and compliance audit. Brokers trace declaration lines to PO lines for valuation verification, HS code validation, and transf

Foreign key linking to customs.tariff_rate. Business justification: Each declaration line has an hs_code and duty_rate_percent. Linking to tariff_rate provides the authoritative rate reference. Not removing duty_rate_percent as it's a point-in-time transactional value
Reference number of the antidumping or countervailing duty investigation or order. Null if not subject to trade remedy measures.
Indicates whether this line item is subject to antidumping or countervailing duties. True if subject to trade remedy measures, false otherwise.
reference: WTO Antidumping Agreement, National Trade Remedy Authority

description: Commercial brand or trademark name of the goods. Used for intellectual property enforcement and product identification.
Reference number of the certificate of origin document supporting the country of origin claim and preferential treatment. Required when preference is claimed.
Detailed textual description of the goods being declared. Includes material composition, intended use, brand, model, and other identifying characteristics required by customs authorities.
ISO 6346 container identification number for containerized shipments. Format: four letters followed by seven digits. Null for non-containerized cargo.

Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 code representing the country where the goods were manufactured, produced, or substantially transformed. Used for preferential tariff determination and trade agreement eligibility.
Timestamp when this declaration line record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.
Declared customs value of the goods for duty calculation purposes. Represents the transaction value or adjusted value per WCO Valuation Agreement (GATT Article VII).
reference: WCO Valuation Agreement, GATT Article VII

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the customs value amount. Indicates the currency in which the goods value is declared.
Indicates whether this line item contains dangerous goods requiring special handling and documentation per IMDG Code or IATA Dangerous Goods Regulations. True if dangerous goods, false otherwise.
reference: IMDG Code, IATA Dangerous Goods Regulations

description: Calculated customs duty amount payable on this line item. Computed as customs value multiplied by duty rate, subject to any preferential treatment or exemptions.
Applicable customs duty rate expressed as a percentage of the customs value. Determined by HS code classification and country of origin.
Calculated excise tax amount payable on this line item for goods subject to special excise duties (e.g., alcohol, tobacco, fuel). Zero if not applicable.
Reference number of the FTZ admission or bonded warehouse entry. Null if not admitted to FTZ.
Indicates whether this line item is admitted into a free trade zone or bonded warehouse with duty suspension. True if FTZ admission, false otherwise.
reference: Revised Kyoto Convention, National FTZ Regulations

description: Total weight of the goods including all packaging materials, expressed in kilograms. Used for freight calculation and customs valuation.
Harmonized System tariff classification code for the commodity. Six to ten digit code used for customs duty determination and trade statistics. Aligned to WCO Harmonized System nomenclature.
Three-letter Incoterms code defining the delivery terms and liability split between buyer and seller. Used for customs valuation adjustments and freight cost allocation.
Sequential line number within the customs declaration. Determines the ordering of line items within the parent declaration.
Full address of the manufacturer or producer of the goods. Required for origin verification and product safety traceability.
Name of the manufacturer or producer of the goods. Required for certain product categories and origin verification purposes.
Manufacturer's model number or part number for the goods. Used for product identification and classification verification.
Timestamp when this declaration line record was last modified. Supports audit trail and change tracking for compliance purposes.
Weight of the goods excluding all packaging materials, expressed in kilograms. Used for duty calculation and statistical purposes.
Number of packages or shipping units containing the goods on this line item. Used for cargo handling and inspection planning.
Two-letter UN/CEFACT code identifying the type of packaging. Examples: CT=carton, PL=pallet, DR=drum, BX=box. Aligned to UN/CEFACT Recommendation 21.
Indicates whether preferential tariff treatment is claimed for this line item under a free trade agreement or trade preference program. True if preference is claimed, false otherwise.
reference: WCO Rules of Origin, FTA Regulations

description: Code identifying the specific trade agreement or preference program under which preferential treatment is claimed. Examples include USMCA, EU-GSP, CPTPP. Null if no preference is claimed.
reference: WTO Trade Agreements, National FTA Regulations

description: Indicates whether this line item contains goods subject to import prohibitions or restrictions (e.g., endangered species, hazardous materials, dual-use goods). True if restricted, false otherwise.
Quantity of goods declared on this line item, expressed in the unit of measure specified. Used for duty calculation and statistical reporting.
Indicates whether this line item is subject to a tariff rate quota or quantitative import restriction. True if quota applies, false otherwise.
reference: WTO Agreement on Agriculture, National Quota Regulations

description: Reference number of the quota allocation or license under which this line item is imported. Null if no quota applies.
Unique serial number or identification number for serialized goods. Required for high-value items, controlled goods, and warranty tracking.
Value of the goods for trade statistics purposes, typically converted to the importing country's currency. Used for balance of payments and trade flow analysis.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the statistical value amount. Typically the local currency of the importing country.
Total of all taxes and duties payable on this line item. Sum of duty amount, VAT amount, excise tax amount, and any other applicable charges.
Four-digit UN number identifying dangerous goods for transport and customs purposes. Format: UN followed by four digits. Null if not dangerous goods.
reference: UN Recommendations on the Transport of Dangerous Goods

description: Standard unit of measure code for the declared quantity. Examples include KGM (kilogram), MTR (meter), PCE (piece), LTR (liter). Aligned to UN/CEFACT Recommendation 20.
Code indicating the WCO valuation method used to determine customs value. Values: 1=Transaction Value, 2=Identical Goods, 3=Similar Goods, 4=Deductive Value, 5=Computed Value, 6=Fall-back Method.
Calculated value-added tax or goods and services tax amount payable on this line item. Computed based on the taxable base (customs value plus duty) and applicable VAT rate.
Applicable value-added tax or goods and services tax rate expressed as a percentage. Applied to the sum of customs value and duty amount.
Total volume of the goods expressed in cubic meters. Used for freight calculation and warehouse space planning.
Unique identifier for the duty assessment record. Primary key for the duty assessment transaction.
Reference to the licensed customs broker who prepared and filed the customs declaration and calculated the duty assessment.

Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Duty assessments must reference the governing service agreement to determine duty payment responsibility (importer vs carrier vs 3PL), apply contracted duty management services, validate Incoterms cos

Foreign key linking to route.plan_leg. Business justification: Duty assessments generate accessorial charges allocated to specific transport legs for landed cost calculation. Finance systems need leg-level duty attribution to reconcile carrier invoices, calculate
Reference to the shipment associated with this duty assessment. Links to the freight shipment being cleared through customs.
Reference to the customs declaration for which this duty assessment was performed. Links to the parent customs entry filing.

Foreign key linking to customs.declaration_line. Business justification: Duty assessments are calculated at the commodity/tariff line level, not just at the declaration header level. duty_assessment already has customs_declaration_id (header link) but lacks a declaration_l
Reference to the original duty assessment record if this is an adjusted or corrected assessment. Creates audit trail for assessment changes. Null for original assessments.

Foreign key linking to procurement.po_line. Business justification: Duty assessments must trace to specific PO lines for landed cost calculation, three-way matching with supplier invoices, and cost allocation to inventory. Enables audit trail linking customs duty char

Foreign key linking to pricing.charge_code. Business justification: Duty assessments generate billable charges that must map to pricing charge codes for invoicing and revenue recognition. Required for integrating customs duty costs into customer billing and financial

Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Duty assessments track supplier/exporter for landed cost calculation by supplier. Procurement teams analyze total cost of ownership including duties, requiring supplier linkage for cost allocation and

Foreign key linking to customs.tariff_rate. Business justification: Duty assessment calculates duties based on a specific tariff rate. Currently has tariff_code and duty_rate_percentage as denormalized values. Linking to tariff_rate provides the authoritative rate sou
Reference to the party legally responsible for paying the assessed duties and taxes. The importer of record as declared on the customs entry.
Reason for adjusting the duty assessment after initial calculation. May include post-entry amendment, reconciliation, or customs authority correction. Null if no adjustment made.
Anti-dumping duty assessed when goods are imported at below fair market value. Additional duty imposed to protect domestic industry from unfair trade practices.
Date when the duty assessment was calculated and issued by the customs authority or broker system. Principal business event timestamp for this transaction.

Method by which the duty assessment was calculated. Automated = system-calculated; Manual = customs officer review; Hybrid = system with officer override; Post_audit = retrospective assessment after release.
Business identifier for the duty assessment. Externally visible reference number used in customs documentation and payment processing.
Name of the system that generated the duty assessment calculation. Typically the customs broker GTM system or customs authority automated system.

Current lifecycle status of the duty assessment. Estimated = preliminary calculation; Assessed = official customs authority calculation; Final = confirmed and payable; Disputed = under review or appeal; Adjusted = modified after initial assessment; Cancelled = voided assessment.
Precise date and time when the duty assessment calculation was executed. Used for audit trail and calculation sequencing.

Incoterms basis used for duty calculation. CIF = Cost Insurance Freight; FOB = Free on Board; DDP = Delivered Duty Paid; DAP = Delivered at Place; EXW = Ex Works; FCA = Free Carrier. Determines which costs are included in the customs value.
Countervailing duty assessed to offset foreign government subsidies. Additional duty imposed when imported goods benefit from subsidies in the country of origin.
reference: WTO Subsidies and Countervailing Measures Agreement

description: Three-letter ISO 3166 country code representing the country where the goods were manufactured or substantially transformed. Determines applicable duty rates and trade preferences.
Timestamp when this duty assessment record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this assessment. Indicates the currency in which duties and taxes are denominated.
Total customs duty assessed on the imported or exported goods. Standard ad valorem or specific duty based on HS Code classification and tariff schedule.
Total customs value of the goods used as the basis for duty calculation. Represents the transaction value adjusted per WCO valuation rules.
Date when a formal dispute or protest was filed with the customs authority regarding this assessment. Null if no dispute filed.
Reason for disputing the duty assessment if status is disputed. May include classification disagreement, valuation dispute, or rate application error. Null if not disputed.

Type of customs entry for which duty is assessed. Consumption = immediate release for domestic use; Warehouse = bonded storage; Temporary = temporary import; FTZ = Free Trade Zone; Carnet = temporary admission under ATA Carnet.
Foreign exchange rate applied to convert invoice currency to local currency for duty calculation. Rate published by customs authority or central bank on the assessment date.
Date for which the exchange rate was published and applied. Typically the date of export, entry filing, or customs authority determination.
Excise tax assessed on specific categories of goods such as alcohol, tobacco, fuel, or luxury items. Product-specific indirect tax.
Fee assessed on cargo loaded or unloaded at US ports. Used to fund harbor maintenance and dredging operations.
reference: US Customs and Border Protection - HMF Regulations

description: Indicates whether this duty assessment is for an import or export transaction. Most assessments are for imports; some jurisdictions assess export duties.
Timestamp when this duty assessment record was last updated. Audit trail for record changes.
Fee charged by customs authority for processing the import entry. Administrative charge for customs clearance services.
reference: US Customs and Border Protection - MPF Regulations

description: Free-text notes or comments regarding the duty assessment. May include special instructions, calculation explanations, or customs officer remarks.
Date when the duties and taxes were paid to the customs authority. Null if payment status is unpaid.
Date by which the assessed duties and taxes must be paid to the customs authority. Failure to pay by this date may result in penalties, interest, or cargo holds.
Reference number or transaction identifier for the duty payment. Links to the payment transaction in the financial system or customs payment portal.
Current payment status of the assessed duties and taxes. Unpaid = not yet paid; Paid = fully settled; Partial = partially paid; Overdue = past due date; Waived = exempted from payment.
Code identifying the customs port or border crossing where the goods entered the country and duty was assessed. UN/LOCODE or national port code.
reference: UN/LOCODE - Code for Trade and Transport Locations

description: Reduced duty rate percentage applied under a preferential tariff program or free trade agreement. Lower than the standard Most Favored Nation (MFN) rate.
Free trade agreement or preferential tariff program applied to reduce or eliminate duty. Examples: USMCA, EU-UK TCA, CPTPP, GSP. Empty if no preference claimed.
Total liability including all duties, taxes, and fees assessed on this customs entry. Sum of customs duty, anti-dumping duty, countervailing duty, VAT, excise tax, and all fees.
Value-Added Tax or Goods and Services Tax (GST) assessed on the imported goods. Consumption tax collected at the point of import.
Unique identifier for the AMS filing record. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: AMS filings operate under service agreements that govern filing obligations, timing commitments, cost allocation, and penalty terms. Linking AMS to agreement supports SLA compliance tracking, penalty

Foreign key linking to network.carrier. Business justification: AMS (Automated Manifest System) filings require carrier identification for CBP advance manifest compliance. Carrier FK enables validation of SCAC/IATA codes, vessel/flight details, and supports regula

Foreign key linking to shipment.consignment. Business justification: AMS filing is required for air/ocean cargo to US for security screening. CBP hold resolution, exam coordination, and release authorization depend on linking AMS to consignment. Mandatory for US-bound

Foreign key linking to customs.declaration. Business justification: An AMS (Automated Manifest System) filing is the advance cargo report that precedes the formal customs declaration. Linking ams_filing to the customs declaration closes the workflow loop: AMS -> Decla

Foreign key linking to customs.hs_classification. Business justification: AMS filings include HS code commodity reporting for advance cargo manifest purposes. ams_filing carries hs_code as a STRING denormalized reference. Replacing it with hs_classification_id FK normalizes

Foreign key linking to customs.trade_party. Business justification: Importer of record in AMS filing is a trade party. Adding importer_trade_party_id FK to trade_party.trade_party_id normalizes the importer reference. Cardinality: N:1 (many AMS filings can have the sa

Foreign key linking to customs.isf_filing. Business justification: AMS filings are directly associated with ISF filings for the same ocean shipment. The ams_filing table already carries isf_number as a STRING denormalized reference to the ISF transaction number. Repl

business_glossary_term: Port Of Unlading Network Node Id (Foreign Key)
description: Foreign key linking to route.network_node. Business justification: AMS advance manifest submissions must specify unlading port for CBP security screening. Required for manifest compliance validation and arrival port coordination. Replaces denormalized port_of_unladin

Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: AMS (Automated Manifest System) filings require PO details for cargo screening and customs clearance. Linking enables automated manifest generation, ensures consistency between customs filings and pro

Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: AMS cargo manifest filings identify shipper (often supplier) for CBP advance screening. Carriers reference supplier master for accurate shipper details in manifest submissions, enabling denied party s
The actual date when the conveyance arrived at the US port of unlading. Updated after arrival occurs.
Sequential number tracking amendments to the original AMS filing. Zero for original filing, incremented for each amendment.
The unique transaction number assigned by US Customs and Border Protection (CBP) upon successful AMS filing submission. This is the official CBP reference for the manifest filing.
The customs bond number guaranteeing payment of duties and compliance with CBP regulations.
The type of customs bond covering this shipment. Single transaction bond or continuous bond for regular importers.
The CBP-assigned filer code identifying the licensed customs broker who submitted this AMS filing on behalf of the importer.

business_glossary_term: Customs and Border Protection (CBP) Hold Indicator
description: Boolean flag indicating whether CBP has placed a hold on this shipment for inspection or further review. True if hold is active.
A detailed description of the cargo being shipped. Must be sufficiently detailed for CBP to assess admissibility and security risk.
The full US address of the consignee. Required for AMS filing and delivery.
The name of the party receiving the goods (the importer or buyer). Required for AMS filing.
The number of containers (TEU/FEU) associated with this AMS filing for ocean shipments.
The name or identifier of the conveyance (truck, rail car, barge) for rail and truck AMS filings.
The three-letter ISO country code identifying where the goods were manufactured or produced. Used for duty assessment and trade compliance.
The date and time when this AMS filing record was first created in the system.
The estimated date when the conveyance is expected to arrive at the US port of unlading. Critical for AMS filing timing requirements.
The facility or terminal where the CBP examination will take place or has taken place.

The type of physical examination ordered by CBP. VACIS = Vehicle and Cargo Inspection System (non-intrusive imaging), CET = Container Examination Team (physical inspection), tailgate = partial inspection, intensive = full unload and inspection.
Current lifecycle status of the AMS filing. Tracks progression from draft through submission, acceptance, or rejection by CBP.
The date and time when the AMS filing was submitted to US CBP. This is the official filing timestamp for compliance purposes.
The transport mode for which this AMS filing was submitted. Ocean filings use AMS Ocean, air filings use AMS Air, and rail/truck use ABI (Automated Broker Interface).
The airline flight number for air shipments. Identifies the specific flight carrying the cargo.

The Incoterm governing the transfer of risk and responsibility between shipper and consignee. Determines liability and cost allocation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
The date and time when this AMS filing record was last modified or updated.
The name of the party to be notified upon arrival of the cargo. Often the consignee or their customs broker.
The total number of pieces or packages in the shipment covered by this AMS filing.
The five-character UN/LOCODE identifying the foreign port where the cargo was loaded onto the conveyance.
reference: UN/LOCODE - United Nations Code for Trade and Transport Locations

description: The reason code or message provided by CBP when an AMS filing is rejected. Used to correct and resubmit.
The date when CBP released the cargo for delivery after completing all inspections and clearance procedures.
The seven-digit IMO ship identification number, a unique permanent identifier for the vessel.
The name of the ocean vessel carrying the cargo. Required for ocean AMS filings.
The total volume of the cargo in cubic meters. Used for capacity planning and dimensional weight calculations.
The carrier-assigned voyage or trip number for ocean shipments. Identifies the specific sailing.
The total gross weight of the cargo in kilograms. Required for AMS filing.
Unique identifier for the denied party screening record. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to pricing.accessorial_charge. Business justification: Denied party screening services incur per-transaction fees when performed by third-party compliance vendors or internal compliance departments. These screening charges must be tracked per screening ev

Foreign key linking to billing.billing_account. Business justification: Denied party screening results block billing and credit extension; direct link enforces compliance holds, prevents invoicing to sanctioned entities, and supports regulatory reporting.
Reference to the shipment being screened for denied party compliance.

Foreign key linking to customs.declaration. Business justification: Denied party screening is performed as part of customs declaration processing. The declaration has denied_party_screening_status indicating this relationship. Adding a FK from screening to declaration

Foreign key linking to customs.trade_party. Business justification: The denied_party_screening table has denormalized party attributes (name, address, city, state, postal_code, country_code, tax_number) that correspond to trade_party master data. Linking to trade_part
Outcome of the compliance analyst review for potential or confirmed matches (approved, rejected, escalated, pending review).
Indicates whether manual analyst review is required for this screening result (true for potential matches, false for clear results).
Date and time when the compliance analyst completed their review of the screening result.
Reference identifier linking this screening record to the complete audit trail for regulatory reporting and compliance verification.
Trade compliance program under which this screening was performed (C-TPAT, AEO, Partners in Protection, Customs Self Assessment, Secure Exports Scheme).
Date and time when this screening record was first created in the system.
Level of escalation for high-risk matches requiring senior management or legal review (none, tier 1, tier 2, tier 3, executive).
Indicates whether the match was determined to be a false positive after analyst review.
Date and time when the regulatory hold was released and the shipment was cleared for processing.
Date and time when this screening record was last updated.
Numerical confidence score (0.00 to 100.00) indicating the strength of the match between the screened party and denied party list entries.
Result of the screening check indicating whether the party matched any denied party list (clear, potential match, confirmed match, false positive).
Unique identifier of the matched entity on the denied party list (e.g., OFAC SDN number).
Name of the denied party entity from the sanctions list that matched the screened party.
Sanctions program or regulatory program associated with the matched denied party (e.g., Iran Sanctions, Counter-Terrorism, Narcotics Trafficking).
Name of the specific denied party list where a match was found (e.g., OFAC SDN, BIS Entity List).
Detailed justification provided by the compliance analyst for overriding a potential or confirmed match and approving the shipment.

Type of shipment party being screened (shipper, consignee, notify party, manufacturer, broker, carrier, freight forwarder). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: shipper|consignee|notify_party|manufacturer|broker|carrier|freight_forwarder — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Indicates whether the shipment is placed on regulatory hold due to denied party screening results, preventing further processing until cleared.
Overall risk score (0.00 to 100.00) assigned to this screening result based on match quality, party type, and destination country risk.
Version number of the denied party screening engine used to perform this check.

Comma-separated list of denied party and restricted entity lists checked during this screening (OFAC SDN, BIS Entity List, EU Consolidated List, UN Sanctions, C-TPAT denied parties, HMT Sanctions, DFAT Sanctions, etc.).
reference: OFAC, BIS, EU, UN, C-TPAT Standards

description: Additional notes or comments recorded during the screening process or analyst review.
Externally-known unique reference number for this screening transaction, used for audit trail and compliance reporting.
Version number of the screening rule set and list data used during this check.
Date and time when the denied party screening check was executed. Principal business event timestamp for this transaction.
Current lifecycle status of the screening check workflow.
Unique identifier for the customs hold record. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to pricing.accessorial_charge. Business justification: Customs holds generate detention, storage, examination, and demurrage charges. Essential for billing customers for hold-related costs and tracking financial impact of customs delays in logistics opera
Reference to the customs broker handling the resolution of this hold.

Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Customs holds trigger SLA breaches and penalty clauses in service agreements. Tracking which agreement governs a held shipment is essential for penalty calculation, dispute resolution, credit note iss
Reference to the shipment that is subject to this customs hold.
Reference to the customs declaration associated with this hold.

Foreign key linking to customs.denied_party_screening. Business justification: A customs hold can be placed as a direct result of a denied party screening match (regulatory_hold_flag on denied_party_screening). Linking hold to the triggering denied_party_screening record provide

Foreign key linking to network.partner. Business justification: Customs holds require physical examination/storage at a facility (CFS, bonded warehouse, examination site) operated by a partner. Links hold to the facility partner for operational coordination, cost

Foreign key linking to billing.invoice. Business justification: Customs holds generate detention, demurrage, and storage charges that must be invoiced to customers; direct link supports hold-related billing and financial impact tracking.

Foreign key linking to contract.penalty_clause. Business justification: Customs holds that cause delivery delays trigger contractual penalty clauses for SLA breaches (on-time delivery commitments). Logistics operators must link holds to penalty clauses to calculate financ

business_glossary_term: Port Of Entry Network Node Id (Foreign Key)
description: Foreign key linking to route.network_node. Business justification: Customs holds are placed at specific examination locations/ports. Required for hold resolution workflow by facility, resource allocation, and examination capacity planning. Replaces denormalized port_
Date and time when the customs hold was actually lifted and the shipment was released. Null if hold is still active.
Flag indicating whether the shipper or consignee holds Authorized Economic Operator (AEO) certification, which may expedite hold resolution. True if certified, False otherwise.

business_glossary_term: Customs-Trade Partnership Against Terrorism (C-TPAT) Certified Indicator
description: Flag indicating whether the shipper or consignee holds C-TPAT certification, which may expedite hold resolution. True if certified, False otherwise.
reference: US Customs and Border Protection (CBP) C-TPAT Program

description: Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where the examination took place (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU).
Date and time when this customs hold record was first created in the system.
Flag indicating whether the customer was notified about the customs hold. True if notification was sent, False otherwise.
Date and time when the customer was notified about the customs hold.
Total duration of the hold in hours, calculated from hold placed timestamp to actual release timestamp. Used for Service Level Agreement (SLA) impact analysis.
Numeric indicator of escalation level for complex or disputed holds. 0 = no escalation, 1 = first-level escalation, 2 = management escalation, 3 = executive escalation.
Explanation of why the hold was escalated to a higher level of review or management intervention.
Detailed narrative of the examination findings, including any discrepancies, violations, or compliance issues discovered during the inspection.
Physical location where the examination was conducted (e.g., port facility, Container Freight Station (CFS), bonded warehouse, Inland Container Depot (ICD)).

Outcome of the customs examination. CLEARED = no issues found, DISCREPANCY_FOUND = documentation or commodity discrepancies identified, VIOLATION_DETECTED = regulatory violation discovered, ADDITIONAL_DUTY_ASSESSED = additional duties or taxes levied, SEIZURE = goods seized, PENALTY_ISSUED = monetary penalty imposed.

Type of physical or documentary examination conducted. VACIS = Vehicle and Cargo Inspection System (non-intrusive imaging), CET = Container Examination Team (physical inspection), INTENSIVE = full intensive examination, TAILGATE = tailgate inspection, DOCUMENT_ONLY = documentary review only, NO_EXAM = hold released without examination.
reference: US Customs and Border Protection (CBP) Examination Standards

description: Anticipated date when the hold is expected to be resolved and the shipment released, based on examination schedule and processing timelines.
Externally-known unique identifier or reference number assigned to this customs hold by the issuing authority or internal system.

Current lifecycle status of the customs hold. ACTIVE = hold is in effect, PENDING_RELEASE = awaiting clearance, RELEASED = hold has been lifted, ESCALATED = requires higher-level review, CANCELLED = hold was cancelled or withdrawn.
Code identifying the customs authority or regulatory agency that placed the hold (e.g., CBP, USDA, FDA, port authority, internal compliance team).
Full name of the customs authority or regulatory agency that issued the hold.
Badge or identification number of the customs officer who placed the hold.
Name of the customs officer or inspector who placed the hold.
Date and time when this customs hold record was last updated in the system.
Monetary penalty amount imposed due to violations or non-compliance discovered during the hold examination, in the currency specified by penalty_currency_code.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the penalty amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).
Date and time when the customs hold was officially placed on the shipment. Principal business event timestamp for this transaction.
Standardized code representing the specific reason for the hold (e.g., random inspection, high-risk commodity, incomplete documentation, valuation discrepancy, denied party screening hit).
Detailed narrative explanation of why the customs hold was placed, including any specific regulatory concerns or compliance issues identified.
Code representing the action taken to resolve the hold (e.g., documentation provided, duty paid, goods returned, goods destroyed, appeal filed).
Detailed notes describing how the hold was resolved, including any corrective actions taken, documentation submitted, or negotiations with customs authorities.

Code identifying which party is responsible for resolving the hold. SHIPPER = shipper responsible, CONSIGNEE = consignee responsible, BROKER = customs broker responsible, CARRIER = carrier responsible, INTERNAL = internal compliance team responsible.
Automated risk score assigned to the shipment that triggered the hold, typically from customs risk management systems. Higher scores indicate higher risk.
reference: World Customs Organization (WCO) Risk Management Standards

description: Flag indicating whether the hold caused a breach of the shipment Service Level Agreement (SLA) for on-time delivery. True if SLA was breached, False otherwise.
Total financial impact of the hold including additional duties, penalties, storage fees, and other charges, in the currency specified by total_financial_impact_currency_code.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the total financial impact amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).

Classification of the customs hold type. CBP_EXAM = Customs and Border Protection examination, USDA_INSPECTION = United States Department of Agriculture inspection, FDA_INSPECTION = Food and Drug Administration inspection, SECURITY_HOLD = security-related detention, COMPLIANCE_REVIEW = internal compliance review, DOCUMENTATION_REVIEW = documentation verification hold.
reference: US Customs and Border Protection (CBP) Hold Classification

description: Unique identifier for the ISF filing record. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: ISF filings are performed under master service agreements that define filing responsibilities, timing SLAs, penalty allocation for late/inaccurate filings, and service fees. Linking ISF to agreement e

Foreign key linking to network.carrier. Business justification: ISF (Importer Security Filing) requires carrier identification for CBP 10+2 compliance. Linking to carrier master data ensures accurate SCAC/IATA code validation, vessel/voyage tracking, and regulator

Foreign key linking to shipment.consignment. Business justification: ISF (10+2) filing is mandatory for ocean shipments to US 24 hours before loading. CBP compliance, penalty avoidance, and shipment release all require linking ISF to the consignment. Critical for US im

Foreign key linking to customs.declaration. Business justification: An ISF filing (Importer Security Filing) is a pre-arrival filing that precedes and is associated with the formal customs declaration for the same shipment. Linking isf_filing to the customs declaratio

Foreign key linking to customs.hs_classification. Business justification: ISF filings require HTSUS (Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States) code reporting to CBP. isf_filing carries htsus_code as a STRING denormalized reference. Replacing it with hs_classification

Actual date when the vessel arrived at the US port of unlading.
Number of times this ISF filing has been amended after initial submission. ISF data must be updated if changes occur before cargo arrival.
Unique identifier for the customs bond that guarantees payment of duties, taxes, and penalties for this ISF filing.
Type of customs bond covering this ISF filing. Single transaction bond covers one shipment. Continuous bond covers multiple shipments over a year.
Full address of the buyer, including street, city, state/province, postal code, and country.

Name of the last known entity to whom the goods are sold or agreed to be sold. If the goods are to be imported otherwise than in pursuance of a purchase, the name of the owner. This is one of the 10 required data elements for ISF-10.

business_glossary_term: US Customs and Border Protection (CBP) Rejection Reason
description: Detailed reason provided by CBP for rejecting the ISF filing. May include error codes, missing data elements, or compliance violations.

business_glossary_term: US Customs and Border Protection (CBP) Response Date
description: Date and time when CBP responded to the ISF filing with acceptance, rejection, or request for amendment.

business_glossary_term: US Customs and Border Protection (CBP) Response Status
description: Status of CBP's response to the ISF filing. Accepted indicates the filing meets all requirements. Rejected indicates the filing contains errors or missing data. Amendment required indicates CBP requires corrections. Pending review indicates CBP has not yet completed their review.
Full address of the consignee, including street, city, state/province, postal code, and country.
Name of the party to whom the goods are being shipped. This is one of the 10 required data elements for ISF-10.
Full address of the consolidator, including street, city, state/province, postal code, and country.
Name of the party who stuffed the container or arranged for the stuffing of the container. This is one of the 10 required data elements for ISF-10.
Physical location where the container was stuffed (loaded). Must include the name and address of the facility. This is one of the 10 required data elements for ISF-10.

Country where the goods were manufactured, produced, or grown, as determined by the applicable rules of origin. Represented as a 3-letter ISO country code. This is one of the 10 required data elements for ISF-10.
Date and time when this ISF filing record was first created in the system.
CBP-issued license number of the customs broker who filed the ISF.
Estimated date when the vessel is expected to arrive at the US port of unlading.
Date and time when the ISF was submitted to US Customs and Border Protection (CBP). Must be submitted at least 24 hours before cargo is laden aboard the vessel at the foreign port.

Current lifecycle status of the ISF filing. Draft indicates the filing is being prepared. Submitted indicates the filing has been sent to CBP. Accepted indicates CBP has accepted the filing. Rejected indicates CBP has rejected the filing due to errors or missing data. Amended indicates the filing has been updated after initial submission. Withdrawn indicates the filing has been cancelled.

Type of ISF filing. ISF-10 is for standard ocean shipments (10 data elements required). ISF-5 is for specific scenarios such as FROB (Foreign Remaining On Board), IE (Immediate Exportation), or TE (Transportation and Exportation) shipments (5 data elements required).
Unique transaction number assigned by US Customs and Border Protection (CBP) upon successful ISF submission. This is the official CBP reference number for the filing.
Date and time when the most recent amendment to this ISF filing was submitted to CBP.
Date and time when this ISF filing record was last modified in the system.
Monetary penalty amount assessed by CBP for ISF violations. Penalties can range from $5,000 to $10,000 per violation depending on severity and whether the violation was timely, accurate, or complete.
Detailed explanation of why a penalty was assessed by CBP. May include late filing, inaccurate data, missing data elements, or failure to update.
Indicates whether this ISF filing is at risk of CBP penalties due to late filing, inaccurate data, or non-compliance. True indicates penalty risk exists. False indicates no known penalty risk.
Foreign port where the cargo was laden aboard the vessel. Represented as a 5-character UN/LOCODE.
Full address of the seller, including street, city, state/province, postal code, and country.

Name of the last known entity by whom the goods are sold or agreed to be sold. If the goods are to be imported otherwise than in pursuance of a purchase, the name of the owner. This is one of the 10 required data elements for ISF-10.
Full address of the ship-to party, including street, city, state/province, postal code, and country.
Name of the first deliver-to party scheduled to physically receive the goods after the goods have been released from customs custody. This is one of the 10 required data elements for ISF-10.
Name of the ocean vessel carrying the cargo to the United States. Required for CBP to identify the specific voyage.
Unique identifier for the specific voyage of the vessel. Assigned by the carrier.

description: Unit of measure for the authorized quantity (e.g., kilograms, pieces, liters, cubic meters). Aligns with customs declaration unit standards.

Date on which the license or permit expires and can no longer be used for customs declarations. After this date, the authorization is no longer valid unless renewed.
reference: World Trade Organization (WTO) Import Licensing Procedures Agreement

description: Alphanumeric code identifying the export control classification for dual-use or controlled technology items. Used for compliance with export control regulations and dual-use export authorizations.

Name of the government agency, regulatory body, or customs authority that issued the license or permit. Examples include national customs administrations, agriculture departments, or trade ministries.
Three-letter ISO country code of the jurisdiction that issued the license or permit.
Timestamp when this license or permit record was most recently updated in the data platform. Used for change tracking and data freshness monitoring.
Official license or permit number issued by the regulatory authority. This is the externally-known unique identifier used for customs declarations and trade compliance filings.

description: Free-text field for additional comments, clarifications, or internal annotations related to this license or permit. Used for operational context and compliance documentation.

description: License number of the previous authorization if this is a renewal. Provides audit trail and continuity for renewed licenses.
Flag indicating whether this license or permit is subject to a tariff rate quota (TRQ) or quantitative import/export restriction. True if quota applies, false otherwise.

description: Available balance of monetary value that can still be utilized under this license or permit. Calculated as authorized_value_amount minus utilized_value_amount.
reference: World Trade Organization (WTO) Import Licensing Procedures Agreement

description: Flag indicating whether this license or permit is a renewal of a previously expired authorization. True if renewal, false if original issuance.
reference: World Trade Organization (WTO) Import Licensing Procedures Agreement

description: Flag indicating whether the goods covered by this license are classified as restricted or controlled items requiring special authorization. True if restricted, false otherwise.
Date on which the license or permit was permanently revoked by the issuing authority. Indicates when the authorization was terminated prior to its natural expiry date.
Explanation or regulatory justification for the revocation of this license or permit. Provides context for compliance enforcement and audit trail.
Name of the operational system from which this license or permit record was extracted. Typically Descartes Customs GTM trade compliance module or equivalent customs management system.
Unique identifier of this license or permit record in the source operational system. Used for data lineage and reconciliation with upstream systems.

Additional terms, restrictions, or conditions imposed by the issuing authority on the use of this license or permit. May include handling requirements, reporting obligations, or geographic restrictions.
reference: World Trade Organization (WTO) Import Licensing Procedures Agreement

description: Date on which the license or permit was suspended by the issuing authority. Indicates when the authorization was temporarily invalidated due to compliance issues or regulatory action.
Explanation or regulatory justification for the suspension of this license or permit. Provides context for compliance review and remediation.
Four-digit UN number identifying the dangerous goods classification if dangerous_goods_indicator is true. Used for hazmat compliance and transport safety.
reference: UN Recommendations on the Transport of Dangerous Goods

description: Cumulative quantity of goods that have been imported or exported against this license or permit to date. Measured in the same unit as authorized_quantity.
Cumulative monetary value of goods that have been imported or exported against this license or permit to date. Expressed in the same currency as authorized_value_amount.

Actual date and time when the asset assignment ended. Captured from GPS telematics, RFID tag reads, or manual check-out. Used for On-Time In-Full (OTIF) performance measurement and variance analysis against planned end. Null for in-progress assignments.

Actual date and time when the asset assignment began. Captured from GPS telematics, RFID tag reads, or manual check-in. Used for On-Time Departure (OTD) performance measurement and variance analysis against planned start.

Total operational cost incurred for this assignment including fuel, driver wages, tolls, and maintenance. Used for OPEX tracking, profitability analysis, and cost-per-kilometer KPI calculation. Captured in the companys base currency.

Planned or actual duration of the assignment in hours. Used for fleet utilization KPI calculation, driver hours-of-service compliance, and asset availability forecasting. Calculated from start and end datetimes.
reference: US Department of Transportation (DOT) - Hours of Service Regulations

description: Business-readable unique identifier for the assignment, typically generated by the Transportation Management System (TMS). Used for operational tracking and communication.

Current lifecycle state of the assignment. Planned indicates scheduled but not confirmed, confirmed for approved assignments, in progress for active assignments, completed for finished assignments, cancelled for voided assignments, suspended for temporarily halted assignments, failed for assignments that could not be completed. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: planned|confirmed|in_progress|completed|cancelled|suspended|failed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Classification of the assignment purpose. Route execution indicates active transport duty, depot standby for available assets, charter for third-party rental, maintenance hold for scheduled service, repair for unscheduled fixes, inspection for regulatory checks, lease out for external rental, reserve for contingency, training for crew qualification, emergency deployment for urgent needs. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: route_execution|depot_standby|charter|maintenance_hold|repair|inspection|lease_out|reserve|training|emergency_deployment — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Total volume of cargo loaded on the asset during this assignment in cubic meters. Used for capacity utilization analysis and dimensional weight (DIM weight) calculations. Null for non-cargo assignments.
reference: International Air Transport Association (IATA) - Cargo Measurement Standards

description: Total weight of cargo loaded on the asset during this assignment in kilograms. Used for load optimization, weight compliance checks, and fuel consumption correlation analysis. Null for non-cargo assignments.
reference: International Maritime Organization (IMO) - SOLAS Weight Verification

business_glossary_term: Carbon Dioxide Equivalent (CO2e) Emissions in Kilograms (kg)
description: Total greenhouse gas emissions in kilograms of CO2 equivalent produced during this assignment. Calculated from fuel consumption using emission factors. Used for sustainability reporting, EU Emissions Trading System (EU ETS) compliance, and Carbon Offsetting and Reduction Scheme for International Aviation (CORSIA) reporting.
reference: ISO 14064 - Greenhouse Gas (GHG) Accounting and Verification

description: Status of regulatory compliance checks for this assignment including DOT inspections, ICAO airworthiness, IMO safety checks, and customs pre-clearance. Passed indicates all checks cleared, failed for violations, pending for in-progress checks, not required for exempt assignments, waived for authorized exceptions.
reference: US Department of Transportation (DOT) - Compliance and Enforcement

description: Date and time when this assignment record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage tracking, and record lifecycle management. Automatically populated by the system.
Boolean flag indicating whether the assignment involves crossing international borders requiring customs clearance and trade compliance. True for international assignments, false for domestic.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the assignment cost amount. Typically the companys base operating currency (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).

Total duration of delays in minutes. Calculated as the difference between planned and actual completion times. Used for Service Level Agreement (SLA) compliance measurement and root cause analysis. Zero if no delay occurred.

Standardized code indicating the primary reason for any delay in completing the assignment. Weather for adverse conditions, traffic for congestion, mechanical for breakdowns, customs for clearance delays, loading delay for shipper delays, driver unavailable for crew issues, route change for diversions, accident for incidents, other for miscellaneous, none if no delay occurred. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: weather|traffic|mechanical|customs|loading_delay|driver_unavailable|route_change|accident|other|none — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Planned or actual distance covered during the assignment in kilometers. Used for fuel consumption analysis, maintenance scheduling based on mileage, and route optimization. Captured from GPS telematics or route planning systems.
reference: ISO 80000-3 - Quantities and Units of Space and Time

description: Actual fuel consumed during the assignment in liters. Captured from vehicle telematics, fuel card transactions, or manual fuel logs. Used for OPEX tracking, Carbon Dioxide Equivalent (CO2e) emissions calculation, and fuel efficiency KPIs.
reference: ISO 14064 - Greenhouse Gas (GHG) Accounting and Verification

description: Boolean flag indicating whether real-time GPS tracking is enabled for this assignment. True if telematics data is being captured, false otherwise. Used for real-time visibility and predictive ETA calculation.

Boolean flag indicating whether the assignment involves transport of hazardous materials requiring special handling, placarding, and regulatory compliance. True if hazmat cargo is present, false otherwise.
reference: IMDG Code - International Maritime Dangerous Goods Code

description: Boolean flag indicating whether any incident (accident, breakdown, delay, cargo damage, security breach) was reported during this assignment. True if an incident occurred, false otherwise. Links to incident or claims management systems.

Date of the most recent regulatory or safety inspection performed on the asset prior to or during this assignment. Used for compliance tracking and maintenance scheduling. Null if no inspection has been performed.

Percentage of the assets maximum capacity utilized during this assignment. Calculated as (actual cargo weight or volume / maximum capacity) * 100. Key performance indicator for fleet efficiency and load optimization.
Date and time when this assignment record was last modified. Used for audit trail, change tracking, and data synchronization. Automatically updated by the system on any change.

Free-text field for operational notes, special instructions, or comments related to the assignment. Used for communication between dispatchers, drivers, and operations teams. May include handling instructions, customer requirements, or incident details.

Scheduled date and time when the asset assignment is planned to end. Used for capacity planning and turnaround time calculation. Corresponds to Estimated Time of Arrival (ETA) for route assignments. Null for open-ended assignments.
Scheduled date and time when the asset assignment is planned to begin. Used for capacity planning and resource scheduling. Corresponds to Estimated Time of Departure (ETD) for route assignments.

Business priority of the assignment. Critical for time-sensitive or high-value shipments, high for express delivery, normal for standard service, low for deferred or backhaul assignments. Influences resource allocation and scheduling decisions.

Unique identifier of the RFID tag attached to the asset for automated tracking and identification. Used for gate-in/gate-out automation, asset location tracking, and inventory management. Null if RFID is not used.

Unique identifier of the security seal applied to the asset (container, trailer, or cargo compartment) to ensure cargo integrity and prevent tampering. Required for customs compliance and high-security shipments. Null if no seal is applied.

Service level agreement (SLA) tier for this assignment. Express for next-day or same-day delivery, standard for 2-5 day delivery, economy for 5+ day delivery, charter for exclusive use, dedicated for contract logistics. Determines performance targets and pricing.

Code identifying the operational system of record that originated this assignment. SAP TM for SAP Transportation Management, ORACLE TMS for Oracle Transportation Management System, MANHATTAN WMS for warehouse-initiated assignments, FOURKITES for visibility platform assignments, MANUAL for manually created assignments, OTHER for miscellaneous sources. Used for data lineage and system integration tracking.
Boolean flag indicating whether the assignment requires temperature-controlled transport (refrigerated or heated). True for cold chain or temperature-sensitive cargo, false otherwise.
Unique identifier for the asset inspection record. Primary key.

The date on which the issued regulatory certificate expires, triggering the need for a subsequent inspection to maintain compliance.

description: The date the regulatory certificate or compliance document was officially issued following successful inspection.
Unique identifier of the regulatory certificate or compliance document issued. Used for regulatory reporting and audit verification.

description: The date by which all required corrective actions must be completed to maintain regulatory compliance and operational status.

Additional free-text notes, observations, or comments recorded by the inspector that provide context beyond structured fields.
Externally-known unique identifier or reference number for this inspection event, often used for regulatory reporting and audit trails.

description: Precise timestamp when the inspection commenced. Used for operational tracking and labor cost allocation.
Current lifecycle state of the inspection event. Tracks progression from scheduling through completion or cancellation.

description: Reference number or identifier for any regulatory filing, report, or submission made to authorities as a result of this inspection (e.g., DOT inspection report number, ICAO occurrence report).

Unique surrogate identifier for each individual transport leg within a service corridor. Primary key for the corridor_leg entity in the Silver Layer lakehouse.

description: Reference to the network node (hub, port, airport, depot, ICD, CFS, or warehouse) where this leg terminates. Together with origin_node_id defines the origin-destination (OD) pair for this leg.

description: Foreign key linking to route.lane. Business justification: A corridor_leg represents a single node-to-node movement segment within a service corridor. A lane is the master origin-destination corridor definition for exactly that type of movement. Linking corri
Reference to the network node (hub, port, airport, depot, ICD, CFS, or warehouse) where this leg originates. Supports hub-and-spoke topology decomposition in SAP TM and Oracle TMS.
reference: SAP TM Transportation Lane; Oracle TMS Network Design

description: Reference to the parent service corridor that this leg belongs to. A corridor is composed of one or more sequential legs representing the full origin-to-destination route.

business_glossary_term: CO2 Equivalent Emission Factor (kg CO2e per tonne-km)
description: Carbon dioxide equivalent (CO2e) emission factor in kg per tonne-kilometre for this leg's transport mode and vehicle type. Used to calculate shipment-level GHG emissions for EU ETS reporting, CORSIA compliance (air legs), and customer sustainability reporting.

description: Timestamp when this corridor leg record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides audit trail for data lineage, SOX compliance, and Silver Layer ingestion tracking.

description: Latest local time (HH:MM, 24-hour) by which freight must be tendered at the origin node to be accepted on this leg's scheduled departure. Critical for shipment booking deadlines in Oracle TMS and customer SLA management.

description: 7-character binary string (Mon=pos1 through Sun=pos7) indicating which days of the week this leg operates departures (1=operates, 0=does not operate). Enables precise booking calendar generation in SAP TM and Oracle TMS scheduling.

description: Frequency at which this leg operates departures. Drives shipment booking availability windows, capacity planning in Blue Yonder, and customer-facing transit time commitments. ON_DEMAND applies to charter and FTL legs.

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the leg destination. Used for import/export compliance, denied party screening in Descartes, and bilateral trade lane reporting.

description: Standardized location code for the leg destination — IATA airport code (3-letter) for air legs, UN/LOCODE (5-character) for ocean/road/rail legs. Used in AWB, BOL, and HAWB generation and customs declarations in Descartes.

description: Physical distance of this transport leg in kilometres. Used for freight rate calculation (per-km tariffs), GHG/CO2e emissions estimation per EU ETS and CORSIA requirements, and network optimization in Blue Yonder.

description: Date from which this corridor leg definition becomes operationally valid and available for shipment assignment. Supports seasonal route activation, network redesign rollouts from Blue Yonder, and historical lane analytics.

description: Date after which this corridor leg definition is no longer operationally valid. Null indicates an open-ended lane with no planned expiry. Used for seasonal route deactivation, carrier contract expiry alignment, and network change management.

description: Indicates whether this leg is enrolled in FourKites real-time visibility tracking (True). When enabled, GPS/IoT telemetry is streamed for predictive ETA updates and exception management. Supports independent leg trackability as defined in the corridor_leg product scope.

description: International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) applicable to this leg, defining the point of risk and cost transfer between shipper and consignee. description: Date on which this corridor leg definition was last reviewed and validated by the network planning team. Supports data quality governance, periodic lane review cycles, and audit trail for network optimization decisions.

description: Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for this corridor leg, used in SAP TM route planning, Oracle TMS load optimization, and EDI interchange with carriers. Unique within the parent corridor.

description: Ordinal position of this leg within the parent corridor, starting at 1. Defines the execution order for multi-modal route decomposition (e.g., leg 1 = road pickup, leg 2 = air haul, leg 3 = road delivery). Aligns with Oracle TMS load optimization sequencing.
reference: SAP TM Stage Sequence; Oracle TMS Shipment Leg Sequence

description: Current operational lifecycle status of this corridor leg. ACTIVE legs are available for shipment assignment; SUSPENDED legs are temporarily unavailable; DISCONTINUED legs are retired from the network. Governs availability in Oracle TMS load optimization.

reference: SAP TM Charge Calculation; Oracle TMS Load Optimization; Blue Yonder Inventory Optimization

description: Maximum volumetric capacity in Cubic Metres (CBM) available on this leg per departure. Used alongside max_weight_capacity_kg for DIM weight-based load optimization and capacity utilization analytics in Oracle TMS.

description: Maximum chargeable or physical weight capacity in kilograms that this leg can accommodate per departure. Used for load optimization in Oracle TMS, capacity planning in Blue Yonder, and freight booking acceptance logic.
reference: IATA Weight and Balance; IMO SOLAS Container Weight Verification; Oracle TMS Load Optimization

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the leg origin. Used for cross-border compliance checks, customs filing, AEO/C-TPAT screening, and trade lane analytics.

description: Standardized location code for the leg origin — IATA airport code (3-letter) for air legs, UN/LOCODE (5-character) for ocean/road/rail legs. Used in AWB, BOL, and HAWB generation and AMS/ISF filings.
reference: IATA Airport Codes; UN/LOCODE (United Nations Code for Trade and Transport Locations)

description: Free-text operational notes or remarks about this corridor leg, such as seasonal restrictions, port congestion advisories, special handling requirements, or network planning rationale. Intended for network planners and operations teams.

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this corridor leg definition was sourced. description: Standard local departure time (HH:MM, 24-hour) for this leg at the origin node. Used as the baseline Estimated Time of Departure (ETD) for shipment scheduling, FourKites tracking initialization, and customer notification triggers.

description: Contracted or standard transit time for this leg expressed in decimal hours. Used as the baseline for ETA calculation, SLA commitment derivation, and predictive ETA modeling in FourKites. Sourced from SAP TM transit time matrix.

description: Maximum temperature in degrees Celsius maintained during transport on this leg. Applicable only when is_temperature_controlled is True. Used for cold chain compliance validation and cargo acceptance rules.

description: Minimum temperature in degrees Celsius maintained during transport on this leg. Applicable only when is_temperature_controlled is True. Used for cold chain compliance validation and cargo acceptance rules.

description: Unique identifier for the hub-and-spoke network configuration record. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to fleet.fleet_depot. Business justification: Hub-spoke configurations define which depots serve as spokes (in addition to hub_node_id); critical for fleet deployment planning, asset allocation, and last-mile service design.
Approval status of the configuration before it becomes active. Ensures governance and sign-off by network planning and operations leadership.
Name or identifier of the person who approved this hub-spoke configuration for operational use.
Date and time when this hub-spoke configuration was approved. Supports audit trail and compliance reporting.
Strategy for allocating carriers to hub-spoke lanes: dedicated carriers per lane, shared carrier pool, dynamic selection via TMS, or contract-based assignment.
Business identifier code for the hub-spoke configuration, used for external reference and reporting. Typically follows a naming convention combining region, service product, and version.
Human-readable name describing the hub-spoke configuration, typically including geographic scope and service type (e.g., 'APAC Express Hub-Spoke Q1 2024').
Current lifecycle status of the hub-spoke configuration. Only active configurations are used for operational route planning and shipment execution.
Version number of this configuration. Incremented when the configuration is updated, supporting change tracking and audit trails.
Daily cutoff time (HH:MM format, hub local time) by which shipments must arrive at the hub to be included in the next consolidation cycle.

Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes covered by this hub-spoke configuration. Defines the geographic boundary for customs and trade compliance.
Date and time when this hub-spoke configuration record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage.
Indicates whether customs clearance is required at the hub for cross-border shipments in this configuration. Triggers Descartes Customs integration.
Indicates whether direct spoke-to-spoke or spoke-to-destination lanes are allowed when volume/weight thresholds are met, bypassing the hub.
Date on which this hub-spoke configuration expires or is superseded by a new configuration. Null indicates an open-ended configuration.

Maximum handling capacity of the hub in Twenty-foot Equivalent Units (TEU) for containerized freight. Used for capacity planning and network design.
Date and time when this hub-spoke configuration record was last updated. Supports change tracking and audit compliance.
Maximum allowable transit time in hours from the hub to any spoke node. Critical for On-Time Delivery (OTD) and On-Time In-Full (OTIF) performance.
Maximum allowable transit time in hours from any spoke node to the hub. Used for SLA validation, route planning, and network design optimization.
Minimum shipment volume in cubic meters (CBM) required to bypass the hub and establish a direct lane from spoke to destination. Used for load optimization and cost efficiency.
Minimum shipment weight in kilograms required to qualify for direct lane bypass, avoiding hub consolidation. Supports FTL and FCL optimization logic.
Identifier of the user or system that last modified this hub-spoke configuration record. Supports accountability and audit trails.
Reference to the Blue Yonder network design model or optimization run that generated or validated this hub-spoke configuration.
Geographic region or market area covered by this hub-spoke configuration (e.g., 'North America', 'APAC', 'EU West'). Used for regional network planning and optimization.
Free-text notes or comments about this hub-spoke configuration, including special handling instructions, seasonal adjustments, or operational constraints.
Primary objective used by Blue Yonder and SAP TM for network optimization: minimize cost, maximize speed, maximize reliability, or balanced multi-objective.

description: Target On-Time In-Full percentage for shipments routed through this hub-spoke configuration. Measures both timeliness and completeness of delivery.
Total number of spoke nodes included in this hub-spoke configuration. Used for network complexity analysis and capacity planning.
Comma-separated list of spoke node codes or identifiers that are served by this hub. Represents the delivery/pickup points connected to the hub in this configuration.
