description: United Nations Code for Trade and Transport Locations (UN/LOCODE) for the lane origin node. Five-character code used in EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) messages, customs declarations, and Bill of Lading (BOL) documentation.
reference: UNECE UN/LOCODE; WCO Customs Data Model; IATA Resolution 600

description: Free-text operational notes or remarks about this lane, capturing special handling instructions, carrier-specific requirements, or network planning context not captured in structured fields. Used by operations and network planning teams.

Name of the regulatory body or internal authority that imposed the current lane restriction (e.g., 'IMO', 'IATA', 'US DOT', 'WCO', 'Internal Network Operations'). Required for compliance audit and regulatory reporting.
reference: IMO; IATA; US DOT; WCO; ISO 28000

description: Date from which the current operational restriction on this lane becomes effective. Used by SAP TM to exclude the lane from carrier selection and by Descartes Customs for trade compliance enforcement.
reference: SAP TM Operational Restrictions; Descartes Global Trade Management

description: Date on which the current operational restriction on this lane expires and normal operations resume. Null indicates an indefinite restriction. Monitored by Blue Yonder for network re-optimization upon restriction lift.
reference: SAP TM Operational Restrictions; Blue Yonder Network Design

description: Categorical type of the most recently applied operational restriction on this lane. Used for exception reporting and compliance audit trails. Detailed restriction history is maintained in the lane_restriction event log.
reference: Descartes Global Trade Management; SAP TM Operational Restrictions; WCO

description: External lane identifier from SAP TM (Transportation Management) used for route planning, freight order management, charge calculation, and carrier selection. Enables bidirectional synchronization between the Silver Layer and SAP TM.
reference: SAP TM Lane Master; SAP S/4HANA Integration

description: Contractual standard transit time in calendar days from origin pickup to destination delivery for this lane. Used as the baseline for SLA compliance measurement, On-Time Delivery (OTD) KPI calculation, and Estimated Time of Arrival (ETA) computation in FourKites.
reference: SAP TM Transit Time Management; FourKites Predictive ETA; IATA Cargo Services Conference

description: Primary mode of transport for this lane: air (IATA-governed), ocean (IMO-governed), road (DOT-governed), rail, or multimodal (combination). Determines applicable regulatory framework, carrier pool, and rate structure in Oracle TMS.
reference: IATA Resolution 600; IMO SOLAS; US DOT 49 CFR; ISO 28000

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this lane record. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change data capture, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks Silver Layer.

Indicates whether FourKites real-time GPS/IoT shipment tracking and predictive ETA calculation is enabled for shipments on this lane. Drives exception management and proactive customer notification workflows.
reference: FourKites Real-Time Visibility Platform; ISO 28000 Supply Chain Security

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each physical or logical node in the transport network. Primary key for the network_node master record.

Foreign key linking to network.network_partner. Business justification: Many terminals, warehouses, and cross-docks are operated by third-party partners. Required for partner performance tracking, operational coordination, cost allocation, and partner SLA compliance monit
Primary street address of the network node facility. Used for carrier pickup/delivery instructions, customs documentation in Descartes, and last-mile routing in Oracle TMS.
reference: GDPR Article 4; ISO 28000 Supply Chain Security Management

description: Indicates whether the node or its operating entity holds Authorized Economic Operator (AEO) certification, enabling expedited customs clearance and reduced inspections. True = AEO certified. Used in Descartes Customs for trade lane compliance scoring.
reference: AEO Authorized Economic Operator Program; WCO World Customs Organization; C-TPAT Customs-Trade Partnership Against Terrorism

description: Node identifier as defined in Blue Yonder Supply Chain Planning for network design, demand forecasting, and inventory optimization models. Used to synchronize network topology between the lakehouse silver layer and Blue Yonder planning outputs.
Name of the city or municipality where the network node is located. Used for geographic display in shipment tracking, carrier selection, and last-mile delivery zone mapping.

Node-level Carbon Dioxide Equivalent (CO2e) emission factor in kg CO2e per CBM processed, used for Greenhouse Gas (GHG) footprint calculation in sustainability reporting. Supports EU Emissions Trading System (EU ETS) and CORSIA compliance reporting.
reference: EU Emissions Trading System (EU ETS); CORSIA Carbon Offsetting and Reduction Scheme for International Aviation; GHG Protocol

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code of the country where the node is physically located (e.g., 'HKG', 'SGP', 'USA'). Used for customs compliance routing in Descartes, trade lane classification, and regulatory reporting.
reference: ISO 3166-1; WCO World Customs Organization; WTO World Trade Organization

description: Timestamp when this network node record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides audit trail for data lineage and SOX compliance.

Indicates whether the node is C-TPAT certified, confirming compliance with US Customs and Border Protection supply chain security standards. True = C-TPAT certified. Required for US-bound shipment routing and ISF filing in Descartes.
reference: C-TPAT Customs-Trade Partnership Against Terrorism; US DOT Department of Transportation

description: Indicates whether the node operates as a customs bonded warehouse or zone, allowing storage of goods under customs control without duty payment until release. True = bonded facility. Used in Descartes Customs for trade compliance routing and ISF/AMS filing.
reference: WCO World Customs Organization; Descartes Customs Global Trade Management; C-TPAT Customs-Trade Partnership Against Terrorism

description: Indicates whether the node is certified and equipped to handle Dangerous Goods (DG) shipments. True = DG-certified facility. Used in SAP TM carrier and route selection to ensure DG shipments are only routed through compliant nodes per IMDG Code and ICAO Technical Instructions.
reference: IMDG Code International Maritime Dangerous Goods; ICAO Technical Instructions for Dangerous Goods; IATA Dangerous Goods Regulations

description: Date from which this network node record is valid and the node is available for route planning and shipment assignment. Used for temporal validity management in SAP TM and Blue Yonder network versioning.

Date after which this network node record is no longer valid. Null indicates the node is open-ended with no planned end date. Used for network topology versioning, decommissioning planning, and historical route analysis.

Location identifier assigned to this node in the FourKites Real-Time Visibility Platform. Used to correlate network node records with live shipment tracking events, predictive ETA calculations, and exception alerts from FourKites.

Indicates whether the node is located within or operates as a Free Trade Zone (FTZ), enabling duty-free handling and value-added services. True = FTZ-designated node. Used for trade compliance routing in Descartes and DDP/DAP Incoterms processing.
reference: WCO World Customs Organization; WTO World Trade Organization; FMC Federal Maritime Commission

description: Maximum volumetric throughput capacity of the node measured in Cubic Meters (CBM) per day. Used for load optimization in Oracle TMS, capacity planning in Blue Yonder, and network bottleneck analysis.
reference: Blue Yonder Supply Chain Planning; Oracle TMS

description: Hierarchical tier of the node within the hub-and-spoke network topology. 'primary' = global mega-hub (e.g., main international gateway); 'secondary' = regional hub; 'tertiary' = country-level distribution center; 'spoke' = local delivery depot. Drives routing hierarchy in SAP TM and Blue Yonder network design.
reference: Blue Yonder Supply Chain Planning; SAP TM Transportation Management

business_glossary_term: International Air Transport Association (IATA) Location Code
description: Three-letter IATA airport or city code assigned to the node where applicable (e.g., 'HKG', 'SIN', 'LHR'). Used for air freight routing, House Air Waybill (HAWB) and Master Air Waybill (MAWB) origin/destination coding, and IATA-compliant carrier communications. Null for non-air nodes.
reference: IATA International Air Transport Association; ICAO International Civil Aviation Organization

business_glossary_term: International Civil Aviation Organization (ICAO) Airport Code
description: Four-letter ICAO airport designator for air cargo terminal nodes (e.g., 'VHHH' for Hong Kong International). Used for air freight routing, MAWB/HAWB coding, and ICAO regulatory compliance. Null for non-airport nodes.
reference: ICAO International Civil Aviation Organization; IATA International Air Transport Association

description: Seven-digit IMO port facility code assigned to sea port nodes. Used for ocean freight Bill of Lading (BOL) routing, vessel manifest filing, and IMO regulatory compliance reporting. Null for non-port nodes.
reference: IMO International Maritime Organization; FMC Federal Maritime Commission

description: WGS-84 decimal latitude coordinate of the network node (e.g., 22.308901 for Hong Kong International Airport). Used for GPS-based distance calculations, FourKites real-time shipment proximity tracking, and Blue Yonder network design optimization.
reference: ISO 6709 Geographic Coordinates; FourKites Real-Time Visibility Platform; WGS-84 Standard

description: WGS-84 decimal longitude coordinate of the network node (e.g., 113.914603 for Hong Kong International Airport). Used in conjunction with latitude for geospatial route planning, ETA calculation, and IoT-based asset tracking integration.
reference: ISO 6709 Geographic Coordinates; FourKites Real-Time Visibility Platform; WGS-84 Standard

business_glossary_term: Maximum Weight Handling Capacity in Kilograms
description: Maximum gross weight in kilograms that the node can handle per shipment or per day (context defined by operational policy). Used for load optimization in Oracle TMS and freight order feasibility checks in SAP TM.
reference: SAP TM Transportation Management; Oracle TMS; IMO International Maritime Organization

description: Externally-known, human-readable unique business code assigned to the node (e.g., 'HKG-HUB-01', 'SIN-CFS-02'). Used as the operational reference key in SAP TM route planning, Blue Yonder network design, and FourKites visibility integration.
reference: SAP TM Transportation Management; Blue Yonder Supply Chain Planning

description: Full descriptive name of the network node (e.g., 'Hong Kong International Gateway Hub', 'Singapore Container Freight Station'). Used in operational reports, carrier communications, and customer-facing shipment tracking.
reference: SAP TM Transportation Management; ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems

description: Current lifecycle status of the network node. Controls whether the node is available for route planning and shipment assignment in SAP TM and Oracle TMS. 'active' = fully operational; 'inactive' = temporarily not in use; 'suspended' = operations halted pending review; 'under_construction' = not yet operational; 'decommissioned' = permanently retired from the network.
reference: SAP TM Transportation Management; ISO 28000 Supply Chain Security Management

description: Classification of the node by its operational role in the transport network. Drives hub-and-spoke topology logic in SAP TM and Blue Yonder. Values: hub (major consolidation/distribution center), gateway (international entry/exit point), sorting_center (parcel sorting facility), warehouse (storage and fulfillment), port (sea port terminal), airport (air cargo terminal), icd (Inland Container Depot), cfs (Container Freight Station), ftz (Free Trade Zone), last_mile_depot (final delivery depot). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hub|gateway|sorting_center|warehouse|port|airport|icd|cfs|ftz|last_mile_depot — promote to reference product]
reference: IMO International Maritime Organization; IATA International Air Transport Association; WCO World Customs Organization

description: Comma-separated list of operating days using ISO weekday abbreviations (e.g., 'MON,TUE,WED,THU,FRI' or 'MON,TUE,WED,THU,FRI,SAT'). Used for freight scheduling, carrier dispatch planning, and SLA transit time calculation in SAP TM.
Local time at which the node ceases operations each day in HH:MM 24-hour format (e.g., '22:00'). Used by SAP TM for cut-off time enforcement, freight order scheduling, and SLA breach risk assessment.
reference: SAP TM Transportation Management; Oracle TMS

description: Local time at which the node begins operations each day in HH:MM 24-hour format (e.g., '06:00'). Used by SAP TM for freight order scheduling, carrier appointment booking, and last-mile delivery window planning.
reference: SAP TM Transportation Management; Oracle TMS

description: Indicates whether the node has the equipment and infrastructure to handle oversized or out-of-gauge cargo (e.g., heavy lift cranes, wide-load bays). True = oversized cargo capable. Used in SAP TM special cargo routing.
reference: SAP TM Transportation Management; US DOT Department of Transportation

description: Node code of the parent node in the hub-and-spoke hierarchy (e.g., a spoke depot's parent is its regional hub). Enables hierarchical network topology traversal for route planning and network performance roll-up reporting. Self-referencing relationship resolved in the linking step.
reference: SAP TM Transportation Management; Blue Yonder Supply Chain Planning

description: Postal or ZIP code of the network node facility address. Used for last-mile delivery zone assignment, carrier rate lookup, and geographic clustering in Blue Yonder network optimization.
reference: GDPR Article 4; Universal Postal Union (UPU) Standards

description: Internal geographic region code grouping the node into a commercial or operational region (e.g., 'APAC', 'EMEA', 'AMER', 'MEA'). Used for network performance reporting, regional P&L allocation in SAP S/4HANA Finance, and Blue Yonder network design segmentation.
reference: Blue Yonder Supply Chain Planning; SAP S/4HANA Finance

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this network node record was sourced (e.g., 'SAP_TM', 'BLUE_YONDER'). Used for data lineage tracking, reconciliation, and master data management governance in the Databricks lakehouse.
reference: ISO 28000 Supply Chain Security Management; SOX Sarbanes-Oxley Act

description: Maximum static storage capacity of the node measured in Cubic Meters (CBM). Used for warehouse capacity planning in Manhattan WMS, inventory optimization in Blue Yonder, and network design decisions.
reference: Manhattan WMS; Blue Yonder Supply Chain Planning

description: Maximum temperature in degrees Celsius that the node's temperature-controlled facility can maintain (e.g., 8.00 for refrigerated). Null if temperature_controlled is False. Used for cold-chain shipment routing and cargo integrity compliance.
reference: IATA Perishable Cargo Regulations; ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems

description: Minimum temperature in degrees Celsius that the node's temperature-controlled facility can maintain (e.g., -25.00 for deep freeze). Null if temperature_controlled is False. Used for cold-chain shipment routing and SLA compliance validation.
reference: IATA Perishable Cargo Regulations; ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems

description: Indicates whether the node has temperature-controlled storage and handling capabilities (cold chain). True = facility supports temperature-sensitive cargo. Used for pharmaceutical, perishable, and cold-chain shipment routing in SAP TM.
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems; IATA Perishable Cargo Regulations

description: Maximum container throughput capacity of the node measured in Twenty-foot Equivalent Units (TEU) per day. Applicable to port, ICD, CFS, and FTZ nodes. Used for ocean freight capacity planning and FCL/LCL load optimization in Oracle TMS.
reference: IMO International Maritime Organization; FMC Federal Maritime Commission

description: IANA time zone identifier for the node's local time zone (e.g., 'Asia/Hong_Kong', 'America/New_York', 'Europe/London'). Critical for accurate ETD/ETA calculation, operating hours enforcement in SAP TM, and SLA compliance measurement across time zones.
reference: IANA Time Zone Database; IATA International Air Transport Association

description: Location identifier code as registered in SAP TM and Oracle TMS for route planning, freight order management, and carrier selection. Serves as the cross-system reference key for transport execution systems.
reference: SAP TM Transportation Management; Oracle TMS

description: Primary transport mode served by this network node. Determines applicable regulatory frameworks (IATA for air, IMO for ocean, DOT for road/rail) and routing logic in SAP TM. 'multimodal' indicates the node serves multiple modes (e.g., an intermodal terminal).
reference: IATA International Air Transport Association; IMO International Maritime Organization; US DOT Department of Transportation

description: Five-character UN/LOCODE identifying the geographic location of the node (e.g., 'HKHKG', 'SGSIN'). Used for Bill of Lading (BOL), customs declarations in Descartes, and EDI messaging per WCO standards. Provides a globally standardized location reference across all transport modes.
reference: WCO World Customs Organization; UNECE UN/LOCODE Standard

description: Timestamp when this network node record was last modified, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks lakehouse silver layer and audit trail maintenance.

Facility identifier code as registered in Manhattan WMS for nodes that perform warehousing and fulfillment operations. Used to link network node records to WMS inventory, labor, and slotting data. Null for non-warehouse nodes.

Unique surrogate identifier for the delivery zone record. Primary key for the route_delivery_zone data product. Entity role: MASTER_RESOURCE — represents a geographic zone asset managed by Transport Shipping for last-mile routing and serviceability.

Foreign key linking to route.network_node. Business justification: route_delivery_zone is currently SILOED (no inbound or outbound FKs). It has hub_node_code (STRING) which is a denormalized code reference to the hub serving this last-mile zone. Adding hub_node_id as

Foreign key linking to network.agent. Business justification: Last-mile delivery zones are frequently serviced by local agents. Required for agent commission calculation, service coverage validation, and agent performance tracking. Essential for agent-based dist

Foreign key linking to fleet.fleet_depot. Business justification: Delivery zones are served from specific depots; linking enables territory management, last-mile route planning, depot workload balancing, and service coverage analysis.

Indicates whether AEO (Authorized Economic Operator) certification is required for carriers operating within this zone. Relevant for cross-border and customs-controlled zones. Enforced during carrier selection in Descartes Customs and SAP TM.
reference: AEO Program; WCO SAFE Framework; C-TPAT; Descartes Customs GTM

description: Pipe-separated list of service product codes eligible for delivery within this zone (e.g., 'EXPRESS|STANDARD|ECONOMY|FREIGHT'). Drives service product availability validation during shipment booking and carrier selection in SAP TM. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product for zone-service-product association]
reference: SAP TM Service Product Configuration; IATA Service Level Standards

description: Total geographic area of the delivery zone in square kilometres. Used in network density analysis, carrier capacity planning, and cost-per-delivery benchmarking in Blue Yonder network design.

Code of the primary carrier or last-mile delivery partner assigned to service this zone. Used in carrier selection logic within SAP TM and Oracle TMS. May be overridden by dynamic carrier selection based on capacity and cost.
reference: SAP TM Carrier Selection; Oracle TMS Carrier Configuration; IATA Carrier Codes

description: Name of the primary city or municipality associated with the delivery zone. Used for last-mile dispatch assignment, carrier lane allocation, and customer-facing serviceability lookup.
reference: SAP TM Zone Configuration; IATA Location Codes

business_glossary_term: Carbon Dioxide Equivalent (CO2e) Emission Factor (kg per Shipment)
description: Estimated CO2e (Carbon Dioxide Equivalent) emission factor in kilograms per shipment for last-mile delivery within this zone. Used for GHG (Greenhouse Gas) emissions reporting, EU ETS compliance, and CORSIA carbon offsetting calculations.
reference: EU Emissions Trading System (EU ETS); CORSIA; GHG Protocol; ISO 14064

description: Indicates whether Cash on Delivery (COD) service is restricted or unavailable within this delivery zone. COD restrictions may apply due to security concerns, carrier capability limitations, or local regulatory requirements. Referenced during e-commerce fulfillment order validation.
reference: PCI DSS; SAP TM Service Product Configuration

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code where the delivery zone is located (e.g., GBR, SGP, USA, DEU). Used for customs compliance, regulatory routing rules, and cross-border serviceability validation per WCO and IMO standards.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; WCO; IMO; US DOT

description: GeoJSON-encoded polygon or multipolygon representing the precise geographic boundary of the delivery zone. Used for GPS-based serviceability validation, FourKites real-time visibility geofencing, and last-mile dispatch optimization. Stored as serialized GeoJSON string per RFC 7946.
reference: RFC 7946 GeoJSON; FourKites Real-Time Visibility; ISO 28000

description: Timestamp when this delivery zone record was first created in the system (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides audit trail for zone master data governance and SOX compliance.

Classification of the zone from a customs and trade compliance perspective: domestic (standard domestic territory), ftz (Free Trade Zone), bonded (customs bonded area), international (cross-border delivery zone), special_economic (Special Economic Zone). Used in Descartes Customs for trade compliance routing and ISF/AMS filing requirements.
reference: WCO; Descartes Customs GTM; C-TPAT; AEO; US DOT; WTO

description: Indicates whether the transport of dangerous goods (DG) is restricted or prohibited within this delivery zone. Enforced during shipment booking validation and carrier selection. Aligned with IMDG Code, ICAO Technical Instructions, and local regulatory requirements.
reference: IMDG Code; ICAO Technical Instructions for Dangerous Goods; US DOT 49 CFR

description: Date from which this delivery zone definition becomes operationally effective. Used for temporal zone management, ensuring routing and pricing rules apply only within the valid date range. Supports zone versioning and network change management.
reference: SAP TM Zone Validity Configuration; ISO 9001

description: Date after which this delivery zone definition is no longer operationally effective. Null for open-ended zones with no planned expiry. Used for zone versioning, network restructuring, and historical serviceability audit.
reference: SAP TM Zone Validity Configuration; ISO 9001

description: Code of the hub, depot, or distribution center assigned to service this delivery zone in the hub-and-spoke network topology. Drives carrier selection, load optimization, and last-mile dispatch routing in SAP TM and Oracle TMS.
reference: SAP TM Network Configuration; Blue Yonder Network Design

description: Indicates whether an Importer Security Filing (ISF) is required for shipments entering this zone. Applicable to US import zones under CBP 10+2 rule. Used in Descartes Customs for automated compliance filing.
reference: US CBP ISF 10+2 Rule; AMS (Automated Manifest System); Descartes Customs GTM

description: Decimal latitude of the geographic centroid of the delivery zone in WGS84 coordinate system. Used for distance-based routing calculations, GPS geofencing in FourKites, and network optimization in Blue Yonder.
reference: WGS84 Coordinate System; FourKites Real-Time Visibility; Blue Yonder Network Design

description: Decimal longitude of the geographic centroid of the delivery zone in WGS84 coordinate system. Used for distance-based routing calculations, GPS geofencing in FourKites, and network optimization in Blue Yonder.
reference: WGS84 Coordinate System; FourKites Real-Time Visibility; Blue Yonder Network Design

description: Maximum dimensional weight (DIM weight) in kilograms accepted for delivery within this zone. DIM weight is calculated from volumetric dimensions and used alongside actual weight for carrier charge calculation and capacity planning.
reference: IATA DIM Weight Calculation Standards; SAP TM Charge Calculation

description: Maximum allowable length in centimetres for a single shipment piece accepted for delivery within this zone. Used in oversized shipment validation during booking and load optimization in Oracle TMS.
reference: SAP TM Constraint Configuration; IATA Cargo Handling Manual

description: Maximum gross weight in kilograms for a single shipment accepted for delivery within this zone. Enforced during shipment booking validation and load optimization. Reflects infrastructure, vehicle, and carrier capability constraints.
reference: SAP TM Constraint Configuration; US DOT; EU Road Transport Regulations

description: Target On-Time Delivery (OTD) percentage for this zone, expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 98.50 = 98.5%). Used as the KPI benchmark for carrier performance management and network optimization reporting.
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management; SAP TM KPI Configuration

description: Indicates whether oversized or overweight shipments are restricted within this delivery zone due to infrastructure limitations, road access constraints, or local regulations. Used in load optimization and carrier selection in Oracle TMS.
reference: US DOT; EU Road Transport Regulations; SAP TM Constraint Configuration

description: Indicates whether a peak season surcharge is applicable for this zone during designated peak periods (e.g., holiday season, Chinese New Year). Drives dynamic pricing adjustments in SAP TM charge calculation.
reference: SAP TM Charge Calculation; IATA GRI (General Rate Increase) Guidelines

description: Starting postal code of the range covered by this delivery zone. Together with postal_code_to, defines the postal code band for zone membership evaluation. Used in SAP TM zone-based routing and pricing surcharge determination.
reference: SAP TM Zone Configuration; Universal Postal Union (UPU)

description: Ending postal code of the range covered by this delivery zone. Together with postal_code_from, defines the postal code band for zone membership evaluation. Null if the zone is defined by a single postal code or polygon only.
reference: SAP TM Zone Configuration; Universal Postal Union (UPU)

description: Indicates whether a remote area surcharge (RAS) applies to shipments delivered within this zone. Remote area surcharges are applied by carriers for zones with limited accessibility or extended delivery routes. Referenced by the pricing and rating domain for charge calculation.
reference: SAP TM Charge Calculation; IATA Tariff Coordination

description: Indicates whether a residential delivery surcharge applies to shipments within this zone. Residential surcharges are levied by carriers for deliveries to non-commercial addresses. Used in pricing and rating charge calculation for B2C and e-commerce fulfillment.
reference: SAP TM Charge Calculation; FedEx/UPS Residential Surcharge Standards

description: Latest time of day (HH:MM, 24-hour local time) by which a shipment must be tendered to the hub to achieve the standard transit time for this zone. Critical for last-mile dispatch scheduling and customer booking cutoff enforcement.
reference: SAP TM Scheduling; IATA Ground Handling Manual

description: Pipe-separated list of days of the week on which delivery service operates within this zone (e.g., 'MON|TUE|WED|THU|FRI'). Used for ETA calculation, SLA management, and dispatch scheduling. Supports zones with restricted weekend or holiday service.
reference: SAP TM Scheduling; IATA Ground Handling Manual

description: Master serviceability flag indicating whether Transport Shipping currently provides delivery service within this zone. A zone may be defined but temporarily unserviceable due to operational, regulatory, or capacity constraints. Used as the primary gate in shipment booking serviceability checks.

Code referencing the standard SLA profile applicable to this delivery zone. Defines OTD (On-Time Delivery) and OTIF (On-Time In-Full) performance targets. Used in SLA entitlement validation and KPI reporting.
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management; SAP TM SLA Configuration

description: Standard number of business days required to deliver a shipment from the assigned hub node to any address within this zone under normal operating conditions. Used as the baseline for SLA commitment, OTD measurement, and customer ETA communication.
reference: SAP TM SLA Configuration; IATA Ground Handling Manual; ISO 9001

description: ISO 3166-2 subdivision code for the state, province, or region within the country (e.g., US-CA, GB-ENG, AU-NSW). Used for sub-national routing rules, tax jurisdiction mapping, and regional SLA configuration.
reference: ISO 3166-2; US DOT; EU Emissions Trading System

description: Indicates whether temperature-controlled (cold chain) delivery is available within this zone. Used during shipment booking to validate serviceability for pharmaceutical, perishable, and temperature-sensitive cargo.
reference: IATA Perishable Cargo Regulations; ISO 9001; SAP TM Service Product Configuration

description: IANA timezone identifier for the delivery zone (e.g., 'Europe/London', 'Asia/Singapore', 'America/New_York'). Used to correctly interpret service cutoff times, ETA/ETD calculations, and SLA breach detection in FourKites and SAP TM.
reference: IANA Time Zone Database; IATA; ISO 8601

description: Standard transit time expressed in total hours for time-definite and express service products where sub-day precision is required. Complements standard_transit_days for same-day, next-day, and time-critical shipment SLA management.
reference: SAP TM SLA Configuration; IATA Time-Definite Services

description: Primary transport mode used for last-mile delivery within this zone. Determines carrier eligibility, equipment type, and routing logic in SAP TM and Oracle TMS. Multimodal zones support more than one mode.
reference: SAP TM Mode of Transport; IMO; IATA; US DOT; ISO 28000

description: Timestamp when this delivery zone record was last modified (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change data capture (CDC), data lineage tracking, and master data governance audit.

Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the delivery zone across systems (SAP TM, Oracle TMS, FourKites). Used as the business key for zone-based routing, surcharge lookup, and carrier selection. Maps to the zone identifier in SAP TM zone-based routing configuration.

Free-text description providing additional context about the delivery zone, including notable geographic features, operational constraints, special handling instructions, or historical notes. Used by operations and network planning teams.

Code of the zone group or zone cluster to which this delivery zone belongs. Zone groups aggregate individual zones for pricing rate card application, carrier contract negotiation, and network planning. Used in SAP TM zone group configuration.
reference: SAP TM Zone Group Configuration; Blue Yonder Network Design

description: Human-readable name of the delivery zone (e.g., 'Greater London Last-Mile Zone', 'Singapore CBD Service Area'). Used in operational dashboards, carrier communications, and customer-facing serviceability checks.

Current lifecycle status of the delivery zone. Active zones are eligible for shipment routing and serviceability validation. Inactive or retired zones are excluded from new shipment assignments. Suspended zones may be temporarily blocked due to operational or regulatory reasons.
reference: SAP TM Zone Configuration; ISO 9001 Quality Management

description: Service tier classification of the zone reflecting delivery density, infrastructure quality, and cost profile (e.g., tier_1 = major urban centre, tier_4 = remote/rural). Used in pricing rate card assignment, SLA differentiation, and network optimization in Blue Yonder.
reference: Blue Yonder Network Design; SAP TM Zone Configuration

description: Classification of the zone by its definition methodology: postal (based on postal code ranges), administrative (country/state/city boundaries), custom (business-defined polygon), last_mile (final delivery leg zone), service_area (carrier or hub service coverage area). Drives routing logic and surcharge applicability in SAP TM.
reference: SAP TM Zone Configuration; IATA Ground Handling Manual

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the service corridor record. Primary key for the service_corridor data product within the route domain.

Foreign key linking to route.network_node. Business justification: service_corridor defines a named multi-leg corridor connecting network nodes. The origin_hub_code STRING field is a denormalized reference to the origin network node. Adding origin_network_node_id as

Foreign key linking to customs.trade_agreement. Business justification: Service corridor planning incorporates trade agreement zones for network optimization. Corridors designed to leverage FTA benefits (e.g., USMCA-compliant routing through Mexico) require explicit agree

Three-digit IATA Air Waybill (AWB) prefix assigned to the primary carrier operating this air corridor. Applicable only for AIR transport mode corridors. Used for MAWB (Master Air Waybill) and HAWB (House Air Waybill) number generation in SAP TM.

Weekly or per-departure capacity of this corridor expressed in Twenty-foot Equivalent Units (TEU) for ocean corridors, or equivalent volumetric capacity for other modes. Used in Blue Yonder capacity planning and SAP TM load optimization.
reference: IMO; FMC; Blue Yonder Supply Chain Planning; SAP TM

description: Comma-separated list of Standard Carrier Alpha Codes (SCAC) or IATA carrier codes for carriers allocated to operate on this corridor. Used in SAP TM carrier selection and Oracle TMS freight audit. Supports multi-carrier corridor configurations.
reference: NMFTA SCAC; IATA; SAP TM; Oracle TMS

description: Carbon Dioxide Equivalent (CO2e) emission factor in kg CO2e per tonne-kilometre for this corridor, based on transport mode and carrier mix. Used for GHG (Greenhouse Gas) emissions reporting, EU ETS compliance, CORSIA carbon offsetting calculations, and sustainability KPI dashboards.
reference: EU Emissions Trading System (EU ETS); CORSIA; IATA; IMO; GHG Protocol

description: Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the service corridor, used in SAP TM route planning and Blue Yonder network design (e.g., 'APAC-AIR-EXP-01', 'TPAC-OCN-FCL-02'). Referenced in EDI messages and carrier lane allocations.
reference: SAP TM Transportation Management; IATA Resolution 600a

description: Human-readable name of the service corridor describing the route path and service product (e.g., 'Express Air Asia-Pacific', 'Ocean FCL Trans-Pacific', 'Road FTL Europe Central'). Used in operational dashboards, carrier communications, and Blue Yonder network design outputs.
reference: SAP TM Transportation Management; Blue Yonder Supply Chain Planning

description: Current operational lifecycle status of the service corridor. ACTIVE corridors are available for shipment routing in SAP TM. SUSPENDED corridors are temporarily unavailable (e.g., due to port congestion, regulatory hold). PLANNED corridors are in network design phase in Blue Yonder. DISCONTINUED corridors are archived.
reference: SAP TM Transportation Management; Blue Yonder Supply Chain Planning

description: Timestamp when this service corridor record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Audit trail field for data governance, SOX compliance, and lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.

Indicates whether customs clearance is required for shipments transiting this corridor. TRUE for cross-border corridors requiring Descartes Customs processing, ISF filing, and AMS submission. FALSE for domestic or intra-customs-union corridors (e.g., intra-EU).
reference: WCO; Descartes Customs; C-TPAT; AEO Program; US CBP AMS/ISF

description: Indicates whether dangerous goods (DG) shipments are permitted on this corridor. Governed by IMDG Code for ocean, ICAO Technical Instructions for air, and ADR for road. Drives carrier selection and compliance checks in SAP TM and Descartes Customs.
reference: IMDG Code; ICAO Technical Instructions for Dangerous Goods; ADR; IATA DGR

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the corridor's destination country (e.g., 'GBR', 'AUS', 'BRA'). Used for customs compliance in Descartes, trade lane reporting, and cross-border regulatory requirements under WCO and WTO frameworks.

IATA airport code or UN/LOCODE of the primary destination hub node for this corridor (e.g., 'LHR' for London Heathrow, 'USLAX' for Los Angeles). Anchors the corridor's terminal network node in SAP TM route planning and FourKites real-time visibility.

Named geographic region where the service corridor terminates (e.g., 'Europe', 'Middle East', 'Latin America'). Used for network topology grouping, lane allocation, and Blue Yonder network design. Distinct from specific destination node.
reference: Blue Yonder Supply Chain Planning; SAP TM

description: Indicates whether the corridor is defined for outbound (origin to destination), inbound (destination to origin), or bilateral (both directions) traffic flow. Drives lane allocation and backhaul optimization in SAP TM and Oracle TMS.
reference: SAP TM Transportation Management; Oracle TMS

description: Total great-circle or road/rail network distance in kilometres for the full corridor from origin hub to destination hub including all intermediate legs. Used for CO2e emissions calculation, freight rate benchmarking, and Blue Yonder network optimization.
reference: IATA; IMO; Blue Yonder Supply Chain Planning; GHG Protocol

description: Indicates whether Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) messaging is enabled for carrier communications on this corridor (e.g., EDI 214 shipment status, EDI 210 freight invoice). Drives automated data exchange configuration in SAP TM and Oracle TMS freight audit.
reference: ANSI X12 EDI; EDIFACT; SAP TM; Oracle TMS

description: Date from which this service corridor becomes operationally active and available for shipment routing in SAP TM. Used for corridor lifecycle management, seasonal schedule changes, and network design transitions.
reference: SAP TM Transportation Management; Blue Yonder Supply Chain Planning

description: Date on which this service corridor ceases to be operationally active. NULL indicates an open-ended corridor with no planned end date. Used for corridor lifecycle management and seasonal schedule deactivation in SAP TM.
reference: SAP TM Transportation Management; Blue Yonder Supply Chain Planning

description: Specific freight product type carried on this corridor. FCL (Full Container Load), LCL (Less than Container Load), FTL (Full Truckload), LTL (Less than Truckload), AIR_FREIGHT, PARCEL, BULK, or BREAKBULK. Governs load optimization rules in Oracle TMS and capacity planning in Blue Yonder. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FCL|LCL|FTL|LTL|AIR_FREIGHT|PARCEL|BULK|BREAKBULK — promote to reference product]

Comma-separated list of days on which the corridor operates (e.g., 'MON,WED,FRI' for tri-weekly ocean service, 'MON,TUE,WED,THU,FRI,SAT,SUN' for daily express). Used in SAP TM departure scheduling and customer-facing ETD commitments.
reference: SAP TM Transportation Management; Oracle TMS

description: Operational frequency at which the service corridor runs (e.g., DAILY for express parcel, WEEKLY for ocean FCL). Drives departure schedule planning in SAP TM, capacity allocation in Blue Yonder, and ETD/ETA commitments communicated to customers.
reference: SAP TM Transportation Management; Blue Yonder Supply Chain Planning

description: Classification of this corridor's role within the hub-and-spoke network topology. HUB_TO_HUB for main trunk routes, HUB_TO_SPOKE for distribution legs, SPOKE_TO_HUB for collection legs, SPOKE_TO_SPOKE for direct point-to-point routes. Used in Blue Yonder network design and SAP TM route planning.
reference: Blue Yonder Supply Chain Planning; SAP TM Transportation Management

description: Comma-separated list of Incoterms (International Commercial Terms) applicable to shipments on this corridor (e.g., 'FOB,CIF,DDP,DAP,EXW'). Governs risk transfer and cost responsibility rules for cross-border shipments. Aligned with ICC Incoterms 2020 and WTO trade facilitation requirements.

Ordered, pipe-delimited sequence of intermediate hub codes (IATA or UN/LOCODE) traversed between origin and destination (e.g., 'DXB|FRA' for Dubai then Frankfurt). Defines the multi-node path that distinguishes a corridor from a single O-D lane. Consumed by SAP TM for multi-leg route planning.
reference: SAP TM Transportation Management; IATA; UN/LOCODE

description: Indicates whether last-mile delivery to the final consignee is included within this corridor's scope. TRUE for D2D (Door-to-Door) corridors. FALSE for port-to-port or hub-to-hub corridors where last-mile is handled separately.
reference: SAP TM Transportation Management; Oracle TMS

description: Total number of transport legs in this service corridor, including origin-to-first-hub, all intermediate legs, and final leg to destination. Derived from the hub sequence but stored for query performance in SAP TM load optimization and Blue Yonder network design.
reference: SAP TM Transportation Management; Blue Yonder Supply Chain Planning

description: Maximum volumetric capacity in Cubic Meters (CBM) per shipment or per departure on this corridor. Used alongside DIM Weight (Dimensional Weight) calculations for air freight rating and load optimization in Oracle TMS and SAP TM charge calculation.

Maximum gross weight in kilograms permitted per shipment on this corridor, based on carrier capacity constraints, regulatory limits, and infrastructure limitations (e.g., bridge weight limits for road, aircraft payload for air). Used in load optimization in Oracle TMS.
reference: IATA; IMO; US DOT; SAP TM; Oracle TMS

description: Reference to the Blue Yonder network design scenario that generated or last optimized this corridor configuration. Enables traceability between network optimization outputs and operational corridor definitions.

ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the corridor's origin country (e.g., 'CHN', 'USA', 'DEU'). Used for customs compliance in Descartes, trade lane reporting, and cross-border regulatory requirements under WCO and WTO frameworks.

Named geographic region where the service corridor originates (e.g., 'Asia-Pacific', 'North America', 'Europe'). Used for network topology grouping, lane allocation, and Blue Yonder network design. Distinct from specific origin node — this is the macro-geographic classification.
reference: Blue Yonder Supply Chain Planning; SAP TM

description: SCAC or IATA code of the primary/preferred carrier for this corridor. Used as the default carrier in SAP TM carrier selection logic. Distinct from carrier_scac_codes which lists all allocated carriers.
reference: NMFTA SCAC; IATA; SAP TM Transportation Management

description: Numeric priority ranking of this corridor relative to alternative corridors for the same O-D pair. Lower values indicate higher priority in SAP TM carrier selection and load optimization. Used when multiple corridors serve the same origin-destination combination.
reference: SAP TM Transportation Management; Oracle TMS

description: Internal SLA (Service Level Agreement) code that maps this corridor to a defined service level tier (e.g., 'P1-NEXT-DAY', 'P2-2DAY', 'STD-5DAY'). Links to SLA entitlement definitions in Salesforce CRM and drives OTD/OTIF KPI measurement.
reference: SAP TM; Salesforce CRM; ISO 9001 Quality Management

description: Classification of the service product offered on this corridor (e.g., EXPRESS for time-definite delivery, STANDARD for deferred, ECONOMY for cost-optimized). Determines SLA commitments, pricing tiers, and priority handling rules in SAP TM and Oracle TMS.
reference: SAP TM Transportation Management; Oracle TMS

description: Standard total transit time in calendar days from origin pickup to destination delivery for this corridor under normal operating conditions. Used as the baseline for SLA commitments, ETA calculation in FourKites, and OTD (On-Time Delivery) KPI measurement.
reference: SAP TM Transportation Management; FourKites; IATA Resolution 600a

description: Standard total transit time expressed in hours for sub-day precision, particularly relevant for express air corridors where transit is measured in hours rather than days (e.g., 18 hours for an overnight express air corridor). Complements standard_transit_days for precise ETA calculation.
reference: SAP TM Transportation Management; FourKites; IATA

description: Expected or actual date on which a suspended corridor resumes operations. NULL if suspension end date is unknown. Used for proactive customer communication in Salesforce CRM and re-routing planning in SAP TM.
reference: FourKites Real-Time Visibility; SAP TM; Salesforce CRM

description: Reason code for corridor suspension. Used for exception reporting, root cause analysis, and regulatory compliance documentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PORT_CONGESTION|REGULATORY_EMBARGO|FORCE_MAJEURE|CARRIER_CAPACITY|INFRASTRUCTURE|SEASONAL|OTHER — promote to reference product]

Date from which the corridor is temporarily suspended (e.g., due to port congestion, regulatory embargo, force majeure). Populated only when corridor_status is SUSPENDED. Used for exception management in FourKites and customer notification in Salesforce CRM.
reference: FourKites Real-Time Visibility; SAP TM; ISO 28000

description: Indicates whether this corridor supports temperature-controlled (cold chain) shipments. Relevant for pharmaceutical, perishable food, and chemical cargo. Drives equipment type selection (reefer containers, temperature-controlled vehicles) in SAP TM and Oracle TMS.
reference: ISO 28000; SAP TM Transportation Management; Oracle TMS

business_glossary_term: FourKites Real-Time Tracking Visibility Enabled Flag
description: Indicates whether real-time shipment tracking and predictive ETA via FourKites is enabled for this corridor. TRUE enables GPS/IoT-based visibility, exception alerts, and predictive ETA updates for shipments on this corridor.

Primary mode of transport for this service corridor. Drives carrier selection, regulatory compliance requirements, and charge calculation in SAP TM. Aligns with IATA (air), IMO (ocean), and DOT (road/rail) regulatory frameworks.

Timestamp of the most recent update to this service corridor record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline and audit trail compliance.

business_glossary_term: Estimated Time of Arrival (ETA) Event ID
description: Unique identifier for the ETA/ETD event record. Primary key for the eta_event product.

Reference to the carrier or Third-Party Logistics (3PL) provider responsible for transporting the shipment on this leg. Links to the carrier master data. Used for carrier performance analysis and Service Level Agreement (SLA) compliance tracking.
Reference to the shipment for which this ETA/ETD event was generated. Links to the shipment master record.

Reference to the destination location (hub, terminal, warehouse, customer site) for this shipment leg. Links to the location master data. Used for Door-to-Door (D2D) transit time calculation and last-mile delivery zone assignment.

Reference to the driver or operator responsible for transporting the shipment on this leg. Links to the workforce master data. Used for driver performance tracking and compliance with Hours of Service (HOS) regulations.

Foreign key linking to customs.customs_hold. Business justification: ETA predictions must account for customs holds to maintain accuracy. Required for predictive ETA adjustment when holds occur, customer notification of customs-related delays, and SLA impact analysis.
Reference to the route plan that governs this shipment leg. Links to the planned route configuration from SAP TM or Blue Yonder network design.

Foreign key linking to route.plan_leg. Business justification: eta_event is a real-time and predictive ETA/ETD event record for a shipment leg. It already links to plan (plan_id) at the plan level, but ETA events are inherently leg-specific — each event tracks th

Reference to the network node (hub, terminal, warehouse, Container Freight Station (CFS), Inland Container Depot (ICD), or delivery zone) where the shipment was located at the time of this event. Links to the network topology master data.
Reference to the specific shipment leg or route segment for which this ETA/ETD event applies. A shipment may have multiple legs across different transport modes.

Reference to the specific vehicle (truck, trailer, aircraft, vessel) transporting the shipment on this leg. Links to the fleet master data. Used for fleet utilization analysis and Global Positioning System (GPS) tracking integration.

The actual recorded time of departure when the shipment left the origin location, hub, or intermediate node. Captured from GPS, carrier Electronic Data Interchange (EDI), or manual confirmation. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

The actual transit time in hours for this shipment leg, calculated from actual Estimated Time of Departure (ETD) to actual Estimated Time of Arrival (ETA). Populated once the leg is completed. Used for Key Performance Indicator (KPI) reporting and continuous improvement.

The Air Waybill (AWB) number for air freight shipments. This is the Master Air Waybill (MAWB) or House Air Waybill (HAWB) number issued by the carrier or freight forwarder. Used for tracking and customs clearance.

The Bill of Lading (BOL) number for ocean or ground freight shipments. This is the Master Bill of Lading (MBL) or House Bill of Lading (HBL) number issued by the carrier or Non-Vessel Operating Common Carrier (NVOCC). Used for tracking and proof of shipment.

The unique container number for Full Container Load (FCL) or Less Than Container Load (LCL) ocean freight shipments. Follows International Organization for Standardization (ISO) 6346 standard for container identification. Used for tracking and terminal handling.
The date and time when this ETA/ETD event record was first created in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.

business_glossary_term: Current Predicted Estimated Time of Arrival (ETA)
description: The most recent predicted estimated time of arrival based on real-time tracking data, traffic conditions, weather, and predictive algorithms from FourKites or SAP TM. This is the current best estimate of when the shipment will arrive. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

Boolean indicator (True/False) that identifies whether a customer notification (email, SMS, Application Programming Interface (API) push) was sent for this ETA/ETD event. True indicates the customer was notified; False indicates no notification was sent. Used for customer communication audit and Service Level Agreement (SLA) compliance.
The date and time when the customer notification was sent for this ETA/ETD event. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Used for audit trail and Service Level Agreement (SLA) compliance verification.

The current customs clearance status for cross-border shipments. Indicates whether the shipment is pending clearance, cleared, on hold, requires inspection, or has been released by customs authorities. Captured from Descartes Customs or Automated Manifest System (AMS) / Importer Security Filing (ISF) integration.

A standardized code indicating the root cause of any delay or exception. Examples include weather, traffic, customs hold, mechanical failure, carrier capacity issue, or documentation error. Used for root cause analysis and carrier performance scorecarding.

A free-text description providing additional context or details about the delay or exception. Captured from carrier Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) messages, driver notes, or customer service case logs.

business_glossary_term: Estimated Time of Arrival (ETA) Variance Hours
description: The difference in hours between the original planned ETA and the current predicted ETA. Positive values indicate a delay; negative values indicate the shipment is ahead of schedule. Used for Service Level Agreement (SLA) monitoring and On-Time Delivery (OTD) / On-Time In-Full (OTIF) Key Performance Indicator (KPI) calculation.

business_glossary_term: Estimated Time of Departure (ETD) Variance Hours
description: The difference in hours between the original planned ETD and the actual ETD. Positive values indicate a late departure; negative values indicate an early departure. Used for root cause analysis of delays and transit time performance.

The system or channel that generated this ETA/ETD event. Indicates whether the event originated from FourKites real-time visibility platform, SAP TM route planning, carrier Electronic Data Interchange (EDI), GPS tracking device, manual operator entry, Oracle TMS, or Blue Yonder supply chain planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FourKites|SAP TM|Carrier EDI|GPS|Manual|Oracle TMS|Blue Yonder — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
The date and time when this ETA/ETD event was generated or recorded. Represents the moment the prediction or update was made. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

value_regex: ETA Update|ETD Update|ETA Prediction|ETD Actual|Exception Alert|On-Time Confirmation
description: The type of ETA/ETD event being recorded. Distinguishes between estimated time of arrival updates, estimated time of departure updates, predictive ETA calculations, actual ETD confirmations, exception alerts, and on-time confirmations.

Boolean indicator (True/False) that identifies whether this ETA/ETD event represents an exception condition requiring attention. True indicates a delay, early arrival, or other deviation from the Service Level Agreement (SLA) or planned schedule. Used to trigger exception management workflows.

The severity level of the exception condition. Critical indicates Service Level Agreement (SLA) breach or customer escalation required; High indicates significant delay; Medium indicates minor delay; Low indicates minimal impact; None indicates no exception.

The specific type of exception or status condition associated with this ETA/ETD event. Categorizes the nature of the deviation (delay, early arrival, on-time, weather delay, traffic delay, customs delay, mechanical failure, route deviation, carrier issue). Used for root cause analysis and Key Performance Indicator (KPI) reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Delay|Early|On-Time|Weather Delay|Traffic Delay|Customs Delay|Mechanical Failure|Route Deviation|Carrier Issue — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

The geographic latitude coordinate of the shipment or vehicle at the time this ETA/ETD event was generated. Captured from Global Positioning System (GPS) or Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) tracking devices. Used for real-time visibility and geofencing.

The geographic longitude coordinate of the shipment or vehicle at the time this ETA/ETD event was generated. Captured from Global Positioning System (GPS) or Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) tracking devices. Used for real-time visibility and geofencing.

business_glossary_term: Original Planned Estimated Time of Arrival (ETA)
description: The initial planned estimated time of arrival as defined in the original route plan or shipment booking. This is the baseline ETA against which all subsequent updates and variances are measured. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

business_glossary_term: Original Planned Estimated Time of Departure (ETD)
description: The initial planned estimated time of departure as defined in the original route plan or shipment booking. This is the baseline ETD for the origin or intermediate hub. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
The planned transit time in hours for this shipment leg as defined in the route plan or service corridor standard. Used as the baseline for On-Time Delivery (OTD) and transit time variance analysis.

The Proof of Delivery (POD) status for this shipment leg. Indicates whether the shipment has not been delivered, has been delivered, delivery was attempted, was refused by the consignee, was damaged, or was lost. Used for Electronic Proof of Delivery (ePOD) integration and claims processing.

The date and time when Proof of Delivery (POD) was captured. This is the actual delivery timestamp recorded by the driver or Electronic Proof of Delivery (ePOD) system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Used for On-Time Delivery (OTD) calculation and billing.

A confidence score (0-100) indicating the reliability of the current predicted ETA. Higher scores indicate greater confidence based on historical accuracy, data quality, and real-time tracking signal strength. Generated by FourKites or SAP TM predictive algorithms.

The service level or product type for this shipment. Indicates express parcel delivery, standard service, economy, same-day, next-day, freight forwarding, Less Than Truckload (LTL), Full Truckload (FTL), Full Container Load (FCL), or Less Than Container Load (LCL). Used for Service Level Agreement (SLA) determination and On-Time Delivery (OTD) measurement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Express|Standard|Economy|Same-Day|Next-Day|Freight|LTL|FTL|FCL|LCL — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Boolean indicator (True/False) that identifies whether this ETA/ETD event represents a Service Level Agreement (SLA) breach. True indicates the shipment will miss or has missed the committed delivery time; False indicates on-time or early. Used for Key Performance Indicator (KPI) dashboards and customer credits processing.

business_glossary_term: Service Level Agreement (SLA) Target Delivery Timestamp
description: The committed delivery date and time as defined in the Service Level Agreement (SLA) or customer contract. This is the contractual deadline against which On-Time Delivery (OTD) and On-Time In-Full (OTIF) performance is measured. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

A description of the traffic conditions at the time of the ETA/ETD event or along the route. Captured from traffic Application Programming Interface (API) integration, Global Positioning System (GPS) data, or driver reports. Used for delay analysis and dynamic route optimization.

The mode of transportation for this shipment leg. Indicates whether the leg is transported by air, ocean, road, rail, or multimodal combination. Aligns with International Air Transport Association (IATA) and International Maritime Organization (IMO) standards.
The date and time when this ETA/ETD event record was last updated in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Used for audit trail and change tracking.

A description of the weather conditions at the time of the ETA/ETD event or along the route. Examples include clear, rain, snow, fog, storm, hurricane. Captured from weather Application Programming Interface (API) integration or driver reports. Used for delay analysis and predictive modeling.
Unique surrogate identifier for each lane-period performance measurement record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this operational measurement entity.
Reference to the carrier (transport service provider) whose performance is being measured on this lane for the defined period. Supports carrier scorecard and selection analytics.
reference: SAP TM Carrier Selection; Oracle TMS Freight Audit

description: Foreign key linking to network.carrier_service. Business justification: Performance metrics must be measured against specific service-level commitments (express vs. standard SLAs differ). Required for service-level SLA compliance reporting, carrier scorecard generation, a
Reference to the origin-destination lane master record that this performance measurement covers. Links to the route domain lane master.
reference: SAP TM Route Planning; Oracle TMS Shipment Execution

description: Foreign key linking to pricing.rate_card. Business justification: Lane performance metrics inform rate card adjustments. Rate_card_id links performance to pricing decisions, enabling performance-based rate optimization, rate card effectiveness analysis, and dynamic

Foreign key linking to route.transit_time_standard. Business justification: lane_performance captures actual vs planned transit performance for a lane over a measurement period. Linking lane_performance to transit_time_standard via transit_time_standard_id enables direct comp

The measured average actual transit time in hours for shipments on this lane during the measurement period. Sourced from FourKites actuals and Oracle TMS freight audit data. Compared against planned_transit_hours to derive delay.
reference: FourKites Real-Time Visibility; Oracle TMS Freight Audit

description: Average number of hours by which shipments on this lane exceeded the planned transit time during the measurement period. Positive values indicate late delivery; negative values indicate early delivery. Key input for SLA breach analysis.
reference: FourKites Exception Management; Oracle TMS Freight Audit; ISO 9001

description: Average dimensional (volumetric) weight in kilograms per shipment on this lane during the measurement period. DIM weight is used by carriers to price low-density shipments. Sourced from Oracle TMS freight audit.
reference: IATA DIM Weight Standards; Oracle TMS Freight Audit

description: Average freight cost per shipment on this lane during the measurement period, in the reporting currency. Sourced from Oracle TMS freight audit. Used for cost-per-lane benchmarking and network optimization analysis.
reference: Oracle TMS Freight Audit; SAP S/4HANA Finance; IFRS

description: Average actual gross weight in kilograms per shipment on this lane during the measurement period. Used for load optimization, carrier rate benchmarking, and CO2e intensity calculations.
reference: IATA Weight Standards; IMO; SAP TM Load Optimization

description: Proportion of carrier invoices for shipments on this lane that matched the contracted rate without dispute during the measurement period. Sourced from Oracle TMS freight audit. Supports carrier financial performance evaluation.
reference: Oracle TMS Freight Audit; SAP S/4HANA Finance; IFRS

description: Composite carrier performance score for this lane-period, typically on a 0–100 scale, derived from OTD rate, OTIF rate, exception rate, and transit time adherence. Primary input for carrier scorecard and SAP TM carrier selection decisions.
reference: SAP TM Carrier Selection; Oracle TMS Freight Audit; ISO 9001

description: Average CO2e emissions in kilograms per shipment on this lane during the measurement period. Calculated from fuel consumption, transport mode, and distance. Supports EU ETS, CORSIA, and GHG sustainability reporting.
reference: EU Emissions Trading System (EU ETS); CORSIA; IMO GHG Strategy; ICAO

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the avg_freight_cost and total_freight_cost monetary fields on this record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).

Average hours of delay attributable to customs clearance processes for shipments on this lane during the measurement period. Sourced from Descartes Customs. Used for cross-border lane performance root cause analysis.
reference: Descartes Customs Global Trade Management; WCO; C-TPAT; AEO

description: Proportion of shipments on this lane that incurred cargo damage during the measurement period, expressed as a decimal. Used for carrier liability assessment, insurance claims analysis, and lane risk scoring.
reference: IMO IMDG Code; IATA Dangerous Goods Regulations; Oracle TMS Freight Audit

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the lane destination. Used for cross-border compliance, customs reporting, and geographic performance segmentation.
Standardized code for the destination hub, port, airport, or facility of the lane (e.g., IATA airport code, UN/LOCODE). Identifies the arrival point for transit time and OTD measurement.
reference: IATA Airport Codes; UN/LOCODE; IMO Port Codes

description: Total number of shipment exceptions (delays, damages, misroutes, customs holds, etc.) recorded on this lane during the measurement period. Sourced from FourKites exception management. Triggers network optimization review.
reference: FourKites Exception Management; Oracle TMS Freight Audit

description: Proportion of shipments on this lane that incurred at least one exception during the measurement period, expressed as a decimal (exception_count / shipment_count). Used for lane risk scoring and network optimization triggers.
reference: FourKites Exception Management; Oracle TMS Freight Audit

description: Identifies the primary operational system of record from which actuals for this lane-period measurement were sourced (FourKites, Oracle TMS, SAP TM, or manual entry). Supports data lineage and audit traceability.
reference: FourKites Real-Time Visibility; Oracle TMS Freight Audit; SAP TM

description: Proportion of shipments on this lane that had active FourKites real-time GPS/IoT tracking during the measurement period. Low coverage rates may indicate data quality issues in actuals-based metrics.

Average hours of delay attributable specifically to the last-mile delivery segment for shipments on this lane during the measurement period. Enables root cause decomposition of total delay into linehaul vs. last-mile components.
reference: FourKites Exception Management; Oracle TMS Shipment Execution

description: The last calendar date of the performance measurement window for this lane-period record. Combined with measurement_period_start_date defines the exact reporting interval.
reference: Oracle TMS Freight Audit; FourKites Real-Time Visibility

description: The first calendar date of the performance measurement window for this lane-period record. Combined with measurement_period_end_date defines the exact reporting interval (e.g., weekly, monthly).
reference: Oracle TMS Freight Audit; FourKites Real-Time Visibility

description: Lifecycle status of this lane-period performance measurement record. Draft = initial load; Confirmed = validated by freight audit; Published = available for scorecard and SLA reporting; Superseded = replaced by a corrected record.
reference: Oracle TMS Freight Audit; ISO 9001 Quality Management

description: Indicates whether this lane-period measurement has triggered a network optimization review in Blue Yonder based on SLA breach, high exception rate, or cost threshold exceedance. True = optimization review triggered.
reference: Blue Yonder Supply Chain Planning; SAP TM Route Planning

description: Number of shipments on this lane that were delivered on or before the planned delivery date/time during the measurement period. Numerator for OTD rate calculation.
reference: FourKites Real-Time Visibility; Oracle TMS Freight Audit

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the lane origin. Used for cross-border compliance, customs reporting, and geographic performance segmentation.
Standardized code for the origin hub, port, airport, or facility of the lane (e.g., IATA airport code, UN/LOCODE). Identifies the departure point for transit time and OTD measurement.
reference: IATA Airport Codes; UN/LOCODE; IMO Port Codes

description: Proportion of shipments on this lane delivered on or before the planned delivery date/time during the measurement period, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.9500 = 95%). Core KPI for SLA monitoring and carrier scorecard.
reference: FourKites Real-Time Visibility; Oracle TMS Freight Audit; ISO 9001

description: Proportion of shipments on this lane delivered both on time and in full (no quantity shortfall) during the measurement period, expressed as a decimal. Composite SLA metric used for customer contract compliance and carrier evaluation.
reference: FourKites Real-Time Visibility; Oracle TMS Freight Audit; ISO 9001

description: Granularity of the measurement window for this lane-period record. Determines the aggregation cadence used for SLA monitoring and carrier scorecard reporting.
reference: Oracle TMS Freight Audit; SAP TM Charge Calculation

description: The contracted or standard planned transit time in hours for this lane as defined in the SLA or service corridor standard. Sourced from SAP TM route planning master data. Baseline for OTD and delay calculations.
reference: SAP TM Route Planning; ISO 9001 Quality Management

description: Proportion of shipments on this lane for which an electronic Proof of Delivery (ePOD) was captured and confirmed during the measurement period. Supports compliance with customer SLA documentation requirements.
reference: FourKites Real-Time Visibility; Oracle TMS Freight Audit; ISO 9001

business_glossary_term: Predictive Estimated Time of Arrival (ETA) Accuracy Rate
description: Proportion of shipments on this lane for which FourKites' predictive ETA was within the agreed tolerance window of the actual delivery time during the measurement period. Measures the reliability of the real-time visibility platform's ETA predictions.
reference: FourKites Real-Time Visibility; ETA Management Standards

description: Timestamp when this lane-period performance measurement record was first created in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and incremental processing.
Timestamp when this lane-period performance measurement record was last modified in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Used for change tracking, SLA audit, and incremental ETL processing.
Total number of shipments executed on this lane during the measurement period. Denominator for OTD, OTIF, and exception rate calculations. Sourced from Oracle TMS shipment execution records.
reference: Oracle TMS Shipment Execution; SAP TM Freight Order Management

description: Indicates whether the lane's OTD rate fell below the contracted SLA threshold (sla_threshold_otd) during the measurement period. True = SLA breached; False = SLA met. Triggers escalation and network optimization review workflows.
reference: SAP TM; Oracle TMS Freight Audit; ISO 9001

business_glossary_term: Service Level Agreement (SLA) On-Time Delivery (OTD) Threshold
