Quantity approved by the claims assessor following survey and investigation. May differ from claimed_quantity where partial loss or damage is confirmed. Used in settlement calculation.
reference: IATA Cargo Claims Handling Manual; ISO 9001

description: Monetary value approved for settlement on this line item following assessment, survey, and liability determination. May be less than claimed_value due to liability caps, depreciation, or partial loss findings.
reference: IATA Warsaw-Montreal Convention; CMR Convention; IFRS 13

description: Date on which this claim line was formally filed with the carrier or insurer. Used to assess compliance with statutory filing deadlines under the Montreal Convention (14 days for damage, 21 days for delay) and CMR Convention.
reference: IATA Warsaw-Montreal Convention Article 31; CMR Convention Article 30

description: Classification of the nature of the claim line: loss (cargo not delivered), damage (cargo delivered in damaged condition), shortage (partial quantity missing), delay (late delivery causing financial loss), contamination, or pilferage. Drives liability framework selection.
reference: IATA Warsaw-Montreal Convention; CMR Convention; IMO Liability Conventions

description: Number of units, pieces, or cargo items being claimed on this line. Represents the quantity declared as lost, damaged, or short-shipped by the claimant.
reference: IATA Cargo Claims Handling Manual; WCO

description: Total monetary value claimed by the claimant for this line item, calculated as claimed_quantity multiplied by unit_value plus any ancillary costs. Represents the claimant's gross demand before assessment.
reference: IATA Warsaw-Montreal Convention; CMR Convention; IFRS 13

description: Recorded condition of the cargo item upon delivery at destination, as noted on the Proof of Delivery (POD) or cargo survey report. Compared against condition_at_origin to establish loss or damage.
reference: IATA Cargo Claims Handling Manual; CMR Convention; ePOD Standards

description: Recorded condition of the cargo item at the point of origin or acceptance by the carrier, as noted on the AWB, BOL, or cargo receipt. Establishes the baseline for damage assessment and liability determination.
reference: IATA Cargo Acceptance Standards; CMR Convention; WCO

description: Timestamp when this claim line record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, SLA measurement, and data lineage tracking in the Silver Layer lakehouse.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary values on this claim line (claimed_value, approved_value, unit_value). Ensures consistent multi-currency claim valuation and financial reporting.

Categorical classification of the damage type for this claim line, enabling trend analysis and root cause reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: physical|water|fire|theft|contamination|temperature|handling|vermin|customs_examination — promote to reference product]
reference: IATA Cargo Claims Handling Manual; IMDG Code; ICAO Technical Instructions

description: Detailed free-text description of the nature and extent of damage, loss, or shortage observed for this claim line item. Captured from cargo survey reports and used in insurer correspondence and settlement negotiations.
reference: IATA Cargo Claims Handling Manual; ISO 9001

description: IMDG/ICAO dangerous goods classification class or division for the cargo item (e.g., Class 3 Flammable Liquids, Division 6.1 Toxic Substances). Populated only when is_dangerous_goods is true. Required for regulatory incident reporting.
reference: IMDG Code; ICAO Technical Instructions for Dangerous Goods; IATA DGR

description: Depreciation rate applied to the cargo item's declared value to reflect age, wear, or market value reduction at the time of loss or damage. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.1500 = 15%). Applied in approved_value calculation.
reference: IATA Cargo Claims Handling Manual; IFRS 13

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the cargo destination for this claim line. Used for cross-border claims, import duty recovery, and jurisdictional liability framework determination.

Gross weight in kilograms of the cargo items on this claim line as declared on the AWB or BOL. Used for liability cap calculation under weight-based conventions (e.g., Montreal Convention SDR per kg).
reference: IATA Warsaw-Montreal Convention Article 22; CMR Convention Article 23; IATA AWB Standards

description: Harmonized System (HS) Code classifying the commodity under the World Customs Organization (WCO) nomenclature. Required for customs and trade compliance reporting, particularly for cross-border claims and duty recovery under DDP or DAP Incoterms.
reference: WCO Harmonized System; Descartes Customs GTM

description: International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) applicable to the shipment for this claim line, determining the point of risk transfer between buyer and seller. Influences liability determination and insurable interest. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Reference number assigned by the cargo insurer for this claim line. Used for insurer correspondence, subrogation tracking, and recovery management.
reference: IATA Cargo Claims Handling Manual; ISO 28000

description: Flag indicating whether the cargo item on this claim line is classified as dangerous goods under IMDG Code or ICAO Technical Instructions. Triggers specialist handling procedures and regulatory notification requirements for dangerous goods claims.
reference: IMDG Code; ICAO Technical Instructions for Dangerous Goods; IATA DGR

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this claim line record. Supports change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer and audit compliance under SOX.

The legal or contractual framework governing carrier liability for this claim line. Determines the applicable liability cap and burden of proof. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: montreal_convention|cmr_convention|hague_visby|cotif|contractual|waiver — promote to reference product if additional conventions are added]
reference: IATA Warsaw-Montreal Convention; CMR Convention; Hague-Visby Rules; COTIF; IMO

description: Maximum carrier liability applicable to this claim line under the governing convention or contract (e.g., SDR per kg under Montreal Convention for air, or CMR limits for road). Constrains the approved_value ceiling.
reference: IATA Warsaw-Montreal Convention Article 22; CMR Convention Article 23; IMO

description: Sequential line number ordering this item within the parent claim. Used for display, sorting, and referencing individual line items in insurer correspondence and settlement documentation.
Current lifecycle status of this individual claim line item. Tracks the workflow state from initial registration through assessment, approval or rejection, and final settlement or withdrawal.
reference: IATA Cargo Claims Handling Manual; ISO 9001 Quality Management

description: Date on which the loss, damage, or shortage for this claim line was first discovered or reported by the consignee or shipper. Critical for determining claim filing timeliness under applicable liability conventions.
reference: IATA Warsaw-Montreal Convention Article 31; CMR Convention Article 30; Hague-Visby Rules

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the cargo origin for this claim line. Used for cross-border claims, customs duty recovery, and WCO trade compliance reporting.

Unit of measure for the claimed quantity (e.g., PCS for pieces, KG for kilograms, CBM for cubic metres, CTN for cartons, PLT for pallets, BAG for bags). Aligns with the unit declared on the AWB or BOL.

Free-text or coded reason for rejection of this claim line, populated when line_status is 'rejected'. Documents the basis for denial (e.g., late filing, inherent vice, force majeure, insufficient evidence) for audit and dispute resolution.
reference: IATA Cargo Claims Handling Manual; CMR Convention; ISO 9001

description: Residual monetary value of the damaged cargo recovered through salvage operations for this claim line. Deducted from the approved_value to determine the net settlement payable.
reference: IATA Cargo Claims Handling Manual; IFRS 13 Fair Value

description: Date on which the claim line was financially settled and payment was made or credit note issued to the claimant. Used for finance reserve accounting and SAP S/4HANA AR reconciliation.
reference: IFRS 9 Financial Instruments; SOX; SAP S/4HANA Finance

description: Indicates whether subrogation rights have been exercised or are being pursued for this claim line, enabling the insurer to recover costs from the liable carrier or third party after settlement.
reference: IATA Cargo Claims Handling Manual; IMO Liability Conventions

description: Monetary amount recovered through subrogation proceedings for this claim line. Populated after recovery is confirmed. Used in finance for net claims cost reporting and P&L impact analysis.
reference: IFRS 9; SOX; IATA Cargo Claims Handling Manual

description: Date on which the cargo survey or physical inspection was conducted for this claim line item. Used to track assessment timelines and SLA compliance for claims processing.
reference: IATA Cargo Claims Handling Manual; ISO 9001

description: Mode of transport applicable to this claim line, determining the governing liability convention (IATA Warsaw-Montreal for air, Hague-Visby/CMR for ocean/road, COTIF for rail). Critical for liability cap calculation.
reference: IATA Warsaw-Montreal Convention; CMR Convention; COTIF Convention; IMO

description: Declared or assessed value per unit of the cargo item at origin, as stated on the commercial invoice. Used as the basis for claim valuation and liability cap comparison under applicable conventions.
reference: IATA Warsaw-Montreal Convention; CMR Convention; IFRS 13 Fair Value

description: Volume in cubic metres (CBM) of the cargo items on this claim line. Used for dimensional weight (DIM Weight) calculations and ocean freight LCL/FCL claim assessments.
reference: IATA DIM Weight Standards; IMO; WCO

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the cargo insurance policy record within the Transport Shipping data platform. Primary key for the claim_policy entity.

Foreign key linking to network.agent. Business justification: Insurance policies may cover agent networks or specific agent relationships (agent E&O insurance, agent liability coverage). Agent coverage under insurance policies for risk management, agent selectio

Foreign key linking to network.carrier. Business justification: Insurance policies often cover specific carrier relationships or carrier-provided insurance programs. Policy-carrier coverage mapping for carrier liability insurance, carrier selection based on insura

Foreign key linking to customer.customer_account. Business justification: Cargo insurance policies are held by specific customer accounts (shippers or consignees). Policy management, premium allocation, coverage verification during claim adjudication, and open-cover declara

Maximum total insurer liability across all claims filed within the policy year, expressed in coverage_limit_currency. Once exhausted, no further claims are payable under this policy until renewal. Critical for reserve accounting in SAP S/4HANA Finance.
reference: IFRS 17 Insurance Contracts; SOX Section 404; Institute Cargo Clauses

description: Indicates whether this policy automatically renews at expiry without requiring explicit action from Transport Shipping. When True, cancellation notice must be given before the renewal_date to prevent automatic continuation.
reference: IFRS 17 Insurance Contracts; Institute Cargo Clauses

description: Name of the insurance broker or intermediary who placed this policy on behalf of Transport Shipping. The broker is the primary point of contact for policy renewals, endorsements, and claim escalations.
reference: IATA Cargo Insurance Guidelines; FMC Regulations

description: Broker's own reference number for this policy placement. Used in broker correspondence, premium invoices, and claim notifications routed through the broker.
Minimum number of calendar days' written notice required by either party to cancel this policy before its expiry_date. Breach of this period may result in premium liability for the full policy term.
reference: Institute Cargo Clauses — Lloyd's Market Association; IFRS 17 Insurance Contracts

description: Classification of cargo types covered under this policy. Determines applicable liability frameworks and special conditions. 'dangerous_goods' aligns to IMDG Code and ICAO Technical Instructions; 'high_value' triggers enhanced survey requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: general|perishable|dangerous_goods|high_value|fragile|bulk|pharmaceutical|automotive — promote to reference product]
reference: IMDG Code (IMO); ICAO Technical Instructions for Dangerous Goods; IATA DGR

description: Maximum number of calendar days after the loss event within which a formal claim must be filed with the insurer. Distinct from the notification period; this is the hard deadline for submitting full claim documentation.
reference: IATA Warsaw-Montreal Convention Article 35; CMR Convention Article 32; IMO Hague-Visby Rules Article III Rule 6

description: Maximum number of calendar days after discovery of loss or damage within which a claim must be notified to the insurer to remain eligible for settlement. Breach of this period may void the claim. Aligned to IATA, CMR, and IMO convention time limits.
reference: IATA Warsaw-Montreal Convention Article 31; CMR Convention Article 30; IMO Hague-Visby Rules Article III Rule 6

description: Indicates whether this policy is co-insured by multiple underwriters sharing the risk proportionally. When True, the co_insurance_lead_insurer and individual underwriter shares must be tracked for claim apportionment.
reference: Lloyd's Market Association Co-Insurance Clauses; IFRS 17 Insurance Contracts

description: Percentage of the total risk retained by the lead insurer named in insurer_name when this is a co-insured policy. Expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 60.00 for 60%). Only applicable when co_insurance_flag is True.
reference: Lloyd's Market Association Co-Insurance Clauses; IFRS 17 Insurance Contracts

description: Maximum monetary amount the insurer will pay for any single claim or in aggregate under this policy, expressed in the policy currency. Serves as the upper bound for settlement calculations. Critical for claim eligibility assessment.
reference: IATA Warsaw-Montreal Convention; CMR Convention Article 23; IFRS 17 Insurance Contracts

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the coverage limit amount and deductible are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency settlement calculations.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS 17 Insurance Contracts

description: Pipe-delimited list of transport modes covered under this policy (e.g., 'air|ocean|road|rail'). Determines claim eligibility based on the mode used for the shipment. Aligned to IATA (air), IMO (ocean), CMR Convention (road), and CIM/COTIF (rail) liability frameworks. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: air|ocean|road|rail|multimodal|courier — promote to reference product]
reference: IATA Warsaw-Montreal Convention; IMO Hague-Visby Rules; CMR Convention; CIM/COTIF Rail Convention

description: Timestamp when this claim policy record was first created in the Transport Shipping data platform. Populated automatically at insert time. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and SOX compliance. Maps to RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED canonical category.
reference: SOX Section 404; GDPR Article 5(2) Accountability; ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems

description: Indicates whether this policy explicitly covers dangerous goods shipments as classified under the IMDG Code (ocean), ICAO Technical Instructions (air), or ADR/RID (road/rail). When True, the policy must reference applicable DG endorsements and special conditions.
reference: IMDG Code (IMO); ICAO Technical Instructions for Dangerous Goods; ADR/RID Regulations

description: Number of calendar days after shipment dispatch within which a declaration must be submitted to the insurer under an open cover policy. Only applicable when declaration_required_flag is True. Null for specific voyage policies.
reference: Institute Cargo Clauses — Lloyd's Market Association

description: Indicates whether individual shipment declarations must be submitted to the insurer under this open cover policy. When True, each shipment must be declared within the agreed declaration period to activate coverage for that consignment.
reference: Institute Cargo Clauses — Lloyd's Market Association; IATA Cargo Insurance Guidelines

description: Fixed monetary amount that Transport Shipping must absorb before the insurer's liability commences on any individual claim. Deducted from gross claim settlement to arrive at net insurer payment. Expressed in coverage_limit_currency.
reference: Institute Cargo Clauses — Lloyd's Market Association; IATA Warsaw-Montreal Convention

description: Method by which the deductible is applied. 'fixed' is a flat monetary amount; 'percentage' is a proportion of the claim value; 'franchise' means the deductible is waived if the claim exceeds the threshold; 'nil' means no deductible applies.
reference: Institute Cargo Clauses — Lloyd's Market Association

description: Agreed mechanism for resolving disputes between Transport Shipping and the insurer regarding claim settlements or policy interpretation. 'arbitration' is most common in international cargo insurance under Lloyd's and London Market policies.
reference: IMO FAL Convention; IATA Resolution 600a; ICC Arbitration Rules

description: Document management system reference or URL pointing to the signed policy wording, schedule, and endorsements stored in the enterprise content management system. Enables auditors and claims handlers to retrieve the authoritative policy text.
reference: SOX Section 404 Document Retention; ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems

description: Calendar date on which the cargo insurance policy becomes binding and coverage commences. Used to determine claim eligibility based on shipment date. Maps to EFFECTIVE_FROM canonical category for MASTER_AGREEMENT role.
reference: Institute Cargo Clauses — Lloyd's Market Association; IFRS 17 Insurance Contracts

description: Structured description of risks, cargo types, or circumstances explicitly excluded from coverage under this policy. Common exclusions include inherent vice, delay, inadequate packing, and nuclear risks. Used during claim eligibility assessment.
reference: Institute Cargo Clauses (A, B, C) — Lloyd's Market Association; IMDG Code (IMO)

description: Calendar date on which the cargo insurance policy coverage terminates. Claims for shipments dispatched after this date are not eligible under this policy. Nullable for open-ended or evergreen open cover policies. Maps to EFFECTIVE_UNTIL canonical category for MASTER_AGREEMENT role.
reference: Institute Cargo Clauses — Lloyd's Market Association; IFRS 17 Insurance Contracts

description: Territorial extent of coverage under this policy. 'worldwide' covers all global shipments; 'regional' covers a defined geographic region; 'domestic' covers in-country shipments only; 'cross_border' covers international shipments excluding domestic legs.
reference: WTO Trade Facilitation Agreement; IMO FAL Convention; WCO Framework of Standards

description: Jurisdiction whose laws govern the interpretation and enforcement of this insurance policy (e.g., 'English Law', 'New York Law', 'German Law'). Determines dispute resolution forum and applicable legal standards for claim settlement.
reference: IMO FAL Convention; WTO Trade Facilitation Agreement; IFRS 17 Insurance Contracts

description: Legal trading name of the insurance company or underwriting syndicate that issued the cargo insurance policy. Used in insurer correspondence, claim submissions, and settlement negotiations.

Insurer's own internal reference or certificate number for this policy, distinct from the policy_number used by Transport Shipping. Required for direct insurer correspondence and electronic claim submission via EDI.
reference: IATA e-freight EDI Standards; IMO FAL Convention

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the jurisdiction in which this insurance policy was issued. Determines applicable regulatory oversight (e.g., FCA in GBR, BaFin in DEU, NAIC in USA) and tax treatment of premiums.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; GDPR Article 3; IFRS 17 Insurance Contracts

description: Valuation method used to calculate the insured value of cargo for settlement purposes. 'invoice_value' uses the commercial invoice amount; 'declared_value' uses the shipper-declared value on the AWB or BOL; 'market_value' uses prevailing market price at destination; 'replacement_cost' uses the cost to replace the goods.
reference: IATA Warsaw-Montreal Convention; CMR Convention; IMO Hague-Visby Rules; Institute Cargo Clauses

description: Indicates whether this policy is structured as an open cover (blanket policy covering all qualifying shipments automatically without individual declaration) rather than a specific voyage or shipment policy. Open cover policies are common for high-volume freight forwarders.
reference: Institute Cargo Clauses — Lloyd's Market Association; IATA Cargo Insurance Guidelines

description: Maximum insurer liability for any single shipment event under this policy, expressed in coverage_limit_currency. May be lower than the aggregate coverage_limit_amount. Used in claim settlement calculations for individual AWB, BOL, or consignment note events.
reference: IATA Warsaw-Montreal Convention Article 22; CMR Convention Article 23; IMO Hague-Visby Rules

description: Externally-known alphanumeric identifier assigned by the insurer to uniquely identify the cargo insurance policy. Used in all insurer correspondence, claim filings, and settlement documentation. Maps to the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER canonical category for MASTER_AGREEMENT role.

Current lifecycle state of the cargo insurance policy. 'active' indicates the policy is in force and eligible for claim filing; 'suspended' indicates temporary coverage halt; 'expired' indicates the policy period has ended without renewal; 'cancelled' indicates early termination; 'pending_renewal' indicates renewal is in progress; 'lapsed' indicates non-renewal after expiry. Maps to LIFECYCLE_STATUS canonical category.
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems; IFRS 17 Insurance Contracts

description: Classification of the cargo insurance policy by coverage scope. 'all_risk' covers all physical loss or damage except named exclusions; 'named_perils' covers only explicitly listed risks; 'war_risk' covers war, strikes, and political risks; 'total_loss_only' covers only complete cargo loss; 'open_cover' is a continuous blanket policy for multiple shipments. Maps to CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE canonical category.
reference: Institute Cargo Clauses (A, B, C) — Lloyd's Market Association; IATA Cargo Insurance Guidelines

description: Annual or periodic insurance premium paid by Transport Shipping to the insurer for this policy. Used in finance for cost allocation, OPEX reporting, and profitability analysis per trade lane or business unit.
reference: IFRS 17 Insurance Contracts; SOX Section 404 Financial Reporting

description: Frequency at which the insurance premium is paid to the insurer. Drives accounts payable scheduling in SAP S/4HANA Finance and cash flow forecasting.
reference: IFRS 17 Insurance Contracts; SAP S/4HANA Finance AP Module

description: Indicates whether the insurer has placed reinsurance for this policy. Relevant for large-value cargo policies where the insurer's net retention is limited. Affects claim recovery timelines and insurer solvency risk assessment.
reference: IFRS 17 Insurance Contracts; Lloyd's Market Association Reinsurance Clauses

description: Date on which the policy is due for renewal. Used to trigger renewal negotiations, premium reviews, and coverage reassessment. Typically aligns with or precedes the expiry_date.
reference: IFRS 17 Insurance Contracts; ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems

description: Free-text field capturing policy-specific endorsements, exclusions, warranties, or special clauses that modify the standard Institute Cargo Clauses terms. Examples include temperature warranty for perishables, packing warranty, or exclusion of specific trade lanes.
reference: Institute Cargo Clauses — Lloyd's Market Association; IATA Cargo Insurance Guidelines

description: Indicates whether the insurer retains the right of subrogation to pursue recovery from liable third parties (carriers, handlers, customs authorities) after settling a claim. When True, Transport Shipping must cooperate with insurer recovery actions and preserve evidence.
reference: CMR Convention Article 37; IATA Warsaw-Montreal Convention Article 37; IMO Hague-Visby Rules

description: Minimum claim value (in coverage_limit_currency) above which an independent cargo survey report is mandatory before the insurer will process the claim. Below this threshold, claims may be settled on documentary evidence alone.
reference: Institute Cargo Clauses — Lloyd's Market Association; IATA Cargo Claims Handling Manual

description: Timestamp when this claim policy record was last modified in the Transport Shipping data platform. Updated on every write operation. Used for change data capture, incremental ETL processing, and audit compliance.
reference: SOX Section 404; GDPR Article 5(2) Accountability; ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the claim reserve record in the Transport Shipping lakehouse. Primary key for the claim_reserve data product.
reference: IAS 37 Provisions, Contingent Liabilities and Contingent Assets; SOX Section 404

description: Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Reserve amounts are set based on contractual liability caps and coverage terms. Risk management requires contract reference to ensure reserves do not exceed maximum contractual exposure and to assess
Reference to the parent cargo claim record for which this financial reserve has been established. Links the reserve to the claim lifecycle in the claim domain.
reference: IAS 37; IATA Warsaw-Montreal Convention; CMR Convention

description: Foreign key linking to network.carrier. Business justification: Reserves are established based on carrier liability exposure; reserve adequacy reviews require carrier risk assessment. Essential for carrier-specific reserve management, financial provisioning, and c

Foreign key linking to network.carrier_service. Business justification: Reserves are established based on service-level liability exposure and historical claims patterns. Actuarial requirement for service-specific reserve adequacy, financial reporting, and service profita

Foreign key linking to customer.customer_account. Business justification: Reserves are established per customer account for financial planning and credit risk management. Enables customer-level reserve reporting, credit exposure analysis, and customer financial health monit

Foreign key linking to claim.liability_determination. Business justification: A financial reserve is established based on the formal liability determination for a cargo claim. The liability determination drives the reserve amount, reserve type, and expected settlement exposure.

Foreign key linking to network.agent. Business justification: Financial reserves are allocated against agent liabilities when agents are potentially liable parties. Reserve management for agent exposures, agent risk scoring, and financial planning for agent-rela

Foreign key linking to claim.claim_policy. Business justification: Financial reserve is established under a specific insurance policy for liability exposure tracking. N:1 relationship (many reserves per policy). Adding claim_policy_id FK allows normalization of polic

Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Financial reserves for procurement claims need PO linkage for budget allocation, transaction-level tracking, and supplier chargeback planning. Real business process: procurement claim financial reserv
Reference to the cargo insurer or underwriter responsible for covering the claim liability. Used for insurer correspondence tracking and subrogation recovery workflows.

Date of the most recent periodic reserve adequacy review conducted by the claims finance team. IAS 37 requires reserves to be reviewed at each reporting date and adjusted to reflect the current best estimate.
Date on which the reserve establishment or most recent material adjustment was formally approved by the authorized approver. Part of the SOX-compliant financial control audit trail.

Name or employee identifier of the authorized approver who sanctioned the reserve establishment or most recent material adjustment. Required for SOX segregation of duties and financial control compliance.

Category of the underlying cargo claim driving this reserve. Determines applicable liability convention (IATA Warsaw-Montreal, CMR, IMO) and maximum reserve ceiling. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cargo_loss|cargo_damage|delay|shortage|misdelivery|dangerous_goods — promote to reference product]
reference: IATA Warsaw-Montreal Convention; CMR Convention; IMO; IMDG Code

description: System timestamp when the claim reserve record was first created in the Transport Shipping lakehouse. Supports audit trail, SOX compliance, and data lineage tracking.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the reserve amounts are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency balance sheet consolidation under IFRS.
reference: ISO 4217; IFRS; IAS 21 Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates

description: Most recent reserve balance after all adjustments (increases and decreases). Reflects the current best estimate of outstanding liability exposure on the balance sheet. Updated with each reserve adjustment posting in SAP S/4HANA Finance.
Indicates whether the underlying claim involves dangerous goods cargo. Triggers enhanced reserve assessment procedures and compliance checks under IMDG Code and ICAO Technical Instructions.
reference: IMDG Code; ICAO Technical Instructions for Dangerous Goods; IMO; IATA

description: Number of calendar days the reserve has been open since establishment_date. Used in reserve aging reports and KPI dashboards to identify long-outstanding reserves requiring management escalation.
Calendar date on which the financial reserve was first established in SAP S/4HANA Finance. Used as the IAS 37 provision recognition date for balance sheet reporting and SOX-compliant financial close.
Foreign exchange rate applied to translate reserve_currency amounts into functional_currency_amount at the reporting date. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA Finance exchange rate tables.
Estimated date by which the underlying claim is expected to be settled and the reserve released. Used for cash flow forecasting, reserve aging analysis, and IFRS 37 disclosure of timing of outflows.
Current reserve amount translated into the entity's functional currency using the closing exchange rate at the reporting date. Required for IFRS consolidated financial statements and SOX reporting.
ISO 4217 three-letter code for the entity's functional currency used in IFRS consolidated reporting (e.g., USD). Paired with functional_currency_amount.

Original estimated liability exposure amount posted to the balance sheet at reserve establishment. Represents the best estimate of the probable outflow of economic resources as required by IAS 37. Denominated in reserve_currency.

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country or region code indicating the primary data privacy jurisdiction applicable to the account hierarchy record. Determines GDPR, CCPA, or other regional data protection obligations for customer data handling.
reference: GDPR Article 4 and Article 44; ISO/IEC 27001 Information Security Management

description: Date on which the most recent denied party and sanctions screening was performed for the enterprise account group. Ensures compliance screening currency per regulatory requirements.
reference: Descartes Customs Denied Party Screening; OFAC Regulations; WCO Customs Compliance

description: Result of the most recent denied party and sanctions screening check for the enterprise account group. Managed in Descartes Customs denied party screening module. Flagged accounts require compliance review before shipment execution.
reference: Descartes Customs Denied Party Screening; WCO Customs Compliance; US DOT Sanctions Compliance; OFAC Regulations

description: Date from which the account hierarchy relationship becomes binding and operationally active. Used to manage time-bound corporate restructuring, mergers, and acquisitions in the customer master.
reference: IFRS 10 Consolidated Financial Statements; Salesforce CRM Effective Dating

description: Date on which the account hierarchy relationship ceases to be binding. Nullable for open-ended relationships. Populated upon corporate restructuring, divestiture, or account termination.
reference: IFRS 10 Consolidated Financial Statements; Salesforce CRM Effective Dating

business_glossary_term: Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) Customer Hierarchy ID
description: SAP S/4HANA Finance customer hierarchy node identifier for the account group. Enables reconciliation between CRM account structures and ERP financial hierarchies for consolidated billing and revenue recognition.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Customer Hierarchy; IFRS 15 Revenue from Contracts with Customers

description: Name of the Transport Shipping global account manager responsible for the enterprise account hierarchy. Sourced from Salesforce CRM account ownership for escalation routing and relationship management.
reference: Salesforce CRM Account Ownership; Transport Shipping Commercial Policy

description: Externally-known business identifier for the account hierarchy relationship, used in Salesforce CRM and SAP S/4HANA Finance for cross-system reference and consolidated billing linkage.
reference: Salesforce CRM Account Hierarchy Standard; SAP S/4HANA Customer Master

description: Numeric depth of the child account within the corporate hierarchy tree, where level 1 represents the ultimate parent (global account). Enables multi-tier enterprise account navigation and reporting.
reference: Salesforce CRM Account Hierarchy; SAP S/4HANA Customer Hierarchy Level

description: Human-readable label for the account hierarchy relationship, typically reflecting the corporate group name (e.g., 'Acme Corp Global Group'). Used in CRM dashboards and enterprise account reporting.
reference: Salesforce CRM Account Hierarchy; SAP S/4HANA Customer Master

description: Materialized path string representing the full ancestry chain from root to child account (e.g., '/1001/2045/3087'). Enables efficient tree traversal queries for enterprise account reporting and consolidated billing without recursive joins.
reference: Salesforce CRM Account Hierarchy; SAP S/4HANA Customer Hierarchy Path

description: Current lifecycle state of the account hierarchy relationship. Controls whether the parent-child linkage is operationally active for billing consolidation, SLA management, and enterprise reporting.
reference: Salesforce CRM Account Status; SAP S/4HANA Customer Master Status

description: Classifies the purpose of the hierarchy structure. Corporate hierarchies reflect legal ownership; billing hierarchies drive consolidated invoicing; operational hierarchies manage service delivery; 3PL/4PL hierarchies manage logistics partner structures.
reference: Salesforce CRM Account Hierarchy; SAP S/4HANA Customer Hierarchy Type

description: Default International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) applicable to the enterprise account group's shipments. Defines risk and cost transfer points for cross-border logistics. Sourced from SAP TM and Oracle TMS shipment configuration. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: ICC Incoterms 2020; WTO International Trade Standards; IMO Cargo Liability

description: The primary industry sector of the enterprise account group (e.g., Retail, Automotive, Pharmaceutical, Technology, Energy). Used for vertical-specific SLA configuration, compliance requirements, and targeted service offerings in Salesforce CRM.
reference: Salesforce CRM Industry Classification; NAICS Industry Classification System

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the account hierarchy relationship record. Supports change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks lakehouse silver layer and audit trail compliance.
reference: SOX Section 404 Internal Controls; GDPR Article 5 Data Integrity; ISO/IEC 27001

description: Maximum number of levels in the account hierarchy subtree rooted at this relationship node. Used for hierarchy validation, UI rendering optimization, and enterprise account structure governance.
reference: Salesforce CRM Account Hierarchy; SAP S/4HANA Customer Hierarchy Configuration

description: Free-text field for capturing additional context, special instructions, or business commentary related to the account hierarchy relationship. Used by global account managers and finance teams for relationship management.
reference: Salesforce CRM Account Notes; SAP S/4HANA Customer Master Notes

description: Percentage of ownership or equity stake held by the parent account in the child account. Relevant for subsidiary and joint venture relationships. Used for IFRS 10 consolidation threshold determination and financial reporting.
reference: IFRS 10 Consolidated Financial Statements; IFRS 3 Business Combinations

description: Standard payment terms code applicable to the enterprise account group (e.g., NET30, NET60, COD). Inherited by child accounts when consolidation is active. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA Finance accounts receivable configuration.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Finance AR Payment Terms; IFRS 15 Revenue from Contracts with Customers

description: Indicates whether the child account inherits the contracted pricing, rate agreements, and GRI exemptions negotiated at the parent account level. Drives tariff application in SAP TM and Oracle TMS.
reference: SAP TM Charge Calculation; Oracle TMS Freight Audit; IATA Rate Agreements

description: Classifies the nature of the parent-child relationship within the account hierarchy. Drives how billing consolidation, SLA inheritance, and revenue roll-up are applied. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: parent|subsidiary|affiliate|regional_division|branch|joint_venture — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]
reference: Salesforce CRM Account Relationship Types; IFRS 10 Consolidated Financial Statements

description: Business reason for the creation or modification of the account hierarchy relationship. Supports audit trail and change management for corporate restructuring events. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: merger|acquisition|divestiture|reorganization|new_subsidiary|account_migration|other — promote to reference product if additional reasons are needed]
reference: IFRS 3 Business Combinations; IFRS 10 Consolidated Financial Statements; SOX Change Management

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the annual contracted revenue amount. Ensures consistent multi-currency financial reporting across global enterprise accounts.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS 21 Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates

description: Indicates whether the child account's revenue is rolled up to the parent account for enterprise-level P&L reporting and EBITDA analysis. Enables group revenue visibility in SAP S/4HANA Finance and CRM dashboards.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Finance Revenue Recognition; IFRS 15 Revenue from Contracts with Customers; SOX Financial Reporting

description: Indicates whether the child account inherits the SLA entitlements (e.g., OTD targets, OTIF commitments, priority service levels) from the parent account. Drives SLA enforcement and KPI reporting at the group level.
reference: Salesforce CRM SLA Management; ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems

description: Percentage of voting rights held by the parent account in the child entity. Determines control classification under IFRS 10 (control threshold typically >50%) for financial consolidation and regulatory reporting.
reference: IFRS 10 Consolidated Financial Statements; FMC Ownership Disclosure Requirements

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each address record in the centralized address book. Primary key for the address_book data product.
Reference to the customer (shipper, consignee, or account holder) who owns or is associated with this address record. Links the address to the customer master.

Foreign key linking to warehouse.facility. Business justification: Address book entries of type 'warehouse' or 'distribution_center' must link to actual facility records for dock scheduling, address validation, and facility-based shipment routing in transport logisti

Foreign key linking to route.network_node. Business justification: Customer addresses map to network nodes for routing and planning. Powers address-to-node geocoding, route optimization, network design, and facility assignment. Essential for translating customer loca

Foreign key linking to route.route_delivery_zone. Business justification: Address book entries must be mapped to delivery zones for last-mile routing, surcharge calculation (residential, remote area), and service availability checks. Zone-to-address mapping is a fundamental

Externally-known unique business identifier or short code assigned to this address record, used in shipment booking, EDI transactions, and TMS integrations (e.g., SAP TM location code, Oracle TMS address code). Enables cross-system address referencing.
reference: SAP TM - Location Master; Oracle TMS - Address Reference

description: Human-readable display name or alias for this address record (e.g., 'London Heathrow Warehouse', 'NYC HQ Billing', 'Shanghai Port CFS'). Used in shipment booking UIs, ePOD systems, and customer-facing communications to identify addresses without displaying full address details.
reference: Salesforce CRM - Address Label; GDPR Article 5 (Data Minimisation)

description: Primary street address line including building number, street name, and any suite or unit designation. This is the first and most critical line of the physical address used on shipping labels, AWBs, BOLs, and customs declarations.
reference: Universal Postal Union (UPU) S42 Address Standard; WCO Customs Declaration

description: Secondary address line for additional location details such as floor number, building name, industrial estate, or complex name. Optional field used when the primary address line is insufficient to uniquely identify the delivery location.
reference: Universal Postal Union (UPU) S42 Address Standard

description: Tertiary address line for additional location context such as district, neighborhood, or landmark reference. Commonly used in international addresses where additional geographic context is required for customs or last-mile delivery.
reference: Universal Postal Union (UPU) S42 Address Standard; WCO Customs Declaration

description: Functional role this address plays in logistics operations. An address may be classified as an origin pickup location, delivery destination, billing address, return address, warehouse address, or notify party address. A single physical address may have multiple roles across different shipments. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: origin|destination|billing|return|warehouse|notify_party — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP TM - Location Role; Incoterms 2020 (ICC); IATA Cargo Handling Manual

description: Current lifecycle state of the address record. Active addresses are available for shipment booking. Pending_validation addresses are awaiting address verification. Deprecated addresses are retained for historical reference but not available for new bookings. Blacklisted addresses are blocked due to compliance or fraud concerns.
reference: ISO 9001 - Quality Management; C-TPAT Compliance Guidelines

description: Classification of the physical address by its operational nature. Drives last-mile delivery rules, customs declaration requirements, and carrier surcharge logic. Residential addresses may attract residential delivery surcharges; port/airport addresses trigger terminal handling charge (THC) logic. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: residential|commercial|warehouse|port|airport|border_crossing|free_trade_zone — promote to reference product]
reference: IATA Ground Handling Manual; IMO Port Facility Standards; US DOT Address Classification

description: Official AEO certificate number issued by the relevant customs authority (e.g., EU EORI-based AEO number, C-TPAT account number). Required for customs pre-clearance, reduced inspection, and trusted trader benefits. Validated in Descartes Customs during shipment processing.
reference: WCO AEO Framework; EU AEO Programme; C-TPAT (US CBP); AEO (Authorized Economic Operator Program)

description: Indicates whether the entity at this address holds Authorized Economic Operator (AEO) certification, which grants expedited customs clearance and reduced inspection rates. AEO-certified addresses enable simplified customs procedures and are prioritized in Descartes Customs trade compliance workflows.
reference: WCO AEO Framework; EU AEO Programme (Regulation EC 648/2005); C-TPAT (US CBP)

description: City or municipality name of the address. Used in route planning, carrier zone determination, customs declarations, and last-mile delivery assignment. Critical for TMS routing and freight rate calculation.
reference: Universal Postal Union (UPU) S42; WCO Customs Declaration; SAP TM Location Master

description: Name of the company, organization, or business entity at this address. Required for commercial shipments, customs declarations, and B2B delivery. Used on shipping labels, air waybills (AWB), bills of lading (BOL), and customs documentation.
reference: IATA Air Waybill Standards; IMO Bill of Lading Requirements; WCO Customs Declaration Standards

description: Email address of the primary contact at this address. Used for shipment notifications, electronic proof of delivery (ePOD) dispatch, customs pre-arrival notifications, and delivery exception alerts.

Full name of the primary contact person at this address. Used for last-mile delivery coordination, customs notifications, and proof of delivery (POD) capture. Required for residential and commercial delivery addresses.
reference: GDPR Article 4 - Personal Data Definition; IATA Cargo Handling Manual

description: Primary telephone number for the contact person at this address, in E.164 international format. Used for last-mile delivery notifications, exception management, and customs clearance coordination. Required by many carriers for residential delivery.
reference: GDPR Article 4; ITU-T E.164 International Numbering Plan

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code of the address (e.g., USA, GBR, CHN, DEU). Mandatory for cross-border shipments, customs declarations, Incoterms application, trade compliance screening, and international freight rate determination.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; WCO Customs Declaration; IATA Air Waybill Standards; IMO Bill of Lading

description: Date and time when this address record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Provides audit trail for address master data governance, GDPR data subject access requests, and SOX compliance reporting.
reference: GDPR Article 5 (Accountability); SOX Section 404; ISO/IEC 27001 Audit Logging

description: Customs and trade compliance zone classification for this address. Free Trade Zone (FTZ) and bonded warehouse addresses require special customs handling in Descartes Customs. Special Economic Zone (SEZ) addresses may have preferential duty treatment. Drives customs declaration routing and duty calculation.
reference: WCO Customs Convention; Descartes Global Trade Management; US CBP FTZ Regulations; WTO Trade Facilitation Agreement

description: Indicates whether dangerous goods (DG) shipments are permitted at this address. When False, the TMS and booking systems will block DG shipments to/from this address. Governed by IMDG Code for ocean, ICAO Technical Instructions for air, and ADR/RID for road/rail transport.
reference: IMDG Code (IMO); ICAO Technical Instructions for Dangerous Goods; ADR European Agreement on Dangerous Goods

description: Latest time of day (HH:MM in 24-hour format) when deliveries or pickups are accepted at this address. Used alongside delivery_window_open to define the acceptable delivery time window for route planning and SLA management.
reference: SAP TM - Time Window Constraints; Oracle TMS - Delivery Scheduling

description: Earliest time of day (HH:MM in 24-hour format) when deliveries or pickups are accepted at this address. Used by TMS route optimization to schedule delivery windows and ensure SLA compliance. Critical for warehouse and commercial addresses with restricted receiving hours.
reference: SAP TM - Time Window Constraints; Oracle TMS - Delivery Scheduling

description: Date from which this address record is valid and available for use in shipment bookings and logistics operations. Supports address lifecycle management and temporal validity for contract logistics, where addresses may be activated on a specific date aligned with contract start.
reference: ISO 8601 Date Standard; SAP TM - Location Validity Period

description: Date after which this address record is no longer valid for new shipment bookings. Null indicates an open-ended validity. Used for address lifecycle management when customers relocate, close facilities, or terminate contracts. Historical shipments referencing this address are retained for audit purposes.
reference: ISO 8601 Date Standard; SAP TM - Location Validity Period; SOX Audit Trail Requirements

business_glossary_term: Economic Operators Registration and Identification (EORI) Number
description: EORI number assigned by EU customs authorities to identify economic operators in customs declarations and trade transactions. Required for all import/export customs filings within the EU. Stored at the address level to support multi-site customers with different EORI registrations per facility.
reference: EU Customs Code (Regulation EU 952/2013); WCO Customs Declaration Standards; Descartes Global Trade Management

description: Type of logistics facility at this address when applicable. Drives specialized handling requirements in Manhattan WMS, carrier selection in SAP TM, and customs processing in Descartes. Cold storage facilities require temperature-controlled transport; bonded warehouses require customs bond management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: distribution_center|cross_dock|cold_storage|bonded_warehouse|cfs|icd|airport_cargo|seaport_terminal — promote to reference product]
reference: Manhattan WMS - Facility Configuration; IMO Port Facility Standards; IATA Cargo Handling Manual

description: Precision level of the geocoded latitude/longitude coordinates. Rooftop indicates exact building-level precision; range_interpolated is street-level; geometric_center is centroid of a postal area; approximate is city/region level; unverified means coordinates have not been validated. Drives confidence in GPS-based routing and geofencing.
reference: Google Maps Geocoding API Standards; FourKites Geofencing Configuration

description: Comma-separated list of HS Code prefixes (Harmonized System codes per WCO) that are restricted or prohibited at this address due to local regulations, facility licensing, or trade compliance requirements. Used by Descartes Customs to flag non-compliant shipments during booking.
reference: WCO Harmonized System (HS) Nomenclature; Descartes Global Trade Management; C-TPAT Compliance

description: Indicates whether this address is the customer's default billing address for invoice generation and accounts receivable processing in SAP S/4HANA Finance. When True, this address is used for all financial documents unless overridden at the shipment level.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Finance - Customer Master; IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition

description: Indicates whether this address is the customer's default delivery destination. When True, this address is pre-populated as the destination in shipment booking workflows, supporting frequent lane customers and e-commerce fulfillment automation.
reference: SAP TM - Default Location Configuration; Oracle TMS - Shipment Booking

description: Indicates whether this address is the customer's default pickup/origin location for shipment booking. When True, this address is pre-populated as the origin in shipment booking workflows in SAP TM and Oracle TMS, reducing data entry errors.
reference: SAP TM - Default Location Configuration; Oracle TMS - Shipment Booking

description: Indicates whether this address is classified as a residential location. Residential addresses trigger carrier-specific residential delivery surcharges (e.g., FedEx, UPS residential fees), require different last-mile delivery protocols, and may affect SLA commitments for D2D (door-to-door) services.
reference: US DOT Residential Delivery Classification; IATA Cargo Handling Manual

description: Date and time when this address record was most recently updated, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Used for change data capture (CDC) in ETL pipelines, optimistic locking in concurrent update scenarios, and audit trail compliance.
reference: GDPR Article 5 (Accountability); SOX Section 404; ISO/IEC 27001 Audit Logging

description: Geographic latitude coordinate of the address in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum), ranging from -90 to +90. Used for GPS-based last-mile routing, geofencing in FourKites real-time visibility, proximity-based warehouse assignment, and route optimization in TMS.
reference: WGS84 Geodetic Datum (ISO 6709); FourKites Real-Time Visibility Platform

description: Geographic longitude coordinate of the address in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum), ranging from -180 to +180. Used alongside latitude for GPS-based routing, geofencing, real-time shipment tracking, and network design optimization in Blue Yonder.
reference: WGS84 Geodetic Datum (ISO 6709); FourKites Real-Time Visibility Platform; Blue Yonder Network Design

description: Postal or ZIP code of the address. Used for carrier zone mapping, freight rate calculation, last-mile delivery routing, dimensional weight (DIM weight) zone surcharges, and address validation. Format varies by country.
reference: Universal Postal Union (UPU) S42 Address Standard; IATA Rate Construction Manual

description: Operational system of record from which this address record originated. Enables data lineage tracking, conflict resolution during master data synchronization, and audit compliance. Salesforce CRM is the primary source for customer-facing addresses; SAP TM and Oracle TMS for logistics locations.
reference: SAP TM; Oracle TMS; Salesforce CRM; Manhattan WMS; Descartes Customs; ISO/IEC 27001 Data Governance

description: Native identifier of this address record in the originating operational system (e.g., Salesforce CRM Address ID, SAP TM Location ID, Oracle TMS Address Code). Enables bidirectional synchronization, change data capture (CDC), and cross-system reconciliation during EDI processing.
reference: SAP TM - Location Master; Salesforce CRM - Address Object; Oracle TMS - Address Reference

description: Free-text field for special handling or access instructions at this address (e.g., 'Deliver to loading dock B', 'Call 30 minutes before arrival', 'Gate code: 1234', 'No deliveries before 08:00'). Transmitted to last-mile carriers and drivers via TMS dispatch notes.
reference: Oracle TMS - Delivery Instructions; Manhattan WMS - Outbound Shipping Notes

description: Full normalized and standardized address string as returned by the address validation provider, conforming to postal authority formatting rules. Used as the canonical address for printing on shipping labels, AWBs, and customs declarations to ensure deliverability.
reference: Universal Postal Union (UPU) S42 Address Standard; USPS Publication 28

description: State, province, or administrative region of the address using ISO 3166-2 subdivision codes where applicable. Required for domestic routing, tax jurisdiction determination, and US/Canada customs filings (AMS, ISF).
reference: ISO 3166-2 Country Subdivision Codes; US CBP AMS/ISF Filing Requirements

description: IANA timezone identifier for the address location (e.g., 'America/New_York', 'Europe/London', 'Asia/Shanghai'). Used to correctly interpret delivery windows, calculate ETA/ETD in local time, and schedule last-mile delivery notifications across global operations.
reference: IANA Time Zone Database; IATA Timetable Standards

description: Name of the third-party address validation service or postal authority database used to validate this address (e.g., USPS, Loqate, SmartyStreets, Google Maps, HERE). Enables audit trail for address quality and supports provider performance benchmarking.
reference: Universal Postal Union (UPU) Address Quality Standards

description: Result of the address standardization and validation process against postal authority databases (e.g., USPS, Royal Mail, Deutsche Post). Validated addresses have been confirmed deliverable. Failed addresses contain errors. Partial addresses are incomplete but usable. Used to enforce address quality gates in shipment booking workflows.
reference: Universal Postal Union (UPU) Address Quality Standards; USPS Address Standardization

description: Date and time when the address was last validated against a postal authority or address verification service. Used to determine if re-validation is required based on address data freshness policies.
reference: Universal Postal Union (UPU) Address Quality Standards; ISO 9001 Quality Management

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the consignee profile record in the Transport Shipping data platform. Primary key for the consignee_profile entity.

Foreign key linking to customs.compliance_program. Business justification: Consignee AEO/C-TPAT compliance program membership determines import examination rates, expedited release eligibility, and DDP authorization. Logistics operators link consignee profiles to active comp

Foreign key linking to customer.customer_account. Business justification: Consignee profiles represent customers acting in the consignee role. This FK links the operational consignee profile to the master customer account record. The consignee_profile table contains role-sp

Foreign key linking to network.agent. Business justification: consignee_profile has customs_broker_code as plain text. The import customs agent/broker is a network agent entity handling clearance for the consignee. Role-prefixed as customs_agent_id. customs_brok

Foreign key linking to customer.address_book. Business justification: A consignee profile represents the destination recipient in a logistics shipment. The consignee's delivery address should be stored in the centralized address_book for address validation, geocoding ac

Foreign key linking to route.network_node. Business justification: Consignees have delivery location nodes for last-mile routing. Required for delivery scheduling, route optimization, last-mile zone assignment, and delivery promise calculation. Replaces denormalized
EDI interchange partner identifier (ISA qualifier + ID) for the consignee, enabling automated transmission of ASNs, delivery confirmations, and customs pre-arrival notifications.
reference: ANSI X12; UN/EDIFACT; GS1 EDI Standards

description: Foreign key linking to customs.license_permit. Business justification: Import license management is a core customs compliance process for consignees importing restricted or controlled goods. Logistics operators must link consignee profiles to their active import license/

Foreign key linking to customs.trade_party. Business justification: Consignee profiles represent importers/receivers in customs. Required for: import declarations, customs broker assignment, duty assessment, and delivery clearance. Critical for inbound customs operati

Foreign key linking to network.carrier. Business justification: Consignees in last-mile logistics designate preferred delivery carriers (e.g., residential consignees preferring specific couriers). This drives last-mile carrier selection in delivery planning. No ex

Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: A consignee profile has a designated primary contact person for delivery coordination (signature requirements, appointment scheduling, ePOD). The consignee_profile currently stores primary_contact_ema

Foreign key linking to route.route_delivery_zone. Business justification: Consignee profiles are mapped to delivery zones for last-mile routing, residential/remote surcharge application, and delivery window scheduling. Zone assignment is fundamental to last-mile logistics o

Foreign key linking to network.tpl_provider. Business justification: Consignees in B2B logistics are often served by a specific 3PL for last-mile delivery and returns. warehouse_dock_number implies 3PL warehouse involvement. Linking to tpl_provider enables automated de
Current lifecycle status of the consignee profile. Blacklisted status is set by Descartes Customs denied-party screening. Pending verification indicates incomplete KYC or trade compliance checks.
reference: Descartes Customs GTM; C-TPAT; AEO Program

description: Indicates whether the consignee requires an Advanced Shipping Notice (ASN) to be transmitted via EDI prior to shipment arrival. When True, ASN generation is triggered in the WMS/TMS integration layer.
reference: GS1 EDI ASN (856); Manhattan WMS ASN Processing; ANSI X12 856

description: Classification of the consignee indicating the nature of the receiving party. Drives SLA entitlements, customs handling rules, and delivery workflows. B2B enterprise and 3PL/4PL partners are recurring; B2C individual may be one-time last-mile recipients. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: b2b_enterprise|b2c_individual|3pl_partner|4pl_partner|government|nvocc|freight_forwarder|broker — promote to reference product]

Maximum outstanding credit amount extended to this consignee for duty deferment, freight charges on account, or COD (Cash on Delivery) waiver arrangements. Managed in SAP S/4HANA Finance accounts receivable.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Finance AR; SOX; IFRS 9

description: C-TPAT membership status of the consignee as maintained in Descartes Customs. Certified status qualifies the consignee for reduced US CBP examination rates and expedited cargo release.

Indicates whether the consignee has authorized Transport Shipping to arrange DDP (Delivered Duty Paid) shipments on their behalf, where the shipper or carrier pre-pays import duties and taxes. Relevant for cross-border e-commerce fulfillment.
reference: ICC Incoterms 2020 DDP; WCO; Descartes Customs GTM

description: Outcome of the most recent denied-party screening check. A match_found or blocked result prevents shipment processing until compliance review is completed.
reference: Descartes Customs GTM; OFAC Sanctions List; EU Consolidated Sanctions List

description: Timestamp of the most recent denied-party screening check performed against this consignee in Descartes Customs. Required for trade compliance audit trails under US EAR, OFAC, and EU dual-use regulations.
reference: Descartes Customs GTM; OFAC; EU Dual-Use Regulation 2021/821; C-TPAT

business_glossary_term: Electronic Proof of Delivery (ePOD) Contact Email
description: Dedicated email address to which electronic proof of delivery (ePOD) documents are transmitted upon successful delivery. May differ from the primary contact email when a separate receiving or logistics team handles POD acknowledgment.
reference: IATA ePOD Standards; Transport Shipping ePOD Policy

business_glossary_term: Electronic Proof of Delivery (ePOD) Contact Phone
description: Mobile or direct phone number for the ePOD recipient contact, used by drivers and last-mile couriers to confirm delivery and obtain digital signature authorization.
GPS latitude coordinate of the consignee delivery location in decimal degrees (WGS84). Used by FourKites for geofencing, last-mile ETA prediction, and proof-of-delivery geo-stamping.
reference: WGS84; FourKites Real-Time Visibility; GPS Standards

description: GPS longitude coordinate of the consignee delivery location in decimal degrees (WGS84). Used alongside latitude for geofencing and route optimization in SAP TM and FourKites.
reference: WGS84; FourKites Real-Time Visibility; GPS Standards

description: Indicates whether the consignee must present government-issued identification at the time of delivery. Applicable for high-value shipments, controlled goods, or regulatory requirements.
reference: Transport Shipping Security Policy; C-TPAT; ISO 28000

description: Consignee's preferred Incoterms rule governing the transfer of risk and cost responsibility for import shipments. Incoterms 2020 standard. Drives duty and tax liability assignment in customs declarations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — promote to reference product]
reference: ICC Incoterms 2020; WCO Customs Data Model; IATA

description: Indicates whether this consignee is a recurring destination party (True) or a one-time recipient such as a B2C last-mile delivery addressee (False). Drives profile retention and SLA assignment logic.
Full legal name of the consignee as it appears on customs declarations, air waybills (AWB), and bills of lading (BOL). Used for trade compliance screening and import documentation.
reference: WCO Customs Data Model; IATA Resolution 600a; GDPR Article 4

description: IETF BCP 47 language tag representing the consignee's preferred language for shipment notifications, delivery alerts, and ePOD communications (e.g., en-US, fr-FR, zh-CN).
reference: IETF BCP 47; GDPR Article 12 (communication in clear language)

description: Standard payment terms code applicable to this consignee for freight charges billed to destination (e.g., NET30, NET60, COD, PREPAID). Sourced from SAP S/4HANA Finance customer master.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Finance Customer Master; IFRS 15

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code representing the consignee's preferred billing and invoicing currency for duty, tax, and freight charge settlements.
Comma-separated list of preferred delivery days of the week (e.g., MON,TUE,WED,THU,FRI). Used by last-mile scheduling to avoid unattended delivery attempts and reduce failed delivery rates.
reference: SAP TM; Oracle TMS Last-Mile Scheduling

description: Consignee-preferred latest time of day (HH:MM, 24-hour format) for delivery attempts. Defines the close of the acceptable delivery window for last-mile scheduling.
reference: SAP TM Delivery Window Configuration; Oracle TMS Scheduling

description: Consignee-preferred earliest time of day (HH:MM, 24-hour format) for delivery attempts. Used by last-mile routing engines in Oracle TMS and SAP TM to schedule delivery slots within SLA windows.
reference: SAP TM Delivery Window Configuration; Oracle TMS Scheduling

description: Timestamp when the consignee profile record was first created in the Transport Shipping data platform. Represents the record audit creation event for lineage and compliance tracking.
reference: GDPR Article 30 (Records of Processing); SOX; ISO 28000

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the consignee profile record. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver layer and audit trail compliance.
Indicates whether the consignee requires a pre-scheduled delivery appointment before a driver can attempt delivery. True triggers appointment scheduling workflow in Oracle TMS.
reference: Oracle TMS Appointment Scheduling; SAP TM

description: External reference identifier from Salesforce CRM linking this consignee profile to the corresponding CRM account record for B2B consignees. Null for one-time B2C recipients.
Indicates whether a consignee signature is mandatory for proof of delivery (POD). When True, drivers must capture a digital signature via ePOD before completing the delivery event.
reference: IATA ePOD Standards; Transport Shipping POD Policy
