reference: Salesforce CRM — Case Object (Status); ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems

description: Classification of the service case by the nature of the customer interaction. Drives routing, SLA assignment, and reporting segmentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: shipment_inquiry|service_failure|complaint|claims_initiation|billing_dispute|general_support|customs_query|returns_rma — promote to reference product if additional types are added]
reference: Salesforce CRM — Case Object (Type); ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems

description: Indicates whether this service case has triggered the initiation of a formal cargo claim in the claims domain. True when the case type is claims_initiation and a claim record has been created. Provides the handoff signal between customer service workflow and financial liability management.
reference: ISO 9001; Federal Maritime Commission (FMC); IATA Resolution 600a

description: The date and time when the service case was formally closed after customer confirmation or automatic closure policy. Distinct from resolved_timestamp — a case may be resolved but remain open pending customer acknowledgement.
reference: Salesforce CRM — Case Object (ClosedDate); ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems

description: Free-text verbatim feedback provided by the customer as part of the post-case satisfaction survey. Complements the numeric CSAT score with qualitative insight for service improvement analysis.
reference: Salesforce CRM — Case Object (CSAT Comments); ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems

description: Customer satisfaction rating provided by the customer upon case closure, typically on a 1–5 scale. Sourced from Salesforce CRM post-case survey. Key KPI (Key Performance Indicator) for service quality measurement and agent performance management.
reference: Salesforce CRM — Case Object (CSAT); ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the shipment destination location referenced in this service case. Used for cross-border service failure analysis, last-mile delivery issue routing, and trade compliance case categorization.
reference: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3; WCO (World Customs Organization); IMO

description: The highest escalation tier reached by this case. Populated when is_escalated is True. Used for escalation pattern analysis and workforce capacity planning. None indicates no escalation occurred.
reference: Salesforce CRM — Case Escalation Rules; ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems

description: Free-text or structured reason provided by the agent when escalating the case. Documents the business justification for escalation, such as SLA breach risk, high-value customer, or regulatory sensitivity.
reference: Salesforce CRM — Case History; ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems

description: The date and time when the most recent escalation event was triggered for this case. Null if the case has never been escalated. Used for escalation response time analysis.
reference: Salesforce CRM — Case History; ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems

description: The date and time when the first substantive agent response was sent to the customer after case creation. Used to calculate SLA (Service Level Agreement) first-response compliance and OTD (On-Time Delivery) of service commitments.
reference: Salesforce CRM — Case Milestone (First Response); SLA governance

description: Indicates whether this service case has been formally escalated to a senior agent, team lead, or management tier at any point in its lifecycle. True if any escalation event has occurred.
reference: Salesforce CRM — Case Object (IsEscalated); ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems

description: The date and time when the service case record was most recently updated in the source system (Salesforce CRM). Used for incremental data pipeline processing and audit trail maintenance in the Silver layer.
reference: Salesforce CRM — Case Object (LastModifiedDate); GDPR Article 5 (data accuracy); SOX

description: The date and time when the case was most recently reopened after a prior resolution or closure. Null if the case has never been reopened. Used alongside reopen_count for resolution quality analytics.
reference: Salesforce CRM — Case History; ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems

description: The communication channel through which the customer submitted this service case. Used for channel analytics, staffing optimization, and omnichannel service reporting. EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) channel applies to B2B automated case submissions.
reference: Salesforce CRM — Case Object (Origin); IATA Customer Service Standards

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the shipment origin location referenced in this service case. Used for cross-border service failure analysis, customs-related case routing, and regulatory compliance reporting.
reference: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3; WCO (World Customs Organization); IMO

description: Indicates whether the requested Proof of Delivery (POD) or Electronic Proof of Delivery (ePOD) document has been successfully retrieved and attached to this case. Used to track case resolution blockers.
reference: FourKites Real-Time Visibility Platform; US DOT; IATA

description: Indicates whether a Proof of Delivery (POD) or Electronic Proof of Delivery (ePOD) document has been requested as part of resolving this service case. Common for delivery dispute and non-receipt inquiry cases.
reference: FourKites Real-Time Visibility Platform; US DOT; IATA

description: Business priority level assigned to the service case, determining response time targets and escalation thresholds per the customer SLA entitlement. Critical cases typically involve time-sensitive freight or enterprise account failures.
reference: Salesforce CRM — Case Object (Priority); SLA (Service Level Agreement) governance

description: Number of times this service case has been reopened after being marked as resolved or closed. A non-zero value indicates resolution quality issues. Used for first-contact resolution (FCR) KPI (Key Performance Indicator) reporting.
reference: Salesforce CRM — Case History; ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems

description: Standardized code describing the action taken to resolve the service case. Distinct from root cause — this captures what was done, not why it happened. Used for resolution effectiveness analytics and SLA compliance reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: reshipped|refunded|credited|apology_issued|information_provided|escalated_to_claim|no_action_required|other — promote to reference product]
reference: Salesforce CRM — Case Object (Resolution); ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems

description: Free-text narrative documenting the specific resolution actions taken, agent findings, and any commitments made to the customer. Captured at case closure and retained for audit and quality review.
reference: Salesforce CRM — Case Object (Resolution); ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems

description: The date and time when the service case was first marked as resolved. Used to calculate total resolution time, SLA (Service Level Agreement) resolution compliance, and agent productivity metrics.
reference: Salesforce CRM — Case Milestone (Resolution); SLA governance; ISO 9001

description: Standardized classification of the underlying root cause identified during case investigation. Critical for operational improvement, KPI (Key Performance Indicator) reporting, and recurring failure analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: carrier_delay|customs_hold|documentation_error|damage_in_transit|lost_shipment|billing_error|system_error|weather_event|address_issue|other — promote to reference product]
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems; Salesforce CRM — Case Object (custom root cause field)

description: The transport mode or service type associated with the shipment or service failure referenced in this case. Drives routing to specialized service teams (e.g., air freight team vs. last-mile delivery team).
reference: IATA; IMO; US DOT; ISO 28000 Supply Chain Security Management

business_glossary_term: Service Level Agreement (SLA) First Response Breach Flag
description: Indicates whether the first response SLA (Service Level Agreement) target was breached for this case. True if the first_response_timestamp exceeded the sla_response_target_hours from case creation. Key KPI (Key Performance Indicator) for customer service quality reporting.
reference: SLA governance; ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems

business_glossary_term: Service Level Agreement (SLA) Resolution Breach Flag
description: Indicates whether the resolution SLA (Service Level Agreement) target was breached for this case. True if the resolved_timestamp exceeded the sla_resolution_target_hours from case creation. Drives escalation workflows and executive service quality dashboards.
reference: SLA governance; ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems

business_glossary_term: Service Level Agreement (SLA) Resolution Target Hours
description: The contracted or policy-defined maximum number of hours within which the case must be fully resolved, as defined by the applicable SLA (Service Level Agreement) for this account segment and priority combination.
reference: SLA (Service Level Agreement) governance; ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems

business_glossary_term: Service Level Agreement (SLA) Response Target Hours
description: The contracted or policy-defined maximum number of hours within which the first agent response must be delivered to the customer, as defined by the applicable SLA (Service Level Agreement) for this account segment and priority combination.
reference: SLA (Service Level Agreement) governance; ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems

description: The native case identifier from Salesforce CRM (e.g., Salesforce Case ID / 18-character Salesforce record ID). Retained for lineage traceability, reconciliation with the source system, and EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) integration cross-referencing.
reference: Salesforce CRM — Case Object (Id); SOX; GDPR Article 5

description: Short, descriptive title of the service case as entered by the customer or agent. Used for case list views, search, and initial triage. Typically 255 characters or fewer.
Customer-facing parcel or shipment tracking number associated with this service case. Used for last-mile delivery inquiries, POD (Proof of Delivery) disputes, and express parcel service failures.
reference: FourKites Real-Time Visibility Platform; US DOT Shipment Tracking Standards

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the customer service preference record. Primary key for the service_preference data product within the customer domain.

Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Customer service preferences (incoterms, service level, transport mode) are contractually defined in a master agreement. Logistics ops teams must validate that preferences align with contracted terms;

Foreign key linking to customs.compliance_program. Business justification: Customers with AEO or C-TPAT compliance program membership require specific service preferences (expedited clearance, reduced examination). Logistics operators configure service preferences directly a
Reference to the customer account for whom these service preferences are defined. Links to the master customer record in the customer domain.

The customer's EDI interchange partner identifier (ISA qualifier + ID or EDIFACT interchange address) used for automated B2B document exchange including ASNs, invoices, and shipment status messages. Applicable for enterprise B2B customers.
reference: EDI X12 ISA Segment; EDIFACT UNB Segment; ANSI ASC X12

description: Reference to the customer's preferred warehouse or distribution center for order fulfillment and storage operations. Used in Manhattan WMS for inventory slotting and order routing.
reference: Manhattan WMS Facility Master; ISO 28000 Supply Chain Security

description: Foreign key linking to network.agent. Business justification: customs_clearance_preference is plain text on service_preference. Customers designate preferred customs brokers/agents for import and export clearance. This drives automated agent assignment in custom

Foreign key linking to network.carrier_service. Business justification: Customer service preferences specify preferred_service_level as plain text. Automated booking systems require a FK to the actual carrier service record to apply correct rates, transit times, and equip

Foreign key linking to route.lane. Business justification: Customer service preferences specify preferred lanes for routing optimization. Powers TMS routing engine, enforces customer-specific lane selection rules, and ensures service level compliance. Critica

Foreign key linking to route.service_corridor. Business justification: Customer service preferences specify preferred corridors driving TMS lane selection and rate negotiation. Existing FK covers preferred_lane_id but not corridor. Logistics domain experts expect service

Foreign key linking to network.tpl_provider. Business justification: Customers in e-commerce logistics designate a preferred 3PL for warehousing and fulfillment. This preference drives automated warehouse assignment and WMS integration. No existing 3PL FK on service_pr

Reference to the customer's preferred carrier for shipment execution. Used in SAP TM carrier selection and Oracle TMS freight audit to prioritize carrier assignment when capacity and rates are comparable.
reference: SAP TM Carrier Master; Oracle TMS Carrier Configuration

description: Foreign key linking to pricing.rate_card. Business justification: Service preferences specify which rate card applies to customer's shipments. Critical for multi-rate-card customers (different cards for air vs ocean, domestic vs international). Drives rating engine

Foreign key linking to contract.service_scope. Business justification: Service preferences must be validated against the contracted service scope to ensure customers only request services covered by their agreement. Booking validation and order acceptance processes depen

Foreign key linking to route.transit_time_standard. Business justification: Customer service preferences define the applicable transit time standard (e.g., 3-day express vs. 7-day economy). TMS uses this during shipment planning to apply the correct standard. Logistics domain

Official AEO certificate number issued by the relevant customs authority. Used in Descartes Customs for automated declaration pre-population and expedited clearance routing. Applicable only when aeo_certified is true.
reference: WCO AEO Framework; EU AEO Regulation; Descartes Customs

description: Indicates whether the customer holds an Authorized Economic Operator (AEO) certification, entitling them to expedited customs processing and reduced inspection rates. Stored to enable AEO-based routing and compliance benefits in Descartes Customs.
reference: WCO AEO Framework; EU AEO Regulation (EC) No 648/2005; C-TPAT

description: Indicates whether the customer requires an Advanced Shipping Notice (ASN) to be transmitted prior to shipment departure. When true, ASN generation is triggered in Manhattan WMS and transmitted via EDI (X12 856 or EDIFACT DESADV) to the customer.
reference: EDI X12 856 ASN; EDIFACT DESADV; Manhattan WMS

description: Customer's preference for Air Waybill (AWB) type on air freight shipments. 'HAWB' (House Air Waybill) is issued by the freight forwarder; 'MAWB' (Master Air Waybill) is issued by the airline; 'either' allows system-driven selection. Impacts IATA cargo documentation and customs filing.
reference: IATA Resolution 600a (AWB); IATA Cargo Services Conference

description: Customer's preference for Bill of Lading (BOL) type on ocean freight shipments. 'HBL' (House Bill of Lading) is issued by the NVOCC or freight forwarder; 'MBL' (Master Bill of Lading) is issued by the ocean carrier. Impacts FMC compliance and customs filing in Descartes Customs.
reference: FMC Regulations; IMO; Descartes Customs; NVOCC Rules

description: Customer's preference for carbon offset participation. 'none' means no offset; 'voluntary' means the customer opts into voluntary carbon offset programs; 'mandatory' means offset is contractually required. Supports CORSIA compliance and sustainability reporting.
reference: CORSIA; EU ETS; GHG Protocol Corporate Standard

description: Indicates whether Cash on Delivery (COD) collection is enabled for this customer's shipments. When true, delivery drivers are authorized to collect payment at the point of delivery. Relevant for B2C and e-commerce fulfillment scenarios.
reference: SAP TM COD Configuration; SAP S/4HANA Finance AR

description: Maximum monetary value authorized for COD collection per shipment for this customer. Enforced during last-mile delivery dispatch to manage cash handling risk. Expressed in the customer's default currency.
reference: SAP TM COD Rules; SAP S/4HANA Finance; PCI DSS

description: Customer's preference for ocean freight container loading. 'FCL' (Full Container Load) provides exclusive use of a container; 'LCL' (Less Than Container Load) consolidates cargo with other shippers. Drives container booking logic in SAP TM and Oracle TMS load optimization.
reference: IMO; FMC; SAP TM Freight Order Management

description: Timestamp when this service preference record was first created in the system. Sourced from Salesforce CRM customer preference creation event. Used for audit trail and data lineage in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity); SOX Section 404; ISO/IEC 27001

description: Indicates how the customer prefers customs clearance to be managed. 'self_clearance' means the customer handles their own customs filings; 'broker_managed' means a third-party customs broker is used; 'transport_managed' means Transport Shipping handles clearance as part of the service. Drives workflow routing in Descartes Customs.
reference: WCO Kyoto Convention; Descartes Customs; C-TPAT; AEO Program

description: Indicates whether the customer is required to declare the cargo value on all shipments. When true, shipment booking validation enforces declared value entry, which is used for customs valuation in Descartes Customs and insurance calculation.
reference: WCO Customs Valuation Agreement; Descartes Customs; IATA

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code representing the customer's preferred billing and invoicing currency for freight charges. Used in SAP S/4HANA Finance revenue recognition and SAP TM charge calculation.

Customer's default International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) governing risk and cost transfer for shipments. Applied automatically during shipment booking in SAP TM. Aligns with ICC Incoterms 2020 rules and WTO trade compliance requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — promote to reference product]
reference: ICC Incoterms 2020; WTO; Descartes Customs Trade Compliance

description: Method used to calculate dimensional weight (DIM weight) for rating and billing purposes. Determines whether IATA volumetric divisor, carrier-specific divisor, or IMDG rules apply. Impacts freight charge calculation in SAP TM.
reference: IATA Resolution 502; SAP TM Charge Calculation

description: Date from which this service preference profile becomes active and is applied to shipment bookings. Supports seasonal and project-based preference management.
Date on which this service preference profile expires and is no longer applied. Nullable for open-ended preferences. Supports contract-aligned and seasonal preference windows.

Indicates whether the customer requires an electronic Proof of Delivery (ePOD) to be captured and transmitted upon shipment completion. When true, last-mile delivery drivers must capture digital signature or photo confirmation. Supports SLA compliance and invoice release workflows.
reference: FourKites ePOD; Oracle TMS Freight Audit; ISO 9001

description: Indicates whether this customer is authorized to ship hazardous materials (hazmat) under IMDG Code (ocean), IATA Dangerous Goods Regulations (air), or DOT regulations (road). When true, hazmat documentation and compliance checks are triggered in Descartes Customs.
reference: IMDG Code; IATA Dangerous Goods Regulations; ICAO Technical Instructions; US DOT 49 CFR

description: Indicates whether the customer requires cargo insurance to be arranged by Transport Shipping for their shipments by default. When true, insurance coverage is automatically included in shipment booking and charge calculation in SAP TM.
reference: ISO 28000; IMO; IATA; SAP TM Charge Calculation

description: Customer's preferred shipping label output format for parcel and freight labels. ZPL (Zebra Programming Language) is standard for thermal printers; PDF for laser printing; EPL for legacy Eltron printers; PNG for digital/web display. Configured in Oracle TMS shipment execution.
reference: Oracle TMS Label Configuration; IATA Resolution 606 (Air Waybill)

description: Timestamp when this service preference record was most recently updated. Tracks preference changes for audit compliance under SOX and GDPR accountability requirements. Used for incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: GDPR Article 5; SOX Section 404; Salesforce CRM Audit Trail

description: Customer's preferred channel for shipment status notifications and alerts. Drives automated notification dispatch in FourKites real-time visibility platform and Salesforce CRM service communications. EDI applies to B2B enterprise customers using EDIFACT or X12 messaging.
reference: FourKites Notification Configuration; EDI X12 / EDIFACT; GDPR Article 7

description: Comma-separated list of shipment lifecycle events that trigger customer notifications (e.g., 'booked,picked_up,in_transit,customs_cleared,out_for_delivery,delivered,exception'). Configures FourKites exception management and Salesforce CRM service case alerts.
reference: FourKites Event Configuration; Salesforce CRM; IATA Cargo Tracking Standards

description: Externally-known business identifier for this service preference profile, used in EDI integrations, API calls, and customer-facing communications. Sourced from Salesforce CRM customer preference records.
reference: Salesforce CRM; EDI X12 / EDIFACT Standards

description: Human-readable label for this service preference profile, enabling customers and operations staff to distinguish between multiple preference sets (e.g., 'Standard B2B Freight', 'E-Commerce Express').
reference: Salesforce CRM; SAP TM Customer Profile

description: Current lifecycle state of the service preference record. 'active' means preferences are applied to new shipment bookings; 'inactive' means preferences are archived; 'pending' means awaiting customer or operations approval; 'suspended' means temporarily disabled.

Classification of the preference profile indicating its scope and application context. 'default' applies to all shipments unless overridden; 'override' supersedes defaults for specific lanes or periods; 'seasonal' applies during defined date windows; 'project' is tied to a specific contract or project.

ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the customer's default shipment destination country. Used in SAP TM route planning and Descartes Customs trade compliance screening for import declarations and HS code validation.
ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the customer's default shipment origin country. Used in SAP TM route planning and Descartes Customs trade compliance screening for export declarations.
Customer's default packaging format for shipments, used to pre-populate booking forms and drive warehouse packing instructions in Manhattan WMS. Supports slotting optimization and labor management.
reference: Manhattan WMS Packaging Configuration; ISO 780 Packaging Marking

description: Customer's preferred shipment service level tier governing transit time and cost trade-off. Maps to SLA entitlements and carrier product codes in SAP TM charge calculation and Oracle TMS load optimization.
reference: SAP TM Service Level Configuration; IATA Service Categories

description: Customer's preferred primary mode of transport for shipments. Drives automated carrier and route selection in SAP TM and Oracle TMS. Values align with IATA (air), IMO (ocean), and DOT (road/rail) modal classifications.
reference: IATA; IMO; US DOT; SAP TM Route Planning

description: Customer's preference for road freight load type. 'FTL' (Full Truckload) provides exclusive truck capacity; 'LTL' (Less Than Truckload) consolidates with other shipments. Used in SAP TM route planning and Oracle TMS load optimization.
reference: US DOT; SAP TM Route Planning; Oracle TMS Load Optimization

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this service preference record originated. Supports data lineage tracking and conflict resolution in the Databricks Silver Layer. 'manual' indicates direct data entry by operations staff.

Comma-separated list of special handling instruction codes applicable to this customer's shipments by default (e.g., 'FRAGILE,TEMP_CTRL,HAZMAT,PERISHABLE,HIGH_VALUE,OVERSIZED'). Drives warehouse handling procedures in Manhattan WMS and carrier booking requirements in SAP TM.
reference: IATA Special Handling Codes (SPH); IMDG Code; Manhattan WMS

description: Indicates whether the customer has opted into carbon emissions reporting for their shipments. When true, GHG (Greenhouse Gas) and CO2e (Carbon Dioxide Equivalent) data is calculated and included in customer sustainability reports, aligned with EU ETS and CORSIA requirements.
reference: EU Emissions Trading System (EU ETS); CORSIA; GHG Protocol; ISO 14064

description: Maximum acceptable temperature in degrees Celsius for temperature-controlled shipments. Used to configure cold chain monitoring alerts in FourKites and warehouse environment controls in Manhattan WMS. Applicable only when temperature_controlled is true.
reference: IATA CEIV Pharma; GDP Guidelines; FourKites IoT Monitoring

description: Minimum acceptable temperature in degrees Celsius for temperature-controlled shipments. Used to configure cold chain monitoring alerts in FourKites and warehouse environment controls in Manhattan WMS. Applicable only when temperature_controlled is true.
reference: IATA CEIV Pharma; GDP Guidelines; FourKites IoT Monitoring

description: Indicates whether this customer's shipments require temperature-controlled handling and transport by default. When true, cold chain equipment and monitoring requirements are applied during booking in SAP TM and warehouse slotting in Manhattan WMS.
reference: IATA CEIV Pharma; GDP Guidelines; Manhattan WMS Cold Chain

description: Granularity of shipment tracking data shared with the customer via FourKites or customer portal. 'full' provides GPS-level real-time updates; 'milestone' provides key event updates (pickup, customs, delivery); 'summary' provides ETA and final status only; 'none' disables customer-facing tracking.
reference: FourKites Real-Time Visibility Platform; IATA Cargo Tracking

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the customer-specific SLA entitlement record in the silver layer lakehouse.

Foreign key linking to network.agent. Business justification: SLA entitlements in logistics are often fulfilled by specific customs brokers or freight agents contractually responsible for meeting transit and clearance SLAs. Agent-specific SLA measurement and bre

Reference to the parent commercial contract or agreement under which this SLA entitlement is granted. Distinct from the entitlement itself — the contract domain holds the template; this record is the customer-specific instantiation.
reference: SAP TM Contract Management; IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition

description: Foreign key linking to network.carrier. Business justification: Enterprise SLA agreements often specify carrier-specific performance guarantees (e.g., 'Carrier X: 99% OTD on EU lanes'). Contract management, performance measurement, and penalty/incentive calculatio

Foreign key linking to network.carrier_service. Business justification: SLA transit time guarantees are tied to specific carrier services (e.g., 'DHL Express Worldwide 2-day'). SLA measurement and OTD reporting require knowing which carrier service the entitlement applies
Reference to the customer account for which this SLA entitlement is instantiated. Links to the customer master in the customer domain.

Foreign key linking to route.lane. Business justification: SLA entitlements are lane-specific contractual commitments. Required for lane-level performance monitoring, OTD/OTIF measurement by lane, penalty/incentive calculation, and contractual compliance repo

Foreign key linking to network.partner. Business justification: Freight forwarders and 3PLs (network partners) are often contractually responsible for SLA delivery, distinct from carriers. SLA entitlements with freight forwarder partners require a partner FK for p

Foreign key linking to pricing.rate_card. Business justification: SLA entitlements are co-negotiated with rate cards in logistics contract management. The rate card defines pricing commitments while the SLA defines service commitments — both are referenced together
Reference to the primary customer contact who receives SLA performance reports and is the designated recipient for escalation notifications under this entitlement.

Foreign key linking to route.service_corridor. Business justification: SLA entitlements are scoped to service corridors (e.g., Asia-Europe express). Corridor-level SLA reporting and customer commitment tracking require this link. Logistics domain experts expect entitleme

Foreign key linking to contract.contract_sla_commitment. Business justification: Customer SLA entitlements must be traceable to the specific contract SLA commitment that backs them. Compliance audits, SLA breach investigations, and penalty calculations require verifying that each

Foreign key linking to network.tpl_provider. Business justification: E-commerce fulfillment SLA entitlements are commonly with 3PL providers (pick-pack-ship SLAs, same-day dispatch). No existing 3PL FK on sla_entitlement. Required for 3PL SLA breach reporting, penalty

Foreign key linking to route.transit_time_standard. Business justification: SLA compliance measurement requires linking customer entitlements to the published transit time standard they are measured against. Logistics KPI reporting (OTD, OTIF) depends on comparing actual vs.

Indicates whether this SLA entitlement automatically renews at the end of the effective period without requiring explicit renegotiation. When True, the entitlement rolls over under the same terms unless terminated.
reference: SAP TM Contract Management; Salesforce CRM Entitlement

business_glossary_term: Claims Resolution Service Level Agreement (SLA) Days
description: Maximum number of calendar days within which a cargo claim must be acknowledged, investigated, and resolved under this SLA entitlement. Drives claims team prioritization and escalation triggers.
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management; CMR Convention; Montreal Convention (Air Cargo Claims)

description: Timestamp when this SLA entitlement record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Provides audit trail for record provenance and data lineage in the silver layer.
reference: GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity); SOX Audit Trail Requirements

description: Source system identifier for this SLA entitlement record in Salesforce CRM. Enables traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational CRM system of record.

ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to all monetary fields in this SLA entitlement record (penalty_cap_amount, incentive amounts). Ensures consistent financial reporting across multi-currency customer accounts.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS Financial Reporting; SAP S/4HANA Finance

description: Latest time of day (HH:MM, 24-hour local time) by which delivery must be completed to be considered on-time under this SLA entitlement. Defines the close of the delivery window for time-definite commitments.
reference: SAP TM Delivery Window; Oracle TMS Time-Definite Delivery

description: Earliest time of day (HH:MM, 24-hour local time) within which delivery must be attempted to be considered on-time under this SLA entitlement. Used for time-definite delivery commitments (e.g., AM delivery windows).
reference: SAP TM Delivery Window; Oracle TMS Time-Definite Delivery

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the destination geography of the lane covered by this SLA entitlement. Null if the entitlement is not lane-specific.
Calendar date from which this SLA entitlement becomes binding and enforceable. Performance measurement begins on or after this date.
reference: IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition; SAP TM Contract Validity

description: Calendar date on which this SLA entitlement expires and ceases to be enforceable. Null indicates an open-ended entitlement with no fixed expiry. Aligns with contract renewal cycles.
reference: IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition; SAP TM Contract Validity

description: Descriptive business name for this SLA entitlement, e.g., 'Gold Express OTD Commitment — APAC Lane'. Used in customer-facing documents and CRM dashboards.

Externally-known, human-readable business identifier for this SLA entitlement, used in customer communications, CRM service cases, and operational reporting. Format: SLA- followed by alphanumeric characters.
reference: Salesforce CRM Entitlement Object; SAP TM Service Level Agreement

description: Current lifecycle state of the SLA entitlement. 'active' means the entitlement is in force; 'suspended' means temporarily paused (e.g., force majeure); 'expired' means the effective period has lapsed; 'pending_activation' means approved but not yet in effect; 'terminated' means cancelled before natural expiry; 'draft' means under negotiation.
reference: Salesforce CRM Entitlement Status; ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems

description: Number of hours after a shipment misses its committed delivery time before an automatic escalation is triggered to the account management or operations team. Enables proactive exception management for high-value customers.
reference: Salesforce CRM Escalation Rules; FourKites Exception Management

description: Free-text description of conditions under which SLA performance failures are excluded from penalty calculations, such as force majeure events, customs delays, natural disasters, consignee unavailability, or regulatory holds. Sourced from contract terms.
reference: Incoterms 2020; WCO Customs Procedures; ISO 28000 Supply Chain Security

description: Number of hours beyond the committed delivery time that are tolerated before a shipment is classified as a late delivery for SLA measurement purposes. Accounts for minor operational variances and is agreed upon at contract negotiation.
reference: SAP TM SLA Configuration; Oracle TMS Tolerance Window

description: Indicates whether a financial incentive or rebate clause is in effect for over-performance against the SLA targets. When True, the incentive_rate_pct field defines the reward structure.
reference: IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition; SAP S/4HANA Finance Contract Accounting

description: Percentage of the applicable freight charge or contract value awarded as a financial incentive or rebate for each percentage point of SLA over-performance above the committed target. Active only when incentive_clause_applicable is True.
reference: IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition; SAP S/4HANA Finance

description: Timestamp when this SLA entitlement record was most recently updated, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Supports change detection, incremental processing, and audit compliance in the Databricks lakehouse.
reference: GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity); SOX Audit Trail Requirements

description: Date on which this SLA entitlement was last formally reviewed with the customer in a business review meeting. Used to track review cadence compliance and trigger upcoming review scheduling.
reference: Salesforce CRM Activity; ISO 9001 Management Review

description: Description of the methodology used to measure SLA performance, including how on-time is defined (e.g., first delivery attempt, POD scan time, ePOD timestamp), exclusions (e.g., force majeure, customs delays, consignee unavailability), and data source (e.g., FourKites, SAP TM, Oracle TMS).
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management; FourKites Real-Time Visibility; SAP TM Performance Reporting

description: Frequency at which SLA performance is measured and reported against the committed targets. Determines the rolling window for OTD, OTIF, and volume threshold calculations.
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management; SAP TM SLA Reporting

description: Minimum number of shipments the customer commits to tender per measurement period to qualify for the SLA entitlement terms. Failure to meet this threshold may trigger tier renegotiation or penalty clauses.
reference: SAP TM Volume Commitment; Oracle TMS Volume Threshold

description: Minimum volume in Twenty-Foot Equivalent Units (TEU) the customer commits to tender per measurement period. Applicable to ocean freight FCL/LCL services. TEU is the standard unit of container capacity measurement per IMO and FMC conventions.
reference: IMO Container Standards; FMC Ocean Shipping Reform Act; SAP TM Volume Commitment

description: Minimum chargeable weight in kilograms the customer commits to tender per measurement period. Applicable primarily to air freight and express services where weight-based volume commitments are standard.
reference: IATA Air Cargo Tariff; SAP TM Volume Commitment

description: Scheduled date for the next formal SLA performance review with the customer. Derived from last_reviewed_date and review_frequency but stored explicitly to support scheduling and CRM activity management.
reference: Salesforce CRM Activity; ISO 9001 Management Review

description: Number of calendar days advance notice required by either party to terminate or renegotiate this SLA entitlement before its effective_until_date. Governs contract exit obligations.
reference: IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition; SAP TM Contract Management

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the origin geography of the lane covered by this SLA entitlement. Null if the entitlement is not lane-specific.

Contractually committed On-Time Delivery (OTD) rate expressed as a percentage (0.00–100.00). Defines the minimum proportion of shipments that must be delivered within the agreed transit time window to meet the SLA. Core KPI for logistics SLA performance measurement.
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management; SAP TM SLA Configuration; IATA Performance Standards

description: Contractually committed On-Time In-Full (OTIF) rate expressed as a percentage (0.00–100.00). Measures the proportion of shipments delivered both on time and with complete quantity/content as ordered. Stricter than OTD alone; commonly required by retail and e-commerce customers.
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management; SAP TM SLA Configuration

description: Maximum financial penalty amount (in the contract currency) that can be levied against Transport Shipping in a single measurement period for SLA under-performance. Caps total financial exposure under the penalty clause.
reference: IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition; SAP S/4HANA Finance; SOX Financial Reporting

description: Indicates whether a financial penalty clause is in effect for under-performance against the SLA targets. When True, the penalty_rate_pct and penalty_cap_amount fields define the financial exposure.
reference: IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition; SAP S/4HANA Finance Contract Accounting

description: Percentage of the applicable freight charge or contract value applied as a financial penalty for each percentage point of SLA under-performance. Active only when penalty_clause_applicable is True.
reference: IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition; SAP S/4HANA Finance; SOX Financial Reporting

description: Latest time of day (HH:MM, 24-hour local time) by which a shipment must be tendered for pickup to qualify for same-day transit under this SLA entitlement. Shipments tendered after this time are counted against the next business day for OTD measurement.
reference: SAP TM Pickup Window; Oracle TMS Cut-Off Configuration; IATA Cargo Acceptance

description: Agreed frequency at which SLA performance is formally reviewed with the customer in a business review meeting. Drives scheduling of Quarterly Business Reviews (QBRs) and account management activities.
reference: Salesforce CRM Account Management; ISO 9001 Management Review

description: Business service line or product family to which this SLA entitlement applies, e.g., Express Parcel Delivery, Freight Forwarding, E-Commerce Fulfillment, Contract Logistics, Customs Brokerage. Aligns with the core business processes of Transport Shipping. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: express_parcel|freight_forwarding|supply_chain_mgmt|customs_brokerage|warehouse_distribution|ecommerce_fulfillment|last_mile|contract_logistics|cross_border — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP TM Service Product; Oracle TMS Service Configuration

description: Transport or logistics service mode to which this SLA entitlement applies, e.g., Air Express, Ocean FCL, Road FTL, Road LTL, Rail, Parcel. Determines which operational processes are governed by this entitlement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: air_express|ocean_fcl|ocean_lcl|road_ftl|road_ltl|rail|parcel|last_mile|warehouse — promote to reference product]
reference: IATA Air Cargo Standards; IMO SOLAS; SAP TM Transport Mode

description: Commercial tier classification of the SLA entitlement, reflecting the level of service commitment and associated pricing. Platinum represents the highest commitment level; standard represents baseline service.
reference: SAP TM Service Level Agreement; Salesforce CRM Entitlement

description: Maximum number of calendar days from shipment pickup to delivery guaranteed under this SLA entitlement. Defines the transit time window used to evaluate OTD compliance. Expressed in whole days; fractional-day commitments use transit_time_guarantee_hours.
reference: SAP TM Transit Time; Oracle TMS Transit Commitment; IATA Air Cargo SLA

description: Maximum number of hours from shipment pickup to delivery guaranteed under this SLA entitlement. Used for express and time-definite services where day-level granularity is insufficient (e.g., next-day AM, same-day delivery). Takes precedence over transit_time_guarantee_days when both are populated.
reference: SAP TM Transit Time; IATA Express Delivery Standards

description: Unique identifier for the consignee record. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to customer.consignee_profile. Business justification: Links fulfillment-side delivery recipient entity to customer's consignee master profile. Essential for delivery planning, address validation, delivery preference enforcement, denied-party screening, a

Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_delivery_zone. Business justification: A consignee's address maps to a specific fulfillment delivery zone, which drives carrier selection, SLA windows, COD availability, and surcharge applicability. Storing fulfillment_delivery_zone_id on

Foreign key linking to customs.trade_party. Business justification: Consignees receiving international shipments are registered trade parties for customs purposes. Enables denied party screening, AEO certification verification, importer-of-record validation, and custo
Security access code or gate code required to enter the delivery location (e.g., building entry code, gated community code).
Indicates whether this consignee record is currently active and available for use in fulfillment operations. True if active, false if deactivated or archived.
Primary street address line for delivery location. Includes street number, street name, and building identifier.
Secondary address line for additional location details such as apartment number, suite, floor, building name, or care-of information.
Classification of the delivery address type: residential home, commercial business, automated locker, PUDO (Pick-Up Drop-Off) point, CFS (Container Freight Station), or ICD (Inland Container Depot).
Status of address validation process: validated (confirmed accurate), unvalidated (not yet checked), corrected (modified by validation service), or failed (could not be validated).
Secondary contact phone number for the consignee. Used when primary contact is unavailable or for escalation during delivery attempts.
Indicates whether this consignee is blacklisted due to fraud, repeated delivery failures, abusive behavior, or security concerns. True if blacklisted, false otherwise.
Explanation of why the consignee was blacklisted (e.g., fraud, repeated refusal to accept delivery, security threat, denied party screening match).
End time of business hours for commercial consignees in HH:MM format (24-hour clock). Used for scheduling delivery attempts during operating hours.
Start time of business hours for commercial consignees in HH:MM format (24-hour clock). Used for scheduling delivery attempts during operating hours.
City or municipality name for the delivery address.
Indicates whether this consignee is eligible to receive COD (Cash on Delivery) shipments. True if COD is permitted, false if COD is restricted due to payment history or risk assessment.

business_glossary_term: Cash on Delivery (COD) Payment Method Preference
description: Preferred payment method for COD (Cash on Delivery) collections: cash, card (credit/debit), mobile_payment (digital wallet), or bank_transfer.
Full legal name or business name of the delivery recipient (consignee). May be an individual person or a company name.
Classification of the consignee entity type: individual person, business organization, government entity, or non-governmental organization.
Primary email address for the consignee. Used for delivery notifications, ePOD (Electronic Proof of Delivery) transmission, and customer communication.
Primary contact phone number for the consignee. Used for delivery coordination, notifications, and exception handling.
Three-letter ISO country code for the delivery address (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU, CHN).
Date and time when this consignee record was first created in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
Customs-specific identifier for the consignee (e.g., importer of record number, customs registration number). Used for cross-border shipments and customs declarations.
Free-text special instructions for the delivery agent regarding access, location, handling, or recipient preferences (e.g., ring doorbell twice, use side entrance, fragile items).
Indicates whether the consignee permits unattended delivery at the door. True if leave-at-door is acceptable, false if recipient presence is required.
Indicates whether the consignee permits delivery to a neighbor if the primary recipient is unavailable. True if neighbor delivery is acceptable.
Free-text instructions specifying a safe place to leave the parcel if the consignee is not present (e.g., back porch, garage, with building concierge).
Indicates whether the consignee requires a signature for delivery confirmation. True if signature is mandatory, false if signature can be waived.
Preferred time window for delivery attempts: morning (before noon), afternoon (noon to 5pm), evening (after 5pm), business_hours (9am-5pm weekdays), or anytime (no preference).

Result of screening against denied party lists (e.g., OFAC, EU sanctions, UN sanctions): cleared (no match), flagged (potential match requiring review), pending (screening in progress), or not_screened.
reference: OFAC, EU Sanctions, UN Security Council, C-TPAT

description: Total number of failed delivery attempts recorded for this consignee address across all historical shipments. Used for delivery risk scoring.
Indicates whether this consignee has a history of failed delivery attempts. True if previous deliveries have failed, false otherwise. Used for risk assessment and route planning.
Precision level of the geocoded coordinates: rooftop (exact building), street (street-level), postal_code (postal code centroid), city (city centroid), or region (regional approximation).
Geographic latitude coordinate of the delivery address in decimal degrees. Used for route optimization and GPS (Global Positioning System) navigation.
Geographic longitude coordinate of the delivery address in decimal degrees. Used for route optimization and GPS (Global Positioning System) navigation.
Two-letter ISO language code for the consignee's preferred communication language (e.g., en, es, fr, de, zh). Used for delivery notifications and customer communications.
Date and time when this consignee record was most recently modified. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
Indicates whether the consignee has opted in to receive delivery notifications via email. True if email notifications are enabled.
Indicates whether the consignee has opted in to receive delivery notifications via mobile app push notifications. True if push notifications are enabled.
Indicates whether the consignee has opted in to receive delivery notifications via SMS text message. True if SMS notifications are enabled.
Postal code or ZIP code for the delivery address. Format varies by country (e.g., 5-digit US ZIP, alphanumeric UK postcode).

Classification of the delivery location as residential (home address), commercial (business address), or mixed_use (building with both residential and commercial units). Affects delivery routing and surcharges.
Code identifying the operational system of record that originated this consignee record (e.g., SAP_TM, ORACLE_TMS, MANHATTAN_WMS, SALESFORCE_CRM, ECOMMERCE_API).
Free-text description of any special handling requirements for deliveries to this consignee (e.g., temperature-sensitive, fragile, hazardous materials, security protocols).
reference: IMDG Code, ICAO Technical Instructions, ADR

description: State, province, or administrative region for the delivery address. Format varies by country (e.g., two-letter US state codes, full province names).
Tax identification number for the consignee (e.g., EIN, VAT number, GST number). Required for customs clearance and DDP (Delivered Duty Paid) shipments.
IANA time zone identifier for the consignee location (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/London, Asia/Shanghai). Used for accurate delivery time window calculations and notifications.
Unique identifier for each physical delivery attempt. Primary key for the delivery attempt record.
Reference to the parent consignment being delivered. Links this attempt to the shipment package.

Foreign key linking to fleet.driver_profile. Business justification: Each delivery attempt is performed by a driver whose profile (license validity, certifications, performance rating, HOS status) must be validated for operational compliance and audit trail. Existing d
Reference to the e-commerce fulfillment order driving this delivery. Links attempt to the original order.
Reference to the last-mile dispatch run that included this delivery attempt. Links to the route execution.

Foreign key linking to fulfillment.parcel. Business justification: A delivery attempt is fundamentally the act of delivering a specific physical parcel to the consignee. delivery_attempt already links to fulfillment_order and last_mile_dispatch, but has no direct FK

Foreign key linking to route.route_delivery_zone. Business justification: Delivery attempts occur within specific delivery zones for performance tracking. Enables zone-level delivery performance analysis, failure rate tracking, and zone-based service improvement initiatives

Foreign key linking to fleet.transport_asset. Business justification: delivery_attempt stores vehicle_registration_number as a denormalized string. A FK to fleet.transport_asset enables per-vehicle delivery performance reporting, incident correlation with fleet records,
Building or gate access code provided to enable delivery agent entry to restricted premises. Confidential information for delivery execution.
Calendar date on which this delivery attempt occurred. Used for daily delivery performance reporting.
GPS latitude coordinate captured at the moment of delivery attempt. Used for geolocation verification and route optimization analysis.
GPS longitude coordinate captured at the moment of delivery attempt. Used for geolocation verification and route optimization analysis.
Sequential number of this delivery attempt for the consignment (1 for first attempt, 2 for second, etc.). Used to track re-delivery attempts.

Standardized code indicating the result of the delivery attempt. Core field for OTD and OTIF KPI calculation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: delivered|failed|refused|no_access|redirected|damaged|address_incorrect — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Precise date and time when the delivery agent arrived at the delivery location and initiated the attempt. Primary business event timestamp for SLA calculation.
Amount to be collected from the recipient at delivery. Null if this is not a COD shipment.
Indicates whether the COD payment was successfully collected from the recipient. True if collected, false if not collected or not applicable.
Three-letter ISO currency code for the COD amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Null if not a COD shipment.
Method used by recipient to pay the COD amount (cash, credit card, mobile payment, check). Null if COD was not collected.
Timestamp when this delivery attempt record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
Primary street address line where the delivery attempt was made. May differ from consignment address if redirected.
Secondary address line (apartment, suite, building) where the delivery attempt was made.
City or municipality where the delivery attempt was made.
Three-letter ISO country code of the delivery location (e.g., USA, CAN, GBR).
Special instructions provided by the consignee or shipper for this delivery (e.g., leave at door, call on arrival, deliver to reception). Guides delivery agent behavior.
Postal or ZIP code of the delivery location. Used for geographic analysis and route optimization.
State, province, or region where the delivery attempt was made.
Code identifying the depot or distribution center from which this delivery attempt was dispatched. Used for operational reporting and network analysis.

business_glossary_term: Electronic Proof of Delivery (ePOD) Photo Captured Flag
description: Indicates whether a photo of the delivered parcel was captured at the delivery location. True if photo was taken, false otherwise.

business_glossary_term: Electronic Proof of Delivery (ePOD) Reference Number
description: Unique reference number linking to the electronic proof of delivery record (signature, photo, barcode scan). Null if delivery was not completed.

business_glossary_term: Electronic Proof of Delivery (ePOD) Signature Captured Flag
description: Indicates whether a digital signature was captured from the recipient at delivery. True if signature was obtained, false otherwise.

Detailed reason code explaining why a delivery attempt failed (e.g., recipient_not_home, business_closed, access_denied, incorrect_address). Null if attempt was successful. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: recipient_not_home|business_closed|access_denied|incorrect_address|security_restriction|weather_delay|vehicle_breakdown — promote to reference product]
Free-text description providing additional context about the delivery failure. Captured by delivery agent for customer service follow-up.
Timestamp when this delivery attempt record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.
Instruction code indicating the next step to be taken following this attempt (e.g., schedule re-attempt, hold at depot, return to sender). Drives workflow automation.
Date on which the next delivery re-attempt is scheduled. Null if no re-attempt is planned or if delivery was successful.
Phone number of the person who received the parcel or was contacted during the delivery attempt. Used for delivery confirmation and follow-up.
Name of the person who received the parcel or who was present at the delivery location during the attempt. May differ from consignee if alternate recipient accepted delivery.
Relationship of the actual recipient to the consignee (e.g., consignee themselves, family member, neighbor, building security). Used for delivery authorization validation.

Indicates whether this delivery attempt resulted in an SLA breach (delivery not completed by target time). True if SLA was breached, false otherwise. Critical for customer service and penalty tracking.

business_glossary_term: Service Level Agreement (SLA) Target Delivery Timestamp
description: Contractual target date and time by which delivery must be completed to meet SLA commitments. Used for OTIF and OTD KPI calculation.
Code identifying the operational system that captured this delivery attempt record (e.g., mobile delivery app, handheld scanner, TMS). Used for data lineage and quality tracking.
Duration in minutes that the delivery agent spent at the delivery location during this attempt. Used for productivity analysis and route optimization.

Weather condition at the time of delivery attempt. Used to analyze weather impact on delivery success rates and SLA performance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: clear|rain|snow|fog|storm|extreme_heat|extreme_cold — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Unique identifier for the dispatch run. Primary key for this entity representing a scheduled last-mile delivery route or courier trip.

Foreign key linking to pricing.carrier_buy_rate. Business justification: Dispatch runs require carrier buy rate linkage for cost allocation per route, profitability analysis, carrier settlement reconciliation, and margin tracking in last-mile delivery operations.

Foreign key linking to network.carrier. Business justification: Dispatch runs are executed by carriers (or internal fleet). Business process: carrier performance by run, carrier payment reconciliation, carrier capacity utilization tracking. Removes denormalized SC

Foreign key linking to fulfillment.delivery_zone. Business justification: Dispatch runs operate within specific delivery zones. The service_area_code STRING should be normalized to FK referencing delivery_zone.fulfillment_delivery_zone_id. This enables proper zone-based rou

Foreign key linking to fleet.depot. Business justification: Dispatch runs originate from a fleet depot; origin_depot_code is a denormalized string on dispatch_run. A proper FK to fleet.depot enables depot-level dispatch volume reporting, capacity planning, and

Foreign key linking to fulfillment.parcel_manifest. Business justification: A dispatch_run (operational driver route) is associated with a parcel_manifest (carrier manifest grouping parcels for a scheduled dispatch). The parcel_manifest defines what parcels are loaded onto th

Foreign key linking to fleet.transport_asset. Business justification: Dispatch run planning assigns specific fleet vehicles to courier routes. Fleet utilization analysis, vehicle maintenance scheduling coordination, and operational cost allocation require authoritative

The actual total distance traveled in kilometers during this dispatch run, captured from GPS or vehicle telematics.
The actual total duration in minutes for this dispatch run, calculated from dispatch timestamp to return timestamp.
The actual number of delivery stops completed during this dispatch run, which may differ from planned due to failed deliveries or route changes.
The number of Cash on Delivery transactions collected during this dispatch run.
The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for COD collections on this dispatch run.
The date and time when this dispatch run record was first created in the system.
Free-text operational notes or special instructions for this dispatch run, used for communication between dispatch coordinators and couriers.
The actual date and time when the dispatch run departed from the origin depot or facility, marking the start of the last-mile delivery route.
The type or service level of this dispatch run, indicating the delivery speed and priority classification.
The status of EDI manifest transmission to the carrier system, indicating whether the dispatch run manifest has been successfully communicated.
The date and time when the EDI manifest was transmitted to the carrier system for this dispatch run.

business_glossary_term: Electronic Proof of Delivery (ePOD) Capture Count
description: The number of electronic proof of delivery records captured during this dispatch run, including signatures and photos.
The number of parcels that could not be delivered during this dispatch run due to recipient unavailability, address issues, or other delivery exceptions.
Boolean indicator of whether this dispatch run includes hazardous materials requiring special handling and compliance documentation.
reference: IMDG Code, 49 CFR Hazardous Materials Regulations

description: The date and time when this dispatch run record was most recently updated in the system.
The manifest document number generated for carrier handover, listing all parcels loaded onto this dispatch run for last-mile delivery.
The status of manifest reconciliation indicating whether all parcels loaded have been accounted for through delivery, return, or exception processing.
The planned total distance in kilometers for this dispatch run based on route optimization.
The planned total duration in minutes for this dispatch run based on route optimization and historical performance.
The number of delivery stops planned for this dispatch run based on route optimization and order allocation.
The date and time when the manifest reconciliation was completed for this dispatch run.
The actual date and time when the dispatch run returned to the origin depot or facility, marking the completion of the last-mile delivery route.
The number of parcels returned to the depot at the end of this dispatch run due to failed delivery attempts or customer refusal.
The predefined route code or zone identifier for this dispatch run, used for route planning and optimization.
The scheduled date for this dispatch run, representing the business day on which the last-mile delivery route is planned to execute.
Business-facing unique identifier or manifest number for the dispatch run, used for operational tracking and carrier handover documentation.
Current lifecycle status of the dispatch run indicating its operational state in the last-mile delivery workflow.
Boolean indicator of whether this dispatch run breached its SLA target completion time.

business_glossary_term: Service Level Agreement (SLA) Target Completion Time
description: The target completion timestamp for this dispatch run based on service level agreements and operational commitments.
The code identifying the operational system of record that created this dispatch run record.
Boolean indicator of whether this dispatch run requires temperature-controlled transport for perishable or sensitive goods.
The total monetary value of Cash on Delivery payments collected during this dispatch run.
The total number of parcels successfully delivered during this dispatch run, representing successful last-mile completions.
The total number of parcels loaded onto the vehicle or assigned to the courier for this dispatch run at departure.
The total volume in cubic meters of all parcels loaded onto this dispatch run, used for vehicle capacity planning.
The total weight in kilograms of all parcels loaded onto this dispatch run.
The percentage of vehicle capacity utilized by this dispatch run, calculated based on weight, volume, or parcel count constraints.
Unique identifier for the delivery zone record. Primary key.

Indicates whether this delivery zone record is active in the system. Inactive zones are retained for historical reference.
Historical average number of delivery attempts required to successfully complete deliveries in this zone. Used for operational planning.
Level of carrier network coverage available in this zone. Affects service availability and delivery reliability.
Primary city or municipality name associated with this delivery zone.

business_glossary_term: Cash on Delivery (COD) Service Available Flag
description: Indicates whether Cash on Delivery (COD) service is available for deliveries in this zone based on carrier capabilities and risk assessment.
Three-letter ISO country code identifying the country in which this delivery zone is located.
Timestamp when this delivery zone record was first created in the system.
Local time cutoff for same-day or next-day delivery eligibility in this zone. Format HH:MM in 24-hour notation.
Date on which this delivery zone configuration ceases to be effective. Null indicates no planned end date.
Date from which this delivery zone configuration becomes effective for operational use.

business_glossary_term: Electronic Proof of Delivery (ePOD) Required Flag
description: Indicates whether electronic Proof of Delivery (ePOD) capture is mandatory for deliveries in this zone.
Number of business days for express delivery service to this zone from the fulfillment center. Used for expedited SLA promise calculation.
Indicates whether fuel surcharge (Bunker Adjustment Factor - BAF) applies to shipments in this zone.
Indicates whether delivery service is available on public holidays in this zone.
Timestamp when this delivery zone record was last modified in the system.
Maximum parcel dimensions (length x width x height) in centimeters that can be delivered to this zone. Format: LxWxH.
Maximum parcel weight in kilograms that can be delivered to this zone based on carrier and infrastructure constraints.
Indicates whether this zone is eligible for next-day delivery service based on cutoff times and carrier network coverage.
Free-text notes capturing special instructions, constraints, or operational considerations for this delivery zone.
Ending postal code of the range covered by this delivery zone. Used for zone lookup during order processing.
Starting postal code of the range covered by this delivery zone. Used for zone lookup during order processing.
Code identifying the primary carrier assigned to service this delivery zone. Used in carrier selection logic.
Indicates whether a remote area surcharge applies to deliveries in this zone due to distance or accessibility constraints.
Indicates whether residential delivery surcharge applies to addresses in this zone.
Indicates whether restricted or regulated goods (e.g., hazardous materials, alcohol, pharmaceuticals) can be delivered to this zone based on local regulations.
Indicates whether this zone is eligible for same-day delivery service based on proximity to fulfillment centers and carrier availability.
Indicates whether this delivery zone is currently serviceable for deliveries. False indicates zone is temporarily or permanently out of service.
Indicates whether recipient signature is required for deliveries in this zone as a default service requirement.
Code identifying the operational system of record from which this delivery zone data originated (e.g., SAP TM, Oracle TMS, Manhattan WMS).
Number of business days for standard delivery service to this zone from the fulfillment center. Used for Service Level Agreement (SLA) promise calculation.
State or province code within the country. Format varies by country (e.g., US state codes, Canadian province codes).
IANA time zone identifier for this delivery zone. Used for scheduling delivery windows and cutoff time calculations.
Indicates whether Saturday and Sunday delivery service is available in this zone.
Latitude coordinate of the geographic centroid of the delivery zone. Used for distance calculations and route optimization.
Longitude coordinate of the geographic centroid of the delivery zone. Used for distance calculations and route optimization.
Human-readable name of the delivery zone for display and reporting purposes.
Priority ranking of this zone for resource allocation and route sequencing. Lower numbers indicate higher priority.
Classification of the delivery zone based on geographic and population density characteristics. Determines service level and surcharge applicability.

Postal or ZIP code of the delivery destination address.
Code identifying the planned delivery route or zone for this dispatch. Used for route optimization and territory management.

Sequential position of this dispatch within the delivery route. Determines the order in which deliveries should be attempted.

Current lifecycle status of the dispatch. Tracks progression from dispatch through delivery or return. Values: dispatched (assigned to driver), in_transit (en route to delivery area), out_for_delivery (active delivery attempt), delivered (successfully completed), failed (delivery unsuccessful), returned_to_depot (consignment returned).
Precise date and time when the consignment was dispatched from the depot or hub for final delivery. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

business_glossary_term: Electronic Proof of Delivery (ePOD) Photo Captured Flag
description: Indicates whether a photo was captured as proof of delivery. True if photo captured, False otherwise.

business_glossary_term: Electronic Proof of Delivery (ePOD) Signature Captured Flag
description: Indicates whether an electronic signature was captured as proof of delivery. True if signature captured, False otherwise.
Date and time when this dispatch record was last modified. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
End of the planned delivery time window communicated to the customer. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
Start of the planned delivery time window communicated to the customer. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
Date and time when the consignment was returned to the depot after a failed delivery attempt. Null if not returned. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
Indicates whether the delivery missed the SLA target delivery timestamp. True if breached, False if met or not yet determined.

business_glossary_term: Service Level Agreement (SLA) Target Delivery Timestamp
description: The committed delivery date and time per the service level agreement. Used to measure on-time delivery performance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
